creation-time callbacks for now\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return api.metrics.ObservableCounter(i64){ .noop = name };\n        }\n\n        // Validate parameters in debug mode\n        const validated_name = api.metrics.validateInstrumentName(name);\n        const validated_description = api.metrics.validateInstrumentDescription(description);\n        const validated_unit = api.metrics.validateInstrumentUnit(unit);\n\n        const observable_counter = try self.allocator.create(sdk.async_instr.ObservableCounter(i64));\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(observable_counter);\n\n        observable_counter.* = sdk.async_instr.ObservableCounter(i64).init(\n            self.allocator,\n            validated_name,\n            validated_description,\n            validated_unit,\n            self.async_config,\n        );\n        errdefer observable_counter.deinit();\n\n        try self.observable_counters_i64.append(self.allocator, observable_counter);\n\n        return api.metrics.ObservableCounter(i64){\n            .bridge = api.metrics.AsyncInstrumentBridge.init(observable_counter),\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn createObservableCounterF64(\n        self: *Meter,\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        advisory: ?api.metrics.AdvisoryParams,\n        callbacks: []const api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback,\n    ) !api.metrics.ObservableCounter(f64) {\n        _ = advisory; // Ignore advisory parameters for now\n        _ = callbacks; // Ignore creation-time callbacks for now\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return api.metrics.ObservableCounter(f64){ .noop = name };\n        }\n\n        // Validate parameters in debug mode\n        const validated_name = api.metrics.validateInstrumentName(name);\n        const validated_description = api.metrics.validateInstrumentDescription(description);\n        const validated_unit = api.metrics.validateInstrumentUnit(unit);\n\n        const observable_counter = try self.allocator.create(sdk.async_instr.ObservableCounter(f64));\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(observable_counter);\n\n        observable_counter.* = sdk.async_instr.ObservableCounter(f64).init(\n            self.allocator,\n            validated_name,\n            validated_description,\n            validated_unit,\n            self.async_config,\n        );\n        errdefer observable_counter.deinit();\n\n        try self.observable_counters_f64.append(self.allocator, observable_counter);\n\n        return api.metrics.ObservableCounter(f64){\n            .bridge = api.metrics.AsyncInstrumentBridge.init(observable_counter),\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn createObservableGaugeI64(\n        self: *Meter,\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        advisory: ?api.metrics.AdvisoryParams,\n        callbacks: []const api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback,\n    ) !api.metrics.ObservableGauge(i64) {\n        _ = advisory; // Ignore advisory parameters for now\n        _ = callbacks; // Ignore creation-time callbacks for now\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return api.metrics.ObservableGauge(i64){ .noop = name };\n        }\n\n        // Validate parameters in debug mode\n        const validated_name = api.metrics.validateInstrumentName(name);\n        const validated_description = api.metrics.validateInstrumentDescription(description);\n        const validated_unit = api.metrics.validateInstrumentUnit(unit);\n\n        const observable_gauge = try self.allocator.create(sdk.async_instr.ObservableGauge(i64));\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(observable_gauge);\n\n        observable_gauge.* = sdk.async_instr.ObservableGauge(i64).init(\n            self.allocator,\n            validated_name,\n            validated_description,\n            validated_unit,\n            self.async_config,\n        );\n        errdefer observable_gauge.deinit();\n\n        try self.observable_gauges_i64.append(self.allocator, observable_gauge);\n\n        return api.metrics.ObservableGauge(i64){\n            .bridge = api.metrics.AsyncInstrumentBridge.init(observable_gauge),\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn createObservableGaugeF64(\n        self: *Meter,\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        advisory: ?api.metrics.AdvisoryParams,\n        callbacks: []const api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback,\n    ) !api.metrics.ObservableGauge(f64) {\n        _ = advisory; // Ignore advisory parameters for now\n        _ = callbacks; // Ignore creation-time callbacks for now\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return api.metrics.ObservableGauge(f64){ .noop = name };\n        }\n\n        // Validate parameters in debug mode\n        const validated_name = api.metrics.validateInstrumentName(name);\n        const validated_description = api.metrics.validateInstrumentDescription(description);\n        const validated_unit = api.metrics.validateInstrumentUnit(unit);\n\n        const observable_gauge = try self.allocator.create(sdk.async_instr.ObservableGauge(f64));\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(observable_gauge);\n\n        observable_gauge.* = sdk.async_instr.ObservableGauge(f64).init(\n            self.allocator,\n            validated_name,\n            validated_description,\n            validated_unit,\n            self.async_config,\n        );\n        errdefer observable_gauge.deinit();\n\n        try self.observable_gauges_f64.append(self.allocator, observable_gauge);\n\n        return api.metrics.ObservableGauge(f64){\n            .bridge = api.metrics.AsyncInstrumentBridge.init(observable_gauge),\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn createObservableUpDownCounterI64(\n        self: *Meter,\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        advisory: ?api.metrics.AdvisoryParams,\n        callbacks: []const api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback,\n    ) !api.metrics.ObservableUpDownCounter(i64) {\n        _ = advisory; // Ignore advisory parameters for now\n        _ = callbacks; // Ignore creation-time callbacks for now\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return api.metrics.ObservableUpDownCounter(i64){ .noop = name };\n        }\n\n        // Validate parameters in debug mode\n        const validated_name = api.metrics.validateInstrumentName(name);\n        const validated_description = api.metrics.validateInstrumentDescription(description);\n        const validated_unit = api.metrics.validateInstrumentUnit(unit);\n\n        const observable_counter = try self.allocator.create(sdk.async_instr.ObservableUpDownCounter(i64));\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(observable_counter);\n\n        observable_counter.* = sdk.async_instr.ObservableUpDownCounter(i64).init(\n            self.allocator,\n            validated_name,\n            validated_description,\n            validated_unit,\n            self.async_config,\n        );\n        errdefer observable_counter.deinit();\n\n        try self.observable_updown_counters_i64.append(self.allocator, observable_counter);\n\n        return api.metrics.ObservableUpDownCounter(i64){\n            .bridge = api.metrics.AsyncInstrumentBridge.init(observable_counter),\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn createObservableUpDownCounterF64(\n        self: *Meter,\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        advisory: ?api.metrics.AdvisoryParams,\n        callbacks: []const api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback,\n    ) !api.metrics.ObservableUpDownCounter(f64) {\n        _ = advisory; // Ignore advisory parameters for now\n        _ = callbacks; // Ignore creation-time callbacks for now\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return api.metrics.ObservableUpDownCounter(f64){ .noop = name };\n        }\n\n        // Validate parameters in debug mode\n        const validated_name = api.metrics.validateInstrumentName(name);\n        const validated_description = api.metrics.validateInstrumentDescription(description);\n        const validated_unit = api.metrics.validateInstrumentUnit(unit);\n\n        const observable_counter = try self.allocator.create(sdk.async_instr.ObservableUpDownCounter(f64));\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(observable_counter);\n\n        observable_counter.* = sdk.async_instr.ObservableUpDownCounter(f64).init(\n            self.allocator,\n            validated_name,\n            validated_description,\n            validated_unit,\n            self.async_config,\n        );\n        errdefer observable_counter.deinit();\n\n        try self.observable_updown_counters_f64.append(self.allocator, observable_counter);\n\n        return api.metrics.ObservableUpDownCounter(f64){\n            .bridge = api.metrics.AsyncInstrumentBridge.init(observable_counter),\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Collect metrics from all instruments managed by this meter\n    pub fn collectMetrics(self: *Meter, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) ![]sdk.MetricData {\n        var metrics = std.ArrayList(sdk.MetricData).init(allocator);\n        errdefer metrics.deinit();\n\n        const timestamp_ns = @as(u64, @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()));\n\n        // Collect from i64 counters\n        for (self.counters_i64.items) |counter| {\n            const value = counter.getValue();\n            if (value == 0) continue; // Skip empty counters\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            errdefer allocator.free(data_points);\n            data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = counter.getStartTimestamp(),\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{ .i64_sum = value },\n            };\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = counter.name,\n                .description = counter.description,\n                .unit = counter.unit,\n                .type = .sum,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from f64 counters\n        for (self.counters_f64.items) |counter| {\n            const value = counter.getValue();\n            if (value == 0) continue; // Skip empty counters\n\n            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);\n            data_point.* = .{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = counter.getStartTimestamp(),\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{ .f64_sum = value },\n            };\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            data_points[0] = data_point.*;\n            allocator.destroy(data_point);\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = counter.name,\n                .description = counter.description,\n                .unit = counter.unit,\n                .type = .sum,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from i64 up-down counters\n        for (self.up_down_counters_i64.items) |counter| {\n            const value = counter.getValue();\n\n            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);\n            data_point.* = .{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = counter.getStartTimestamp(),\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{ .i64_sum = value },\n            };\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            data_points[0] = data_point.*;\n            allocator.destroy(data_point);\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = counter.name,\n                .description = counter.description,\n                .unit = counter.unit,\n                .type = .sum,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from f64 up-down counters\n        for (self.up_down_counters_f64.items) |counter| {\n            const value = counter.getValue();\n\n            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);\n            data_point.* = .{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = counter.getStartTimestamp(),\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{ .f64_sum = value },\n            };\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            data_points[0] = data_point.*;\n            allocator.destroy(data_point);\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = counter.name,\n                .description = counter.description,\n                .unit = counter.unit,\n                .type = .sum,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from i64 gauges\n        for (self.gauges_i64.items) |gauge| {\n            const value = gauge.getValue() orelse continue; // Skip if no value recorded\n\n            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);\n            data_point.* = .{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = null, // Gauges don't have start times\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{ .i64_gauge = value },\n            };\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            data_points[0] = data_point.*;\n            allocator.destroy(data_point);\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = gauge.name,\n                .description = gauge.description,\n                .unit = gauge.unit,\n                .type = .gauge,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from f64 gauges\n        for (self.gauges_f64.items) |gauge| {\n            const value = gauge.getValue() orelse continue; // Skip if no value recorded\n\n            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);\n            data_point.* = .{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = null, // Gauges don't have start times\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{ .f64_gauge = value },\n            };\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            data_points[0] = data_point.*;\n            allocator.destroy(data_point);\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = gauge.name,\n                .description = gauge.description,\n                .unit = gauge.unit,\n                .type = .gauge,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from i64 histograms\n        for (self.histograms_i64.items) |histogram| {\n            const count = histogram.getCount();\n            if (count == 0) continue; // Skip empty histograms\n\n            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);\n            data_point.* = .{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = histogram.getStartTimestamp(),\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{\n                    .i64_histogram = .{\n                        .count = count,\n                        .sum = histogram.getSum(),\n                        .min = histogram.getMin(),\n                        .max = histogram.getMax(),\n                        .boundaries = histogram.getBoundaries(),\n                        .bucket_counts = try histogram.getCounts(allocator),\n                    },\n                },\n            };\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            data_points[0] = data_point.*;\n            allocator.destroy(data_point);\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = histogram.name,\n                .description = histogram.description,\n                .unit = histogram.unit,\n                .type = .histogram,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from f64 histograms\n        for (self.histograms_f64.items) |histogram| {\n            const count = histogram.getCount();\n            if (count == 0) continue; // Skip empty histograms\n\n            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);\n            data_point.* = .{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = histogram.getStartTimestamp(),\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{\n                    .f64_histogram = .{\n                        .count = count,\n                        .sum = histogram.getSum(),\n                        .min = histogram.getMin(),\n                        .max = histogram.getMax(),\n                        .boundaries = histogram.getBoundaries(),\n                        .bucket_counts = try histogram.getCounts(allocator),\n                    },\n                },\n            };\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            data_points[0] = data_point.*;\n            allocator.destroy(data_point);\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = histogram.name,\n                .description = histogram.description,\n                .unit = histogram.unit,\n                .type = .histogram,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from observable instruments\n        try self.collectAsynchronousMetrics(allocator, &metrics);\n\n        return metrics.toOwnedSlice();\n    }\n\n    /// Collect metrics from all observable instruments\n    fn collectAsynchronousMetrics(self: *Meter, allocator: std.mem.Allocator, metrics: *std.ArrayList(sdk.MetricData)) !void {\n        // Collect from i64 observable counters\n        for (self.observable_counters_i64.items) |counter| {\n            const data_points = try counter.collect(allocator);\n            if (data_points.len == 0) continue; // Skip if no measurements\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = counter.name,\n                .description = counter.description,\n                .unit = counter.unit,\n                .type = .sum,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from f64 observable counters\n        for (self.observable_counters_f64.items) |counter| {\n            const data_points = try counter.collect(allocator);\n            if (data_points.len == 0) continue; // Skip if no measurements\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = counter.name,\n                .description = counter.description,\n                .unit = counter.unit,\n                .type = .sum,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from i64 observable gauges\n        for (self.observable_gauges_i64.items) |gauge| {\n            const data_points = try gauge.collect(allocator);\n            if (data_points.len == 0) continue; // Skip if no measurements\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = gauge.name,\n                .description = gauge.description,\n                .unit = gauge.unit,\n                .type = .gauge,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from f64 observable gauges\n        for (self.observable_gauges_f64.items) |gauge| {\n            const data_points = try gauge.collect(allocator);\n            if (data_points.len == 0) continue; // Skip if no measurements\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = gauge.name,\n                .description = gauge.description,\n                .unit = gauge.unit,\n                .type = .gauge,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from i64 observable up-down counters\n        for (self.observable_updown_counters_i64.items) |counter| {\n            const data_points = try counter.collect(allocator);\n            if (data_points.len == 0) continue; // Skip if no measurements\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = counter.name,\n                .description = counter.description,\n                .unit = counter.unit,\n                .type = .sum,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from f64 observable up-down counters\n        for (self.observable_updown_counters_f64.items) |counter| {\n            const data_points = try counter.collect(allocator);\n            if (data_points.len == 0) continue; // Skip if no measurements\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = counter.name,\n                .description = counter.description,\n                .unit = counter.unit,\n                .type = .sum,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n    }\n};\n",
  "old_text": "const std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const AsyncInstrumentConfig = @import(\"async_instrument_config.zig\").AsyncInstrumentConfig;\n    const MetricData = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricData;\n    const MetricDataPoint = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricDataPoint;\n    const Resource = @import(\"../resource/resource.zig\").Resource;\n    const async_instr = @import(\"async_instruments.zig\");\n    const sync_instr = @import(\"instruments.zig\");\n};\n\n/// Basic meter implementation (formerly StandardMeter)\npub const Meter = struct {\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    is_shutdown: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    scope: api.InstrumentationScope,\n    resource: sdk.Resource,\n\n    // Synchronous instruments\n    counters_i64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.sync_instr.StandardCounter(i64)),\n    counters_f64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.sync_instr.StandardCounter(f64)),\n    up_down_counters_i64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.sync_instr.StandardUpDownCounter(i64)),\n    up_down_counters_f64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.sync_instr.StandardUpDownCounter(f64)),\n    gauges_i64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.sync_instr.StandardGauge(i64)),\n    gauges_f64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.sync_instr.StandardGauge(f64)),\n    histograms_i64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.sync_instr.StandardHistogram(i64)),\n    histograms_f64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.sync_instr.StandardHistogram(f64)),\n\n    // Observable instruments\n    observable_counters_i64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.async_instr.ObservableCounter(i64)),\n    observable_counters_f64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.async_instr.ObservableCounter(f64)),\n    observable_gauges_i64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.async_instr.ObservableGauge(i64)),\n    observable_gauges_f64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.async_instr.ObservableGauge(f64)),\n    observable_updown_counters_i64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.async_instr.ObservableUpDownCounter(i64)),\n    observable_updown_counters_f64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.async_instr.ObservableUpDownCounter(f64)),\n\n    // Configuration for async instruments\n    async_config: sdk.AsyncInstrumentConfig,\n\n    pub fn init(\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        scope: api.InstrumentationScope,\n        resource: sdk.Resource,\n    ) !Meter {\n        return .{\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .scope = scope,\n            .resource = resource,\n            .is_shutdown = .init(false),\n            .counters_i64 = .empty,\n            .counters_f64 = .empty,\n            .up_down_counters_i64 = .empty,\n            .up_down_counters_f64 = .empty,\n            .gauges_i64 = .empty,\n            .gauges_f64 = .empty,\n            .histograms_i64 = .empty,\n            .histograms_f64 = .empty,\n            .observable_counters_i64 = .empty,\n            .observable_counters_f64 = .empty,\n            .observable_gauges_i64 = .empty,\n            .observable_gauges_f64 = .empty,\n            .observable_updown_counters_i64 = .empty,\n            .observable_updown_counters_f64 = .empty,\n            .async_config = .default,\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn meter(self: *Meter) api.metrics.Meter {\n        return api.metrics.Meter{ .bridge = api.metrics.MeterBridge.init(self) };\n    }\n\n    pub fn deinit(self: *Meter) void {\n        const instruments = .{\n            self.counters_i64,\n            self.counters_f64,\n            self.up_down_counters_i64,\n            self.up_down_counters_f64,\n            self.gauges_i64,\n            self.gauges_f64,\n            self.histograms_i64,\n            self.histograms_f64,\n            self.observable_counters_i64,\n            self.observable_counters_f64,\n            self.observable_gauges_i64,\n            self.observable_gauges_f64,\n            self.observable_updown_counters_i64,\n            self.observable_updown_counters_f64,\n        };\n        inline for (instruments) |list| {\n            for (list.items) |instrument| {\n                instrument.deinit();\n                self.allocator.destroy(instrument);\n            }\n            // The captured var is const, which blocks calling\n            // deinit. But ArrayLists are just pointers to memory\n            // so I can get away with calling deinit on a mutable\n            // copy.\n            // TODO: why can't this just capture the pointer?\n            var mutable_list = list;\n            mutable_list.deinit(self.allocator);\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn shutdown(self: *Meter) void {\n        self.is_shutdown.store(true, .release);\n    }\n\n    pub fn createCounterI64(\n        self: *Meter,\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        advisory: ?api.metrics.AdvisoryParams,\n    ) !api.metrics.Counter(i64) {\n        _ = advisory; // Ignore advisory parameters for now\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return api.metrics.Counter(i64){ .noop = name };\n        }\n\n        // Validate parameters in debug mode\n        const validated_name = api.metrics.validateInstrumentName(name);\n        const validated_description = api.metrics.validateInstrumentDescription(description);\n        const validated_unit = api.metrics.validateInstrumentUnit(unit);\n\n        const counter = try self.allocator.create(sdk.sync_instr.StandardCounter(i64));\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(counter);\n\n        counter.* = try sdk.sync_instr.StandardCounter(i64).init(\n            validated_name,\n            validated_description,\n            validated_unit,\n            self,\n        );\n        errdefer counter.deinit();\n\n        try self: *Meter,\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        advisory: ?api.metrics.AdvisoryParams,\n        callbacks: []const api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback,\n    ) !api.metrics.ObservableUpDownCounter(f64) {\n        _ = advisory; // Ignore advisory parameters for now\n        _ = callbacks; // Ignore creation-time callbacks for now\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return api.metrics.ObservableUpDownCounter(f64){ .noop = name };\n        }\n\n        // Validate parameters in debug mode\n        const validated_name = api.metrics.validateInstrumentName(name);\n        const validated_description = api.metrics.validateInstrumentDescription(description);\n        const validated_unit = api.metrics.validateInstrumentUnit(unit);\n\n        const observable_counter = try self.allocator.create(sdk.async_instr.ObservableUpDownCounter(f64));\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(observable_counter);\n\n        observable_counter.* = sdk.async_instr.ObservableUpDownCounter(f64).init(\n            self.allocator,\n            validated_name,\n            validated_description,\n            validated_unit,\n            self.async_config,\n        );\n        errdefer observable_counter.deinit();\n\n        try self.observable_updown_counters_f64.append(self.allocator, observable_counter);\n\n        return api.metrics.ObservableUpDownCounter(f64){\n            .bridge = api.metrics.AsyncInstrumentBridge.init(observable_counter),\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Collect metrics from all instruments managed by this meter\n    pub fn collectMetrics(self: *Meter, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) ![]sdk.MetricData {\n        var metrics = std.ArrayList(sdk.MetricData).init(allocator);\n        errdefer metrics.deinit();\n\n        const timestamp_ns = @as(u64, @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()));\n\n        // Collect from i64 counters\n        for (self.counters_i64.items) |counter| {\n            const value = counter.getValue();\n            if (value == 0) continue; // Skip empty counters\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            errdefer allocator.free(data_points);\n            data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = counter.getStartTimestamp(),\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{ .i64_sum = value },\n            };\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = counter.name,\n                .description = counter.description,\n                .unit = counter.unit,\n                .type = .sum,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from f64 counters\n        for (self.counters_f64.items) |counter| {\n            const value = counter.getValue();\n            if (value == 0) continue; // Skip empty counters\n\n            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);\n            data_point.* = .{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = counter.getStartTimestamp(),\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{ .f64_sum = value },\n            };\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            data_points[0] = data_point.*;\n            allocator.destroy(data_point);\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = counter.name,\n                .description = counter.description,\n                .unit = counter.unit,\n                .type = .sum,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from i64 up-down counters\n        for (self.up_down_counters_i64.items) |counter| {\n            const value = counter.getValue();\n\n            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);\n            data_point.* = .{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = counter.getStartTimestamp(),\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{ .i64_sum = value },\n            };\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            data_points[0] = data_point.*;\n            allocator.destroy(data_point);\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = counter.name,\n                .description = counter.description,\n                .unit = counter.unit,\n                .type = .sum,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from f64 up-down counters\n        for (self.up_down_counters_f64.items) |counter| {\n            const value = counter.getValue();\n\n            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);\n            data_point.* = .{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = counter.getStartTimestamp(),\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{ .f64_sum = value },\n            };\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            data_points[0] = data_point.*;\n            allocator.destroy(data_point);\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = counter.name,\n                .description = counter.description,\n                .unit = counter.unit,\n                .type = .sum,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from i64 gauges\n        for (self.gauges_i64.items) |gauge| {\n            const value = gauge.getValue() orelse continue; // Skip if no value recorded\n\n            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);\n            data_point.* = .{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = null, // Gauges don't have start times\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{ .i64_gauge = value },\n            };\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            data_points[0] = data_point.*;\n            allocator.destroy(data_point);\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = gauge.name,\n                .description = gauge.description,\n                .unit = gauge.unit,\n                .type = .gauge,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from f64 gauges\n        for (self.gauges_f64.items) |gauge| {\n            const value = gauge.getValue() orelse continue; // Skip if no value recorded\n\n            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);\n            data_point.* = .{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = null, // Gauges don't have start times\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{ .f64_gauge = value },\n            };\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            data_points[0] = data_point.*;\n            allocator.destroy(data_point);\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = gauge.name,\n                .description = gauge.description,\n                .unit = gauge.unit,\n                .type = .gauge,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from i64 histograms\n        for (self.histograms_i64.items) |histogram| {\n            const count = histogram.getCount();\n            if (count == 0) continue; // Skip empty histograms\n\n            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);\n            data_point.* = .{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = histogram.getStartTimestamp(),\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{\n                    .i64_histogram = .{\n                        .count = count,\n                        .sum = histogram.getSum(),\n                        .min = histogram.getMin(),\n                        .max = histogram.getMax(),\n                        .boundaries = histogram.getBoundaries(),\n                        .bucket_counts = try histogram.getCounts(allocator),\n                    },\n                },\n            };\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            data_points[0] = data_point.*;\n            allocator.destroy(data_point);\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = histogram.name,\n                .description = histogram.description,\n                .unit = histogram.unit,\n                .type = .histogram,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from f64 histograms\n        for (self.histograms_f64.items) |histogram| {\n            const count = histogram.getCount();\n            if (count == 0) continue; // Skip empty histograms\n\n            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);\n            data_point.* = .{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = histogram.getStartTimestamp(),\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{\n                    .f64_histogram = .{\n                        .count = count,\n                        .sum = histogram.getSum(),\n                        .min = histogram.getMin(),\n                        .max = histogram.getMax(),\n                        .boundaries = histogram.getBoundaries(),\n                        .bucket_counts = try histogram.getCounts(allocator),\n                    },\n                },\n            };\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            data_points[0] = data_point.*;\n            allocator.destroy(data_point);\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = histogram.name,\n                .description = histogram.description,\n                .unit = histogram.unit,\n                .type = .histogram,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from observable instruments\n        try self.collectAsynchronousMetrics(allocator, &metrics);\n\n        return metrics.toOwnedSlice();\n    }\n\n    /// Collect metrics from all observable instruments\n    fn collectAsynchronousMetrics(self: *Meter, allocator: std.mem.Allocator, metrics: *std.ArrayList(sdk.MetricData)) !void {\n        // Collect from i64 observable counters\n        for (self.observable_counters_i64.items) |counter| {\n            const data_points = try counter.collect(allocator);\n            if (data_points.len == 0) continue; // Skip if no measurements\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = counter.name,\n                .description = counter.description,\n                .unit = counter.unit,\n                .type = .sum,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from f64 observable counters\n        for (self.observable_counters_f64.items) |counter| {\n            const data_points = try counter.collect(allocator);\n            if (data_points.len == 0) continue; // Skip if no measurements\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = counter.name,\n                .description = counter.description,\n                .unit = counter.unit,\n                .type = .sum,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from i64 observable gauges\n        for (self.observable_gauges_i64.items) |gauge| {\n            const data_points = try gauge.collect(allocator);\n            if (data_points.len == 0) continue; // Skip if no measurements\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = gauge.name,\n                .description = gauge.description,\n                .unit = gauge.unit,\n                .type = .gauge,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from f64 observable gauges\n        for (self.observable_gauges_f64.items) |gauge| {\n            const data_points = try gauge.collect(allocator);\n            if (data_points.len == 0) continue; // Skip if no measurements\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = gauge.name,\n                .description = gauge.description,\n                .unit = gauge.unit,\n                .type = .gauge,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from i64 observable up-down counters\n        for (self.observable_updown_counters_i64.items) |counter| {\n            const data_points = try counter.collect(allocator);\n            if (data_points.len == 0) continue; // Skip if no measurements\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = counter.name,\n                .description = counter.description,\n                .unit = counter.unit,\n continue; // Skip if no measurements\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = counter.name,\n                .description = counter.description,\n                .unit = }\n    }\n};\n",

@import(\"../common/scope_context.zig\").InstrumentationScopeMapContext;\n    const Resource = @import(\"../resource/resource.zig\").Resource;\n    const PipelineBuilder = @import(\"../common/pipeline.zig\").PipelineBuilder;\n    const Meter = @import(\"meter.zig\").Meter;\n    const Reader = @import(\"reader.zig\").Reader;\n};\n\n/// Basic meter provider with caching and configuration\npub const MeterProvider = struct {\n    // internal state fields\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    resource: sdk.Resource,\n    cache: std.HashMapUnmanaged(api.InstrumentationScope, *sdk.Meter, sdk.InstrumentationScopeMapContext, 80),\n    readers: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(sdk.Reader),\n    mutex: std.Thread.Mutex,\n\n    pub fn init(\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        resource: sdk.Resource,\n    ) MeterProvider {\n        return .{\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .resource = resource,\n            .cache = .empty,\n            .readers = .empty,\n            .mutex = .{},\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn deinit(self: *MeterProvider) void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Iterate over the meters.\n        var iter = self.cache.iterator();\n        while (iter.next()) |kv| {\n            // Unregister the meter from the processors\n            for (self.readers.items) |*reader| reader.unregisterMeter(kv.value_ptr.*);\n\n            // Clean up the meter.\n            kv.key_ptr.deinitOwned(self.allocator);\n            kv.value_ptr.*.deinit();\n            self.allocator.destroy(kv.value_ptr.*);\n        }\n        self.cache.deinit(self.allocator);\n\n        // Iterate over the processors.\n        for (self.readers.items) |reader| {\n            // Clean up the processor.\n            reader.deinit();\n            reader.destroy();\n        }\n        self.readers.deinit(self.allocator);\n\n        // Clean up the resource.\n        self.resource.deinitOwned(self.allocator);\n    }\n\n    /// Destroys the provider instance (assumes deinit() was already called)\n    pub fn destroy(self: *MeterProvider) void {\n        self.allocator.destroy(self);\n    }\n\n    /// Interface defined method to get a meter.\n    ///\n    /// The provided scope is copied internally.\n    pub fn getMeterWithScope(self: *MeterProvider, scope: api.InstrumentationScope) !api.metrics.Meter {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Check cache first\n        if (self.cache.get(scope)) |meter| {\n            return meter.meter();\n        }\n\n        // Create a locally owned Scope.\n        const owned_scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initOwned(self.allocator, scope);\n        errdefer owned_scope.deinitOwned(self.allocator);\n\n        // Create new SDK meter\n        const sdk_meter = try self.allocator.create(sdk.Meter);\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(sdk_meter);\n\n        sdk_meter.* = try sdk.Meter.init(self.allocator, owned_scope, self.resource, self);\n\n        // Register the meter with the processor for collection\n        //\n        // Iterating over processors should be thread safe as they can\n        // only be mutated at start-up / single threaded.\n        for (self.readers.items) |*reader| reader.registerMeter(sdk_meter);\n\n        try self.cache.put(self.allocator, owned_scope, sdk_meter);\n\n        return sdk_meter.meter();\n    }\n\n    /// Interface defined method to force the attached processor to flush.\n    pub fn forceFlush(self: *MeterProvider, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.FlushResult {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        for (self.readers.items) |*reader| {\n            reader.collect();\n            const flush_result = reader.forceFlush(timeout_ms);\n            switch (flush_result) {\n                .success => {},\n                .failure => return .failure,\n                .timeout => return .timeout,\n            }\n        }\n        return .success;\n    }\n\n    pub fn shutdown(self: *MeterProvider, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult {\n        // The mutex block is distinct because the mutex must be released before\n        // forceFlush can be called.\n        {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n            // Flag each meter as shutdown to stop collection and instrument creation\n            var iter = self.cache.iterator();\n            while (iter.next()) |kv| {\n                kv.value_ptr.*.shutdown();\n            }\n        }\n        const flush_result = self.forceFlush(timeout_ms);\n        return switch (flush_result) {\n            .success => .success,\n            .failure => .failure,\n            .timeout => .timeout,\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Attach a processor to this provider.\n    ///\n    /// This method is not thread-safe and should only be called during initialization.\n    pub fn registerProcessor(self: *MeterProvider, processor: sdk.Reader) !void {\n        try self.readers.append(self.allocator, processor);\n    }\n\n    /// Convert the provider into an API interface.\n    pub fn meterProvider(self: *MeterProvider) api.metrics.MeterProvider {\n        return api.metrics.MeterProvider{ .bridge = api.metrics.MeterProviderBridge.init(self) };\n    }\n\n    /// Generate a pipelinebuilder for this provider.\n    pub fn pipelineBuilder(self: *MeterProvider) sdk.PipelineBuilder(*MeterProvider) {\n        return .init(self);\n    }\n};\n",

  "new_text": "//! OpenTelemetry Basic Meter Provider SDK Implementation\n//!\n//! This module provides the basic concrete implementation of MeterProvider for the SDK.\n//! It manages meter lifecycle, caching, and configuration.\n//!\n//! See: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/metrics/sdk.md\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const Meter = @import(\"meter.zig\").Meter;\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n};\n\n/// Standard Counter implementation that forwards measurements to readers\npub fn StandardCounter(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        metadata_hash: u64,\n\n        pub fn init(\n            name: []const u8,\n            description: ?[]const u8,\n            unit: ?[]const u8,\n            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        ) !@This() {\n            const metadata_hash = sdk.MetricMetadata.computeHash(\n                name,\n                unit orelse \"\",\n                .Counter,\n                parent_meter.scope.name,\n                parent_meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                parent_meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n            );\n\n            return .{\n                .name = name,\n                .description = description,\n                .unit = unit,\n                .meter = parent_meter,\n                .metadata_hash = metadata_hash,\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            _ = self;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n            return self.name;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateCounterValue(i64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Counter.add\", \"Negative value provided\", \"counter values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == i64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n             return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == f64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Counter,\n \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordI64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: i64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordF64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: f64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            return self.aggregation.getStartTime();\n        }\n\n        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Standard UpDownCounter implementation with sum aggregation (allowing negative)\npub fn StandardUpDownCounter(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        aggregation: sdk.aggregations.SumAggregation(T),\n        mutex: std.Thread.Mutex,\n\n        pub fn init(\n            name: []const u8,\n            description: ?[]const u8,\n            unit: ?[]const u8,\n            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        ) !@This() {\n            return .{\n                .name = name,\n (T == f64) {\n                self.mutex.lock();\n                defer self.mutex.unlock();\n                self.aggregation.add(value);\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordI64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: i64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordF64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: f64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getValue(self: *@This()) T {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            return self.aggregation.getValue();\n        }\n\n        pub fn reset(self: *@This()) void {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            self.aggregation.reset();\n        }\n\n        pub fn getStartTimestamp(self: *@This()) u64 {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            return self.aggregation.getStartTime();\n        }\n\n        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Standard Gauge implementation with last value aggregation\npub fn StandardGauge(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        aggregation: sdk.aggregations.LastValueAggregation(T),\n        mutex: std.Thread.Mutex,\n\n        pub fn init(\n   "old_text": "//! OpenTelemetry Basic Meter Provider SDK Implementation\n//!\n//! This module provides the basic concrete implementation of MeterProvider for the SDK.\n//! It manages meter lifecycle, caching, and configuration.\n//!\n//! See: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/metrics/sdk.md\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const Meter = @import(\"meter.zig\").Meter;\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n};\n\n/// Standard Counter implementation that forwards measurements to readers\npub fn StandardCounter(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        metadata_hash: u64,\n\n        pub fn init(\n            name: []const u8,\n            description: ?[]const u8,\n            unit: ?[]const u8,\n            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        ) !@This() {\n            const metadata_hash = sdk.MetricMetadata.computeHash(\n                name,\n                unit orelse \"\",\n                .Counter,\n                parent_meter.scope.name,\n                parent_meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                parent_meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n            );\n\n            return .{\n                .name = name,\n                .description = description,\n                .unit = unit,\n                .meter = parent_meter,\n                .metadata_hash = metadata_hash,\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            _ = self;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n            return self.name;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateCounterValue(i64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Counter.add\", \"Negative value provided\", \"counter values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == i64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn addF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if             return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == f64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordI64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: i64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordF64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: f64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getStartTimestamp(self: *@This()) u64 {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            return self.aggregation.getStartTime();\n        }\n\n        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Standard UpDownCounter implementation with sum aggregation (allowing negative)\npub fn self.aggregation.reset();\n        }\n\n        pub fn getStartTimestamp(self: *@This()) u64 {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            return self.aggregation.getStartTime();\n        }\n\n        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Standard Gauge implementation It manages meter lifecycle, caching, and configuration.\n//!\n//! See: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/metrics/sdk.md\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const Meter = @import(\"meter.zig\").Meter;\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n};\n\n/// Standard Counter implementation that forwards measurements to readers\npub fn StandardCounter(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        metadata_hash: u64,\n\n        pub fn init(\n            name: []const u8,\n            description: ?[]const u8,\n            unit: ?[]const u8,\n            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        ) !@This() {\n            const metadata_hash = sdk.MetricMetadata.computeHash(\n                name,\n                unit orelse \"\",\n                .Counter,\n                parent_meter.scope.name,\n                parent_meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                parent_meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n            );\n\n            return .{\n                .name = name,\n                .description = description,\n                .unit = unit,\n                .meter = parent_meter,\n                .metadata_hash = metadata_hash,\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            _ = self;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n            return self.name;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateCounterValue(i64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Counter.add\", \"Negative value provided\", \"counter values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == i64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn addF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if             return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == f64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordI64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: i64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordF64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: f64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            return self.aggregation.getStartTime();\n        }\n\n        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Standard UpDownCounter }

/// Standard UpDownCounter implementation with sum aggregation (allowing negative)
pub fn StandardUpDownCounter(comptime T: type) type {
    return struct {
        name: []const u8,
        description: ?[]const u8,
        unit: ?[]const u8,
        parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,
        aggregation: sdk.aggregations.SumAggregation(T),
        mutex: std.Thread.Mutex,

        pub fn init(
            name: []const u8,
            description: ?[]const u8,
            unit: ?[]const u8,
            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,
        ) !@This() {
            return .{
                .name = name,
                .description = description,
                .unit = unit,
                .parent_meter = parent_meter,
                .aggregation = .init(),
                .mutex = std.Thread.Mutex{},
            };
        }

        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {
            _ = self;
        }

        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {
            return self.name;
        }

        pub fn addI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {
            _ = ctx;
            _ = attributes;
            if (T == i64) {
                self.mutex.lock();
                defer self.mutex.unlock();
                self.aggregation.add(value);
            } else {
                unreachable;
            }
        }

        pub fn addF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {
            _ = ctx;
            _ = attributes;
            if (T == f64) {
                self.mutex.lock();
                defer self.mutex.unlock();
                self.aggregation.add(value);
            } else {
                unreachable;
            }
        }

        pub fn recordI64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: i64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {
            unreachable;
        }

        pub fn recordF64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: f64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {
            unreachable;
        }

        pub fn getValue(self: *@This()) T {
            self.mutex.lock();
            defer self.mutex.unlock();
            return self.aggregation.getValue();
        }

        pub fn reset(self: *@This()) void {
            self.mutex.lock();
            defer self.mutex.unlock();
            self.aggregation.reset();
        }

        pub fn getStartTimestamp(self: *@This()) u64 {
            self.mutex.lock();
            defer self.mutex.unlock();
            return self.aggregation.getStartTime();
        }

        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {
            _ = self;
            return true;
        }
    };
}

/// Standard Gauge implementation with last value aggregation
pub fn StandardGauge(comptime T: type) type {
    return struct {
        name: []const u8,
        description: ?[]const u8,
        unit: ?[]const u8,
        parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,
        aggregation: sdk.aggregations.LastValueAggregation(T),

/// Standard Gauge implementation with last value aggregation
pub fn StandardGauge(comptime T: type) type {
    return struct {
        name: []const u8,
        description: ?[]const u8,
        unit: ?[]const u8,
        parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,
        aggregation: sdk.aggregations.LastValueAggregation(T),
        mutex: std.Thread.Mutex,

        pub fn init(
            name: []const u8,
            description: ?[]const u8,
            unit: ?[]const u8,
            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,
        ) !@This() {
            return .{
                .name = name,
                .description = description,
                .unit = unit,
                .parent_meter = parent_meter,
                .aggregation = .init(),
                .mutex = std.Thread.Mutex{},
            };
        }

        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {
            _ = self;
        }

        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {
            return self.name;
        }

        pub fn addI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {
            _ = self;
            _ = ctx;
            _ = attributes;
            _ = value;
            unreachable;
        }

        pub fn addF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {
            _ = self;
            _ = ctx;
            _ = attributes;
            _ = value;
            unreachable;
        }

        pub fn recordI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {
            _ = ctx;
            _ = attributes;
            if (T == i64) {
                self.mutex.lock();
                defer self.mutex.unlock();
                self.aggregation.record(value);
            } else {
                unreachable;
            }
        }

        pub fn recordF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {
            _ = ctx;
            _ = attributes;
            if (T == f64) {
                self.mutex.lock();
                defer self.mutex.unlock();
                self.aggregation.record(value);
            } else {
                unreachable;
            }
        }

        pub fn getValue(self: *@This()) ?T {
            self.mutex.lock();
            defer self.mutex.unlock();
            return self.aggregation.getValue();
        }

        pub fn reset(self: *@This()) void {
            self.mutex.lock();
            defer self.mutex.unlock();
            self.aggregation.reset();
        }

        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {
            _ = self;
            return true;
        }
    };
}

    return struct {
        name: []const u8,
        description: ?[]const u8,
        unit: ?[]const u8,
        parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,
        aggregation: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(T),

!@This() {\n            const metadata_hash = sdk.MetricMetadata.computeHash(\n                name,\n                unit orelse \"\",\n                .UpDownCounter,\n                parent_meter.scope.name,\n                parent_meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                parent_meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n            );\n\n            return .{\n                .name = name,\n                .description = description,\n                .unit = unit,\n                .meter = parent_meter,\n                .metadata_hash = metadata_hash,\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            _ = self;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n            return self.name;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == i64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .UpDownCounter,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self,    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordI64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: i64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue)
