pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Standard Gauge implementation that forwards measurements to readers\npub fn StandardGauge(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        metadata_hash: u64,\n\n        pub fn init(\n            name: []const u8,\n            description: ?[]const u8,\n            unit: ?[]const u8,\n            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        ) !@This() {\n            const metadata_hash = sdk.MetricMetadata.computeHash(\n                name,\n                unit orelse \"\",\n                .Gauge,\n                parent_meter.scope.name,\n                parent_meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                parent_meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n            );\n\n            return .{\n                .name = name,\n                .description = description,\n                .unit = unit,\n                .meter = parent_meter,\n                .metadata_hash = metadata_hash,\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            _ = self;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n            return self.name;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            _ = self;\n            _ = ctx;\n            _ = attributes;\n            _ = value;\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            _ = self;\n            _ = ctx;\n            _ = attributes;\n            _ = value;\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == i64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Gauge,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == f64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Gauge,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Standard Histogram implementation with histogram aggregation\npub fn StandardHistogram(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        aggregation: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(T),\n        mutex: std.Thread.Mutex,\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n\n        pub fn init(\n            allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n            name: []const u8,\n            description: ?[]const u8,\n            unit: ?[]const u8,\n            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n            config: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregationConfig,\n        ) !@This() {\n            return .{\n                .name = name,\n                .description = description,\n                .unit = unit,\n                .parent_meter = parent_meter,\n                .aggregation = try .init(allocator, config),\n                .mutex = std.Thread.Mutex{},\n                .allocator = allocator,\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            self.aggregation.deinit(self.allocator);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n            return self.name;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addI64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: i64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addF64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: f64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateHistogramValue(i64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Histogram.record\", \"Negative value provided\", \"histogram values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n            _ = attributes;\n            if (T == i64) {\n                self.mutex.lock();\n                defer self.mutex.unlock();\n                self.aggregation.record(value);\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateHistogramValue(f64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Histogram.record\", \"Negative value provided\", \"histogram values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n            _ = attributes;\n            if (T == f64) {\n                self.mutex.lock();\n                defer self.mutex.unlock();\n                self.aggregation.record(value);\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn getCount(self: *@This()) u64 {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            return self.aggregation.getCount();\n        }\n\n        pub fn getSum(self: *@This()) T {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            return self.aggregation.getSum();\n        }\n\n        pub fn getMin(self: *@This()) ?T {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            return self.aggregation.getMin();\n        }\n\n        pub fn getMax(self: *@This()) ?T {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            return self.aggregation.getMax();\n        }\n\n        pub fn getStartTimestamp(self: *@This()) u64 {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            return self.aggregation.getStartTime();\n        }\n\n        pub fn getBoundaries(self: *@This()) []const f64 {\n            return self.aggregation.getBoundaries();\n        }\n\n        pub fn getCounts(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) ![]u64 {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            const counts = try allocator.dupe(u64, self.aggregation.getCounts());\n            return counts;\n        }\n\n        pub fn reset(self: *@This()) void {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            self.aggregation.reset();\n        }\n\n        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n    };\n}\n",
  "old_text": "//! OpenTelemetry Basic Meter Provider SDK Implementation\n//!\n//! This module provides the basic concrete implementation of MeterProvider for the SDK.\n//! It manages meter lifecycle, caching, and configuration.\n//!\n//! See: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/metrics/sdk.md\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const Meter = @import(\"meter.zig\").Meter;\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n};\n\n/// Standard Counter implementation that forwards measurements to readers\npub fn StandardCounter(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        metadata_hash: u64,\n\n        pub fn init(\n            name: []const u8,\n            description: ?[]const u8,\n            unit: ?[]const u8,\n            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        ) !@This() {\n            const metadata_hash = sdk.MetricMetadata.computeHash(\n                name,\n                unit orelse \"\",\n                .Counter,\n                parent_meter.scope.name,\n                parent_meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                parent_meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n            );\n\n            return .{\n                .name = name,\n                .description = description,\n                .unit = unit,\n                .meter = parent_meter,\n                .metadata_hash = metadata_hash,\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            _ = self;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n            return self.name;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateCounterValue(i64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Counter.add\", \"Negative value provided\", \"counter values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == i64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn addF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateCounterValue(f64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Counter.add\", \"Negative value provided\", \"counter values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == f64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordI64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: i64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordF64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: f64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Standard UpDownCounter implementation that forwards measurements to readers (allowing negative)\npub fn StandardUpDownCounter(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        metadata_hash: u64,\n\n        pub fn init(\n            name: []const u8,\n            description: ?[]const u8,\n            unit: ?[]const u8,\n            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        ) !@This() {\n            const metadata_hash = sdk.MetricMetadata.computeHash(\n                name,\n                unit orelse \"\",\n                .UpDownCounter,\n                parent_meter.scope.name,\n                parent_meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                parent_meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n            );\n\n            return .{\n                .name = name,\n                .description = description,\n                .unit = unit,\n                .meter = parent_meter,\n                .metadata_hash = metadata_hash,\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            _ = self;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n            return self.name;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == i64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .UpDownCounter,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn addF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == f64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .UpDownCounter,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordI64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: i64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordF64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: f64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Standard Gauge implementation with last value aggregation\npub fn StandardGauge(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        aggregation: sdk.aggregations.LastValueAggregation(T),\n        mutex: std.Thread.Mutex,\n\n        pub fn init(\n            name: []const u8,\n            description: ?[]const u8,\n            unit: ?[]const u8,\n            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        ) !@This() {\n            return .{\n                .name = name,\n                .description = description,\n                .unit = unit,\n                .parent_meter = parent_meter,\n                .aggregation = .init(),\n                .mutex = std.Thread.Mutex{},\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            _ = self;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n            return self.name;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            _ = self;\n            _ = ctx;\n            _ = attributes;\n            _ = value;\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            _ = self;\n            _ = ctx;\n            _ = attributes;\n            _ = value;\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            _ = ctx;\n            _ = attributes;\n            if (T == i64) {\n                self.mutex.lock();\n                defer self.mutex.unlock();\n                self.aggregation.record(value);\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            _ = ctx;\n            _ = attributes;\n            if (T == f64) {\n                self.mutex.lock();\n                defer self.mutex.unlock();\n                self.aggregation.record(value);\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn getValue(self: *@This()) ?T {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            return self.aggregation.getValue();\n        }\n\n        pub fn reset(self: *@This()) void {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            self.aggregation.reset();\n        }\n\n        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Standard Histogram implementation with histogram aggregation\npub fn StandardHistogram(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        aggregation: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(T),\n        mutex: std.Thread.Mutex,\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n\n        pub fn init(\n            allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n            name: []const u8,\n            description: ?[]const u8,\n            unit: ?[]const u8,\n            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n            config: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregationConfig,\n        ) !@This() {\n            return .{\n                .name = name,\n                .description = description,\n                .unit = unit,\n                .parent_meter = parent_meter,\n                .aggregation = try .init(allocator, config),\n                .mutex = std.Thread.Mutex{},\n                .allocator = allocator,\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            self.aggregation.deinit(self.allocator);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n            return self.name;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addI64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: i64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addF64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: f64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateHistogramValue(i64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Histogram.record\", \"Negative value provided\", \"histogram values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n            _ = attributes;\n            if (T == i64) {\n                self.mutex.lock();\n                defer self.mutex.unlock();\n                self.aggregation.record(value);\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateHistogramValue(f64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Histogram.record\", \"Negative value provided\", \"histogram values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n            _ = attributes;\n            if (T == f64) {\n                self.mutex.lock();\n                defer self.mutex.unlock();\n                self.aggregation.record(value);\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn getCount(self: *@This()) u64 {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            return self.aggregation.getCount();\n        }\n\n        pub fn getSum(self: *@This()) T {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            return self.aggregation.getSum();\n        }\n\n        pub fn getMin(self: *@This()) ?T {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            return self.aggregation.getMin();\n        }\n\n        pub fn getMax(self: *@This()) ?T {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            return self.aggregation.getMax();\n        }\n\n        pub fn getStartTimestamp(self: *@This()) u64 {\n /// Standard Histogram implementation with histogram aggregation
pub fn StandardHistogram(comptime T: type) type {
    return struct {
        name: []const u8,
        description: ?[]const u8,
        unit: ?[]const u8,
        parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,
        aggregation: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(T),
        mutex: std.Thread.Mutex,
        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,

        pub fn init(
            allocator: std.mem.Allocator,
            name: []const u8,
            description: ?[]const u8,
            unit: ?[]const u8,
            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,
            config: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregationConfig,
        ) !@This() {
            return .{
                .name = name,
                .description = description,
                .unit = unit,
                .parent_meter = parent_meter,
                .aggregation = try .init(allocator, config),
                .mutex = std.Thread.Mutex{},
                .allocator = allocator,
            };
        }

        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {
            self.aggregation.deinit(self.allocator);
        }

        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {
            return self.name;
        }

        pub fn addI64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: i64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {
            unreachable;
        }

        pub fn addF64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: f64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {
            unreachable;
        }

        pub fn recordI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {
            if (!api.metrics.validateHistogramValue(i64, value)) {
                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, "Histogram.record", "Negative value provided", "histogram values must be non-negative");
                return; // Return early in validation mode
            }
            _ = ctx;
            _ = attributes;
            if (T == i64) {
                self.mutex.lock();
                defer self.mutex.unlock();
                self.aggregation.record(value);
            } else {
                unreachable;
            }
        }

        pub fn recordF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {
            if (!api.metrics.validateHistogramValue(f64, value)) {
                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, "Histogram.record", "Negative value provided", "histogram values must be non-negative");
                return; // Return early in validation mode
            }
            _ = ctx;
            _ = attributes;
            if (T == f64) {
                self.mutex.lock();
                defer self.mutex.unlock();
                self.aggregation.record(value);
            } else {
                unreachable;
            }
        }

        pub fn getCount(self: *@This()) u64 {
            self.mutex.lock();
            defer self.mutex.unlock();
            return self.aggregation.getCount();
        }

        pub fn getSum(self: *@This()) T {
            self.mutex.lock();
            defer self.mutex.unlock();
            return self.aggregation.getSum();
        }

        pub fn getMin(self: *@This()) ?T {
            self.mutex.lock();
            defer self.mutex.unlock();
            return self.aggregation.getMin();
        }

        pub fn getMax(self: *@This()) ?T {
            self.mutex.lock();
            defer self.mutex.unlock();
            return self.aggregation.getMax();
        }

        pub fn getStartTimestamp(self: *@This()) u64 {
            self.mutex.lock();
            defer self.mutex.unlock();
            return self.aggregation.getStartTime();
        }

        pub fn getBoundaries(self: *@This()) []const f64 {
            return self.aggregation.getBoundaries();
        }

        pub fn getCounts(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) ![]u64 {
            self.mutex.lock();
            defer self.mutex.unlock();
            const counts = try allocator.dupe(u64, self.aggregation.getCounts());
            return counts;
        }

        pub fn reset(self: *@This()) void {
            self.mutex.lock();
            defer self.mutex.unlock();
            self.aggregation.reset();
        }

        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {
            _ = self;
            return true;
        }
    };
}

 .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n            if (T == f64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Standard Histogram implementation that forwards measurements to readers\npub fn StandardHistogram(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        metadata_hash: u64,\n\n        pub fn init(\n            allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n            name: []const u8,\n            description: ?[]const u8,\n            unit: ?[]const u8,\n            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n            config: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregationConfig,\n        ) !@This() {\n            _ = allocator; // Unused in Phase 1\n            _ = config; // Unused in Phase 1\n\n            const metadata_hash = sdk.MetricMetadata.computeHash(\n                name,\n                unit orelse \"\",\n                .Histogram,\n                parent_meter.scope.name,\n                parent_meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                parent_meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n            );\n\n            return .{\n                .name = name,\n                .description = description,\n                .unit = unit,\n                .meter = parent_meter,\n                .metadata_hash = metadata_hash,\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            _ = self;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n            return self.name;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addI64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: i64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addF64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: f64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateHistogramValue(i64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Histogram.record\", \"Negative value provided\", \"histogram values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == i64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateHistogramValue(f64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Histogram.record\", \"Negative value provided\", \"histogram values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n\n };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n    };\n}\n",
  "old_text": "//! OpenTelemetry Basic Meter Provider SDK Implementation\n//!\n//! This module provides the basic concrete implementation of MeterProvider for the SDK.\n//! It manages meter lifecycle, caching, and configuration.\n//!\n//! See: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/metrics/sdk.md\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const Meter = @import(\"meter.zig\").Meter;\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n};\n\n/// Standard Counter implementation that forwards measurements to readers\npub fn StandardCounter(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        metadata_hash: u64,\n\n        pub fn init(\n            name: []const u8,\n            description: ?[]const u8,\n            unit: ?[]const u8,\n            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        ) !@This() {\n            const metadata_hash = sdk.MetricMetadata.computeHash(\n                name,\n                unit orelse \"\",\n                .Counter,\n                parent_meter.scope.name,\n                parent_meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                parent_meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n            );\n\n            return .{\n                .name = name,\n                .description = description,\n                .unit = unit,\n                .meter = parent_meter,\n                .metadata_hash = metadata_hash,\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            _ = self;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n            return self.name;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateCounterValue(i64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Counter.add\", \"Negative value provided\", \"counter values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == i64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn addF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateCounterValue(f64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Counter.add\", \"Negative value provided\", \"counter values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == f64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordI64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: i64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordF64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: f64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Standard UpDownCounter implementation that forwards measurements to readers (allowing negative)\npub fn StandardUpDownCounter(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        metadata_hash: u64,\n\n        pub fn init(\n            name: []const u8,\n            description: ?[]const u8,\n            unit: ?[]const u8,\n            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        ) !@This() {\n            const metadata_hash = sdk.MetricMetadata.computeHash(\n                name,\n                unit orelse \"\",\n                .UpDownCounter,\n                parent_meter.scope.name,\n                parent_meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                parent_meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n            );\n\n            return .{\n                .name = name,\n                .description = description,\n                .unit = unit,\n                .meter = parent_meter,\n                .metadata_hash = metadata_hash,\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            _ = self;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n            return self.name;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == i64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .UpDownCounter,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn addF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == f64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .UpDownCounter,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordI64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: i64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordF64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: f64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Standard Gauge implementation that forwards measurements to readers\npub fn StandardGauge(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        metadata_hash: u64,\n\n        pub fn init(\n            name: []const u8,\n            description: ?[]const u8,\n            unit: ?[]const u8,\n            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        ) !@This() {\n            const metadata_hash = sdk.MetricMetadata.computeHash(\n                name,\n                unit orelse \"\",\n                .Gauge,\n                parent_meter.scope.name,\n                parent_meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                parent_meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n            );\n\n            return .{\n                .name = name,\n                .description = description,\n                .unit = unit,\n                .meter = parent_meter,\n                .metadata_hash = metadata_hash,\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            _ = self;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n            return self.name;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            _ = self;\n            _ = ctx;\n            _ = attributes;\n            _ = value;\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            _ = self;\n            _ = ctx;\n            _ = attributes;\n            _ = value;\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == i64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Gauge,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == f64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Gauge,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Standard Histogram implementation with histogram aggregation\npub fn StandardHistogram(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        aggregation: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(T),\n        mutex: std.Thread.Mutex,\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n\n        pub fn init(\n            allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n            name: []const u8,\n            description: ?[]const u8,\n            unit: ?[]const u8,\n            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n            config: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregationConfig,\n        ) !@This() {\n            return .{\n                .name = name,\n                .description = description,\n                .unit = unit,\n                .parent_meter = parent_meter,\n                .aggregation = try .init(allocator, config),\n                .mutex = std.Thread.Mutex{},\n                .allocator = allocator,\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            self.aggregation.deinit(self.allocator);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n            return self.name;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addI64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: i64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addF64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: f64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateHistogramValue(i64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Histogram.record\", \"Negative value provided\", \"histogram values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n            _ = attributes;\n            if (T == i64) {\n                self.mutex.lock();\n                defer self.mutex.unlock();\n                self.aggregation.record(value);\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateHistogramValue(f64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Histogram.record\", \"Negative value provided\", \"histogram values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n            _ = attributes;\n            if (T == f64) {\n                self.mutex.lock();\n                defer self.mutex.unlock();\n                self.aggregation.record(value);\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn getCount(self: *@This()) u64 {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            return self.aggregation.getCount();\n        }\n\n        pub fn getSum(self: *@This()) T {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            return self.aggregation.getSum();\n        }\n\n        pub fn getMin(self: *@This()) ?T {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            return self.aggregation.getMin();\n        }\n\n        pub fn getMax(self: *@This()) ?T {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            return self.aggregation.getMax();\n        }\n\n        pub fn getStartTimestamp(self: *@This()) u64 {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            return self.aggregation.getStartTime();\n        }\n\n        pub fn getBoundaries(self: *@This()) []const f64 {\n            return self.aggregation.getBoundaries();\n        }\n\n        pub fn getCounts(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) ![]u64 {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            const counts = try allocator.dupe(u64, self.aggregation.getCounts());\n            return counts;\n        }\n\n        pub fn reset(self: *@This()) void {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            self.aggregation.reset();\n        }\n\n        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n    };\n}\n",

    /// Collect metrics from all instruments managed by this meter
    pub fn collectMetrics(self: *Meter, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) ![]sdk.MetricData {
        var metrics = std.ArrayList(sdk.MetricData).init(allocator);
        errdefer metrics.deinit();

        const timestamp_ns = @as(u64, @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()));

        // Collect from i64 counters
        for (self.counters_i64.items) |counter| {
            const value = counter.getValue();
            if (value == 0) continue; // Skip empty counters

            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);
            errdefer allocator.free(data_points);
            data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{
                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,
                .start_timestamp_ns = counter.getStartTimestamp(),
                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet
                .value = .{ .i64_sum = value },
            };

            try metrics.append(.{
                .name = counter.name,
                .description = counter.description,
                .unit = counter.unit,
                .type = .sum,
                .data_points = data_points,
                .scope = self.scope,
                .resource = self.resource,
            });
        }

        // Collect from f64 counters
        for (self.counters_f64.items) |counter| {
            const value = counter.getValue();
            if (value == 0) continue; // Skip empty counters

            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);
            data_point.* = .{
                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,
                .start_timestamp_ns = counter.getStartTimestamp(),
                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet
                .value = .{ .f64_sum = value },
            };

            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);
            data_points[0] = data_point.*;
            allocator.destroy(data_point);

            try metrics.append(.{
                .name = counter.name,
                .description = counter.description,
                .unit = counter.unit,
                .type = .sum,
                .data_points = data_points,

    /// Collect metrics from all instruments managed by this meter
    pub fn collectMetrics(self: *Meter, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) ![]sdk.MetricData {
        var metrics = std.ArrayList(sdk.MetricData).init(allocator);
        errdefer metrics.deinit();

        const timestamp_ns = @as(u64, @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()));

        // Collect from i64 counters
        for (self.counters_i64.items) |counter| {
            const value = counter.getValue();
            if (value == 0) continue; // Skip empty counters

            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);
            errdefer allocator.free(data_points);
            data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{
                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,
                .start_timestamp_ns = counter.getStartTimestamp(),
                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet
                .value = .{ .i64_sum = value },
            };

            try metrics.append(.{
                .name = counter.name,
                .description = counter.description,
                .unit = counter.unit,
                .type = .sum,
                .data_points = data_points,
                .scope = self.scope,
                .resource = self.resource,
            });
        }

        // Collect from f64 counters
        for (self.counters_f64.items) |counter| {
            const value = counter.getValue();
            if (value == 0) continue; // Skip empty counters

            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);
            data_point.* = .{
                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,
                .start_timestamp_ns = counter.getStartTimestamp(),
                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet
                .value = .{ .f64_sum = value },
            };

            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);
            data_points[0] = data_point.*;
            allocator.destroy(data_point);

            try metrics.append(.{
                .name = counter.name,
                .description = counter.description,
                .unit = counter.unit,
                .type = .sum,
                .data_points = data_points,
                .scope = self.scope,
                .resource = self.resource,
            });
        }

        // Collect from i64 up-down counters
        for (self.up_down_counters_i64.items) |counter| {
            const value = counter.getValue();

            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);
            data_point.* = .{
                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,
                .start_timestamp_ns = counter.getStartTimestamp(),
                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet
                .value = .{ .i64_sum = value },
            };

            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);
            data_points[0] = data_point.*;
            allocator.destroy(data_point);

            try metrics.append(.{
                .name = counter.name,
                .description = counter.description,
                .unit = counter.unit,
                .type = .sum,
                .data_points = data_points,
                .scope = self.scope,
                .resource = self.resource,
            });
        }

        // Collect from f64 up-down counters
        for (self.up_down_counters_f64.items) |counter| {
            const value = counter.getValue();

            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);
            data_point.* = .{
                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,
                .start_timestamp_ns = counter.getStartTimestamp(),
                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet
                .value = .{ .f64_sum = value },
            };

            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);
            data_points[0] = data_point.*;
            allocator.destroy(data_point);

            try metrics.append(.{
                .name = counter.name,
                .description = counter.description,
                .unit = counter.unit,
                .type = .sum,
                .data_points = data_points,
                .scope = self.scope,
                .resource = self.resource,
            });
        }

        // Collect from i64 gauges
        for (self.gauges_i64.items) |gauge| {
            const value = gauge.getValue() orelse continue; // Skip if no value recorded

            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);
            data_point.* = .{
                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,
                .start_timestamp_ns = null, // Gauges don't have start times
                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet
                .value = .{ .i64_gauge = value },
            };

            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);
            data_points[0] = data_point.*;
            allocator.destroy(data_point);

            try metrics.append(.{
                .name = gauge.name,
                .description = gauge.description,
                .unit = gauge.unit,
                .type = .gauge,
                .data_points = data_points,
                .scope = self.scope,
                .resource = self.resource,
            });
        }

        // Collect from f64 gauges
        for (self.gauges_f64.items) |gauge| {
            const value = gauge.getValue() orelse continue; // Skip if no value recorded

            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);
            data_point.* = .{
                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,
                .start_timestamp_ns = null, // Gauges don't have start times
                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet
                .value = .{ .f64_gauge = value },
            };

            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);
            data_points[0] = data_point.*;
            allocator.destroy(data_point);

            try metrics.append(.{
                .name = gauge.name,
                .description = gauge.description,
                .unit = gauge.unit,
                .type = .gauge,
                .data_points = data_points,
                .scope = self.scope,
                .resource = self.resource,
            });
        }

        // Collect from i64 histograms
        for (self.histograms_i64.items) |histogram| {
            const count = histogram.getCount();
            if (count == 0) continue; // Skip empty histograms

            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);
            data_point.* = .{
                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,
                .start_timestamp_ns = histogram.getStartTimestamp(),
                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet
                .value = .{
                    .i64_histogram = .{
                        .count = count,
                        .sum = histogram.getSum(),
                        .min = histogram.getMin(),
                        .max = histogram.getMax(),
                        .boundaries = histogram.getBoundaries(),
                        .bucket_counts = try histogram.getCounts(allocator),
                    },
                },
            };

            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);
            data_points[0] = data_point.*;
            allocator.destroy(data_point);

            try metrics.append(.{
                .name = histogram.name,
                .description = histogram.description,
                .unit = histogram.unit,
                .type = .histogram,
                .data_points = data_points,
                .scope = self.scope,
                .resource = self.resource,
            });
        }

        // Collect from f64 histograms
        for (self.histograms_f64.items) |histogram| {
            const count = histogram.getCount();
            if (count == 0) continue; // Skip empty histograms

            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);
            data_point.* = .{
                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,
                .start_timestamp_ns = histogram.getStartTimestamp(),
                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet
                .value = .{
                    .f64_histogram = .{

                        .sum = histogram.getSum(),
                        .min = histogram.getMin(),

                .unit = histogram.unit,
                .type = .histogram,
                .data_points = data_points,
                .scope = self.scope,
                .resource = self.resource,

        try self.collectAsynchronousMetrics(allocator, &metrics);

implementation (formerly StandardMeter)\npub const Meter = struct {\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    is_shutdown: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    scope: api.InstrumentationScope,\n    resource: sdk.Resource,\n    provider: ?*@import(\"meter_provider.zig\").MeterProvider,\n\n    // Synchronous instruments\n    counters_i64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.sync_instr.StandardCounter(i64)),\n std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.sync_instr.StandardHistogram(f64)),\n\n    // Observable instruments\n    observable_counters_i64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.async_instr.ObservableCounter(i64)),\n    observable_counters_f64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.async_instr.ObservableCounter(f64)),\n    observable_gauges_i64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.async_instr.ObservableGauge(i64)),\n    observable_gauges_f64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.async_instr.ObservableGauge(f64)),\n    observable_updown_counters_i64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.async_instr.ObservableUpDownCounter(i64)),\n    observable_updown_counters_f64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.async_instr.ObservableUpDownCounter(f64)),\n\n    // Configuration for async instruments\n    async_config: sdk.AsyncInstrumentConfig,\n\n    pub fn init(\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        scope: api.InstrumentationScope,\n        resource: sdk.Resource,\n        provider: ?*@import(\"meter_provider.zig\").MeterProvider,\n    ) !Meter {\n        return .{\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .scope = scope,\n            .resource = resource,\n            .provider = provider,\n            .is_shutdown = .init(false),\n            .counters_i64 = .empty,\n            .counters_f64 = .empty,\n            .up_down_counters_i64 = .empty,\n            .up_down_counters_f64 = .empty,\n            .gauges_i64 = .empty,\n            .gauges_f64 = .empty,\n            .histograms_i64 = .empty,\n            .histograms_f64 = .empty,\n            .observable_counters_i64 = .empty,\n            .observable_counters_f64 = .empty,\n            .observable_gauges_i64 = .empty,\n            .observable_gauges_f64 = .empty,\n            .observable_updown_counters_i64 = .empty,\n            .observable_updown_counters_f64 = .empty,\n            .async_config = .default,\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn meter(self: *Meter) api.metrics.Meter {\n        return api.metrics.Meter{ .bridge = api.metrics.MeterBridge.init(self) };\n    }\n\n    pub fn deinit(self: *Meter) void {\n        const instruments = .{\n            self.counters_i64,\n            self.counters_f64,\n            self.up_down_counters_i64,\n            self.up_down_counters_f64,\n            self.gauges_i64,\n            self.gauges_f64,\n            self.histograms_i64,\n            self.histograms_f64,\n            self.observable_counters_i64,\n            self.observable_counters_f64,\n            self.observable_gauges_i64,\n            self.observable_gauges_f64,\n            self.observable_updown_counters_i64,\n            self.observable_updown_counters_f64,\n        };\n        inline for (instruments) |list| {\n            for (list.items) |instrument| {\n                instrument.deinit();\n                self.allocator.destroy(instrument);\n            }\n            // The captured var is const, which blocks calling\n            // deinit. But ArrayLists are just pointers to memory\n            // so I can get away with calling deinit on a mutable\n            // copy.\n            // TODO: why can't this just capture the pointer?\n            var mutable_list = list;\n            mutable_list.deinit(self.allocator);\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn shutdown(self: *Meter) void {\n        self.is_shutdown.store(true, .release);\n    }\n\n    pub fn createCounterI64(\n        self: *Meter,\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        advisory: ?api.metrics.AdvisoryParams,\n    ) !api.metrics.Counter(i64) {\n        _ = advisory; // Ignore advisory parameters for now\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return api.metrics.Counter(i64){ .noop = name };\n        }\n\n        // Validate parameters in debug mode\n        const validated_name = api.metrics.validateInstrumentName(name);\n        const validated_description = api.metrics.validateInstrumentDescription(description);\n        const validated_unit = api.metrics.validateInstrumentUnit(unit);\n\n        const counter = try self.allocator.create(sdk.sync_instr.StandardCounter(i64));\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(counter);\n\n        counter.* = try sdk.sync_instr.StandardCounter(i64).init(\n            validated_name,\n            validated_description,\n            validated_unit,\n            self,\n        );\n        errdefer counter.deinit();\n\n        try self.counters_i64.append(self.allocator, counter);\n\n        return api.metrics.Counter(i64){\n            .bridge = api.metrics.InstrumentBridge.init(counter),\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn createCounterF64(\n        self: *Meter,\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        advisory: ?api.metrics.AdvisoryParams,\n    ) !api.metrics.Counter(f64) {\n        _ = advisory; // Ignore advisory parameters for now\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return api.metrics.Counter(f64){ .noop = name };\n        }\n\n        // Validate parameters in debug mode\n        const validated_name = api.metrics.validateInstrumentName(name);\n        const validated_description = api.metrics.validateInstrumentDescription(description);\n        const validated_unit = api.metrics.validateInstrumentUnit(unit);\n\n        const counter = try self.allocator.create(sdk.sync_instr.StandardCounter(f64));\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(counter);\n\n        counter.* = try sdk.sync_instr.StandardCounter(f64).init(\n            validated_name,\n            validated_description,\n            validated_unit,\n            self,\n        );\n        errdefer counter.deinit();\n\n        try self.counters_f64.append(self.allocator, counter);\n\n        return api.metrics.Counter(f64){\n            .bridge = api.metrics.InstrumentBridge.init(counter),\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn createUpDownCounterI64(\n        self: *Meter,\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        advisory: ?api.metrics.AdvisoryParams,\n    ) !api.metrics.UpDownCounter(i64) {\n        _ = advisory; // Ignore advisory parameters for now\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return api.metrics.UpDownCounter(i64){ .noop = name };\n        }\n\n        // Validate parameters in debug mode\n        const validated_name = api.metrics.validateInstrumentName(name);\n        const validated_description = api.metrics.validateInstrumentDescription(description);\n        const validated_unit = api.metrics.validateInstrumentUnit(unit);\n\n        const counter = try self.allocator.create(sdk.sync_instr.StandardUpDownCounter(i64));\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(counter);\n\n        counter.* = try sdk.sync_instr.StandardUpDownCounter(i64).init(\n            validated_name,\n            validated_description,\n            validated_unit,\n            self,\n        );\n        errdefer counter.deinit();\n\n        try self.up_down_counters_i64.append(self.allocator, counter);\n\n        return api.metrics.UpDownCounter(i64){\n            .bridge = api.metrics.InstrumentBridge.init(counter),\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn createUpDownCounterF64(\n        self: *Meter,\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        advisory: ?api.metrics.AdvisoryParams,\n    ) !api.metrics.UpDownCounter(f64) {\n        _ = advisory; // Ignore advisory parameters for now\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return api.metrics.UpDownCounter(f64){ .noop = name };\n        }\n\n        // Validate parameters in debug mode\n        const validated_name = api.metrics.validateInstrumentName(name);\n        const validated_description = api.metrics.validateInstrumentDescription(description);\n        const validated_unit = api.metrics.validateInstrumentUnit(unit);\n\n        const counter = try self.allocator.create(sdk.sync_instr.StandardUpDownCounter(f64));\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(counter);\n\n        counter.* = try sdk.sync_instr.StandardUpDownCounter(f64).init(\n            validated_name,\n            validated_description,\n            validated_unit,\n            self,\n        );\n        errdefer counter.deinit();\n\n        try self.up_down_counters_f64.append(self.allocator, counter);\n\n        return api.metrics.UpDownCounter(f64){\n            .bridge = api.metrics.InstrumentBridge.init(counter),\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn createGaugeI64(\n        self: *Meter,\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        advisory: ?api.metrics.AdvisoryParams,\n    ) !api.metrics.Gauge(i64) {\n        _ = advisory; // Ignore advisory parameters for now\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return api.metrics.Gauge(i64){ .noop = name };\n        }\n\n        // Validate parameters in debug mode\n        const validated_name = api.metrics.validateInstrumentName(name);\n        const validated_description = api.metrics.validateInstrumentDescription(description);\n        const validated_unit = api.metrics.validateInstrumentUnit(unit);\n\n        const counter = try self.allocator.create(sdk.sync_instr.StandardGauge(i64));\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(counter);\n\n        counter.* = try sdk.sync_instr.StandardGauge(i64).init(\n            validated_name,\n            validated_description,\n            validated_unit,\n            self,\n        );\n        errdefer counter.deinit();\n\n        try self.gauges_i64.append(self.allocator, counter);\n\n        return api.metrics.Gauge(i64){\n            .bridge = api.metrics.InstrumentBridge.init(counter),\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn createGaugeF64(\n        self: *Meter,\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        advisory: ?api.metrics.AdvisoryParams,\n    ) !api.metrics.Gauge(f64) {\n        _ = advisory; // Ignore advisory parameters for now\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return api.metrics.Gauge(f64){ .noop = name };\n        }\n\n        // Validate parameters in debug mode\n        const validated_name = api.metrics.validateInstrumentName(name);\n        const validated_description = api.metrics.validateInstrumentDescription(description);\n        const validated_unit = api.metrics.validateInstrumentUnit(unit);\n\n        const counter = try self.allocator.create(sdk.sync_instr.StandardGauge(f64));\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(counter);\n\n        counter.* = try sdk.sync_instr.StandardGauge(f64).init(\n            validated_name,\n            validated_description,\n            validated_unit,\n            self,\n        );\n        errdefer counter.deinit();\n\n        try self.gauges_f64.append(self.allocator, counter);\n\n        return api.metrics.Gauge(f64){\n            .bridge = api.metrics.InstrumentBridge.init(counter),\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn createHistogramI64(\n        self: *Meter,\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        advisory: ?api.metrics.AdvisoryParams,\n    ) !api.metrics.Histogram(i64) {\n        _ = advisory; // Ignore advisory parameters for now\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return api.metrics.Histogram(i64){ .noop = name };\n        }\n\n        // Validate parameters in debug mode\n        const validated_name = api.metrics.validateInstrumentName(name);\n        const validated_description = api.metrics.validateInstrumentDescription(description);\n        const validated_unit = api.metrics.validateInstrumentUnit(unit);\n\n        const histogram = try self.allocator.create(sdk.sync_instr.StandardHistogram(i64));\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(histogram);\n\n        histogram.* = try sdk.sync_instr.StandardHistogram(i64).init(\n            self.allocator,\n            validated_name,\n            validated_description,\n            validated_unit,\n            self,\n            .{}, // Use default config\n        );\n        errdefer histogram.deinit();\n\n        try self.histograms_i64.append(self.allocator, histogram);\n\n        return api.metrics.Histogram(i64){\n            .bridge = api.metrics.InstrumentBridge.init(histogram),\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn createHistogramF64(\n        self: *Meter,\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        advisory: ?api.metrics.AdvisoryParams,\n    ) !api.metrics.Histogram(f64) {\n        _ = advisory; // Ignore advisory parameters for now\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return api.metrics.Histogram(f64){ .noop = name };\n        }\n\n        // Validate parameters in debug mode\n        const validated_name = api.metrics.validateInstrumentName(name);\n        const validated_description = api.metrics.validateInstrumentDescription(description);\n        const validated_unit = api.metrics.validateInstrumentUnit(unit);\n\n        const histogram = try self.allocator.create(sdk.sync_instr.StandardHistogram(f64));\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(histogram);\n\n        histogram.* = try sdk.sync_instr.StandardHistogram(f64).init(\n            self.allocator,\n            validated_name,\n            validated_description,\n            validated_unit,\n            self,\n            .{}, // Use default config\n        );\n        errdefer histogram.deinit();\n\n        try self.histograms_f64.append(self.allocator, histogram);\n\n        return api.metrics.Histogram(f64){\n            .bridge = api.metrics.InstrumentBridge.init(histogram),\n        };\n    }\n\n    // Observable instrument creation methods\n\n    pub fn createObservableCounterI64(\n        self: *Meter,\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        advisory: ?api.metrics.AdvisoryParams,\n        callbacks: []const api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback,\n    ) !api.metrics.ObservableCounter(i64) {\n        _ = advisory; // Ignore advisory parameters for now\n        _ = callbacks; // Ignore creation-time callbacks for now\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return api.metrics.ObservableCounter(i64){ .noop = name };\n        }\n\n        // Validate parameters in debug mode\n        const validated_name = api.metrics.validateInstrumentName(name);\n        const validated_description = api.metrics.validateInstrumentDescription(description);\n        const validated_unit = api.metrics.validateInstrumentUnit(unit);\n\n        const observable_counter = try self.allocator.create(sdk.async_instr.ObservableCounter(i64));\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(observable_counter);\n\n        observable_counter.* = sdk.async_instr.ObservableCounter(i64).init(\n            self.allocator,\n            validated_name,\n            validated_description,\n            validated_unit,\n            self.async_config,\n        );\n        errdefer observable_counter.deinit();\n\n        try self.observable_counters_i64.append(self.allocator, observable_counter);\n\n        return api.metrics.ObservableCounter(i64){\n            .bridge = api.metrics.AsyncInstrumentBridge.init(observable_counter),\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn createObservableCounterF64(\n        self: *Meter,\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        advisory: ?api.metrics.AdvisoryParams,\n        callbacks: []const api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback,\n    ) !api.metrics.ObservableCounter(f64) {\n        _ = advisory;
