  "old_text": "const std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const AsyncInstrumentConfig = @import(\"async_instrument_config.zig\").AsyncInstrumentConfig;\n    const MetricData = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricData;\n    const MetricDataPoint = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricDataPoint;\n    const Resource = @import(\"../resource/resource.zig\").Resource;\n    const async_instr = @import(\"async_instruments.zig\");\n    const sync_instr = @import(\"instruments.zig\");\n};\n\n/// Basic meter implementation (formerly StandardMeter)\npub const Meter = struct {\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    is_shutdown: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    scope: api.InstrumentationScope,\n    resource: sdk.Resource,\n    provider: ?*@import(\"meter_provider.zig\").MeterProvider,\n\n    // Synchronous instruments\n    counters_i64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.sync_instr.StandardCounter(i64)),\n    counters_f64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.sync_instr.StandardCounter(f64)),\n    up_down_counters_i64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.sync_instr.StandardUpDownCounter(i64)),\n    up_down_counters_f64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.sync_instr.StandardUpDownCounter(f64)),\n    gauges_i64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.sync_instr.StandardGauge(i64)),\n    gauges_f64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.sync_instr.StandardGauge(f64)),\n    histograms_i64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.sync_instr.StandardHistogram(i64)),\n    histograms_f64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.sync_instr.StandardHistogram(f64)),\n\n    // Observable instruments\n    observable_counters_i64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.async_instr.ObservableCounter(i64)),\n    observable_counters_f64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.async_instr.ObservableCounter(f64)),\n    observable_gauges_i64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.async_instr.ObservableGauge(i64)),\n    observable_gauges_f64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.async_instr.ObservableGauge(f64)),\n    observable_updown_counters_i64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.async_instr.ObservableUpDownCounter(i64)),\n    observable_updown_counters_f64: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.async_instr.ObservableUpDownCounter(f64)),\n\n    // Configuration for async instruments\n    async_config: sdk.AsyncInstrumentConfig,\n\n    pub fn init(\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        scope: api.InstrumentationScope,\n        resource: sdk.Resource,\n        provider: ?*@import(\"meter_provider.zig\").MeterProvider,\n    ) !Meter {\n        return .{\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .scope = scope,\n            .resource = resource,\n            .provider = provider,\n            .is_shutdown = .init(false),\n            .counters_i64 = .empty,\n            .counters_f64 = .empty,\n            .up_down_counters_i64 = .empty,\n            .up_down_counters_f64 = .empty,\n            .gauges_i64 = .empty,\n            .gauges_f64 = .empty,\n            .histograms_i64 = .empty,\n            .histograms_f64 = .empty,\n            .observable_counters_i64 = .empty,\n            .observable_counters_f64 = .empty,\n            .observable_gauges_i64 = .empty,\n            .observable_gauges_f64 = .empty,\n            .observable_updown_counters_i64 = .empty,\n            .observable_updown_counters_f64 = .empty,\n            .async_config = .default,\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn meter(self: *Meter) api.metrics.Meter {\n        return api.metrics.Meter{ .bridge = api.metrics.MeterBridge.init(self) };\n    }\n\n    pub fn deinit(self: *Meter) void {\n        const instruments = .{\n            self.counters_i64,\n            self.counters_f64,\n            self.up_down_counters_i64,\n            self.up_down_counters_f64,\n            self.gauges_i64,\n            self.gauges_f64,\n            self.histograms_i64,\n            self.histograms_f64,\n            self.observable_counters_i64,\n            self.observable_counters_f64,\n            self.observable_gauges_i64,\n            self.observable_gauges_f64,\n            self.observable_updown_counters_i64,\n            self.observable_updown_counters_f64,\n        };\n        inline for (instruments) |list| {\n            for (list.items) |instrument| {\n                instrument.deinit();\n                self.allocator.destroy(instrument);\n            }\n            // The captured var is const, which blocks calling\n            // deinit. But ArrayLists are just pointers to memory\n            // so I can get away with calling deinit on a mutable\n            // copy.\n            // TODO: why can't this just capture the pointer?\n            var mutable_list = list;\n            mutable_list.deinit(self.allocator);\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn shutdown(self: *Meter) void {\n        self.is_shutdown.store(true, .release);\n    }\n\n    pub fn createCounterI64(\n        self: *Meter,\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        advisory: ?api.metrics.AdvisoryParams,\n    ) !api.metrics.Counter(i64) {\n        _ = advisory; // Ignore advisory parameters for now\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return api.metrics.Counter(i64){ .noop = name };\n        }\n\n        // Validate parameters in debug mode\n        const validated_name = api.metrics.validateInstrumentName(name);\n        const validated_description = api.metrics.validateInstrumentDescription(description);\n        const validated_unit = api.metrics.validateInstrumentUnit(unit);\n\n        const counter = try self.allocator.create(sdk.sync_instr.StandardCounter(i64));\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(counter);\n\n        counter.* = try sdk.sync_instr.StandardCounter(i64).init(\n            validated_name,\n            validated_description,\n            validated_unit,\n            self,\n        );\n        errdefer counter.deinit();\n\n        try self: *Meter,\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        advisory: ?api.metrics.AdvisoryParams,\n        callbacks: []const api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback,\n    ) !api.metrics.ObservableUpDownCounter(f64) {\n        _ = advisory; // Ignore advisory parameters for now\n        _ = callbacks; // Ignore creation-time callbacks for now\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return api.metrics.ObservableUpDownCounter(f64){ .noop = name };\n        }\n\n        // Validate parameters in debug mode\n        const validated_name = api.metrics.validateInstrumentName(name);\n        const validated_description = api.metrics.validateInstrumentDescription(description);\n        const validated_unit = api.metrics.validateInstrumentUnit(unit);\n\n        const observable_counter = try self.allocator.create(sdk.async_instr.ObservableUpDownCounter(f64));\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(observable_counter);\n\n        observable_counter.* = sdk.async_instr.ObservableUpDownCounter(f64).init(\n            self.allocator,\n            validated_name,\n            validated_description,\n            validated_unit,\n            self.async_config,\n        );\n        errdefer observable_counter.deinit();\n\n        try self.observable_updown_counters_f64.append(self.allocator, observable_counter);\n\n        return api.metrics.ObservableUpDownCounter(f64){\n            .bridge = api.metrics.AsyncInstrumentBridge.init(observable_counter),\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Collect metrics from all instruments managed by this meter\n    pub fn collectMetrics(self: *Meter, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) ![]sdk.MetricData {\n        var metrics = std.ArrayList(sdk.MetricData).init(allocator);\n        errdefer metrics.deinit();\n\n        const timestamp_ns = @as(u64, @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()));\n\n        // Collect from i64 counters\n        for (self.counters_i64.items) |counter| {\n            const value = counter.getValue();\n            if (value == 0) continue; // Skip empty counters\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            errdefer allocator.free(data_points);\n            data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = counter.getStartTimestamp(),\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{ .i64_sum = value },\n            };\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = counter.name,\n                .description = counter.description,\n                .unit = counter.unit,\n                .type = .sum,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from f64 counters\n        for (self.counters_f64.items) |counter| {\n            const value = counter.getValue();\n            if (value == 0) continue; // Skip empty counters\n\n            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);\n            data_point.* = .{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = counter.getStartTimestamp(),\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{ .f64_sum = value },\n            };\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            data_points[0] = data_point.*;\n            allocator.destroy(data_point);\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = counter.name,\n                .description = counter.description,\n                .unit = counter.unit,\n                .type = .sum,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from i64 up-down counters\n        for (self.up_down_counters_i64.items) |counter| {\n            const value = counter.getValue();\n\n            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);\n            data_point.* = .{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = counter.getStartTimestamp(),\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{ .i64_sum = value },\n            };\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            data_points[0] = data_point.*;\n            allocator.destroy(data_point);\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = counter.name,\n                .description = counter.description,\n                .unit = counter.unit,\n                .type = .sum,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from f64 up-down counters\n        for (self.up_down_counters_f64.items) |counter| {\n            const value = counter.getValue();\n\n            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);\n            data_point.* = .{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = counter.getStartTimestamp(),\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{ .f64_sum = value },\n            };\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            data_points[0] = data_point.*;\n            allocator.destroy(data_point);\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = counter.name,\n                .description = counter.description,\n                .unit = counter.unit,\n                .type = .sum,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from i64 gauges\n        for (self.gauges_i64.items) |gauge| {\n            const value = gauge.getValue() orelse continue; // Skip if no value recorded\n\n            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);\n            data_point.* = .{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = null, // Gauges don't have start times\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{ .i64_gauge = value },\n            };\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            data_points[0] = data_point.*;\n            allocator.destroy(data_point);\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = gauge.name,\n                .description = gauge.description,\n                .unit = gauge.unit,\n                .type = .gauge,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from f64 gauges\n        for (self.gauges_f64.items) |gauge| {\n            const value = gauge.getValue() orelse continue; // Skip if no value recorded\n\n            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);\n            data_point.* = .{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = null, // Gauges don't have start times\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{ .f64_gauge = value },\n            };\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            data_points[0] = data_point.*;\n            allocator.destroy(data_point);\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = gauge.name,\n                .description = gauge.description,\n                .unit = gauge.unit,\n                .type = .gauge,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from i64 histograms\n        for (self.histograms_i64.items) |histogram| {\n            const count = histogram.getCount();\n            if (count == 0) continue; // Skip empty histograms\n\n            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);\n            data_point.* = .{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = histogram.getStartTimestamp(),\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{\n                    .i64_histogram = .{\n                        .count = count,\n                        .sum = histogram.getSum(),\n                        .min = histogram.getMin(),\n                        .max = histogram.getMax(),\n                        .boundaries = histogram.getBoundaries(),\n                        .bucket_counts = try histogram.getCounts(allocator),\n                    },\n                },\n            };\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            data_points[0] = data_point.*;\n            allocator.destroy(data_point);\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = histogram.name,\n                .description = histogram.description,\n                .unit = histogram.unit,\n                .type = .histogram,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from f64 histograms\n        for (self.histograms_f64.items) |histogram| {\n            const count = histogram.getCount();\n            if (count == 0) continue; // Skip empty histograms\n\n            const data_point = try allocator.create(sdk.MetricDataPoint);\n            data_point.* = .{\n                .timestamp_ns = timestamp_ns,\n                .start_timestamp_ns = histogram.getStartTimestamp(),\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // MVP: no attribute support yet\n                .value = .{\n                    .f64_histogram = .{\n                        .count = count,\n                        .sum = histogram.getSum(),\n                        .min = histogram.getMin(),\n                        .max = histogram.getMax(),\n                        .boundaries = histogram.getBoundaries(),\n                        .bucket_counts = try histogram.getCounts(allocator),\n                    },\n                },\n            };\n\n            const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n            data_points[0] = data_point.*;\n            allocator.destroy(data_point);\n\n            try metrics.append(.{\n                .name = histogram.name,\n                .description = histogram.description,\n                .unit = histogram.unit,\n                .type = .histogram,\n                .data_points = data_points,\n                .scope = self.scope,\n                .resource = self.resource,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Collect from observable instruments\n        try self.collectAsynchronousMetrics(allocator, &metrics);\n\n        return metrics.toOwnedSlice();\n    }\n\n    /// Collect metrics from all observable instruments\n    fn ## Agent

There are several issues I need to fix:

1. The `recordMeasurementFn` function signature is generic (uses `anytype`) which makes the whole structure comptime-only
2. There are some compilation errors in the aggregation state code
3. Missing InstrumentType reference

Let me fix these issues one by one:

**Use Tool: edit_file (toolu_014zx58KTpDb9Xu9FrdMogTr)**
```json

  "display_description": "Fix recordMeasurement function signature to avoid generic anytype parameter",
  "path": "otel-zig/src/sdk/metrics/reader.zig",
  "mode": "edit"
}
```

Processors handle the timing and batching\n//! of metric exports.\n//!\n//! See: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/metrics/sdk.md#metricreader\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const BasicMeter = @import(\"meter.zig\").Meter;\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n};\n\n/// Union type for metric values to avoid generic anytype parameters\npub const MetricValue = union(enum) {\n    i64: i64,\n    f64: f64,\n};\n\n/// Metric reader interface\npub const Reader = union(enum) {\n    noop: void,\n    bridge: BridgeReader,\n\n    pub fn collect(self: *Reader) void {\n        switch (self.*) {\n            .noop => {},\n            .bridge => |reader| reader.collectFn(reader.reader_ptr),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn registerMeter(self: *Reader, meter: *sdk.BasicMeter) void {\n        switch (self.*) {\n            .noop => {},\n            .bridge => |reader| reader.registerMeterFn(reader.reader_ptr, meter),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn unregisterMeter(self: *Reader, meter: *sdk.BasicMeter) void {\n        switch (self.*) {\n            .noop => {},\n            .bridge => |reader| reader.unregisterMeterFn(reader.reader_ptr, meter),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn forceFlush(self: *Reader, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult {\n        return switch (self.*) {\n            .noop => .success,\n            .bridge => |reader| reader.forceFlushFn(reader.reader_ptr, timeout_ms),\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn shutdown(self: *Reader, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult {\n        return switch (self.*) {\n            .noop => .success,\n            .bridge => |reader| reader.shutdownFn(reader.reader_ptr, timeout_ms),\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up reader resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *const Reader) void {\n        switch (self.*) {\n            .noop => {},\n            .bridge => |reader| reader.deinitFn(reader.reader_ptr),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Destroy reader memory\n    pub fn destroy(self: *const Reader) void {\n        switch (self.*) {\n            .noop => {},\n            .bridge => |reader| reader.destroyFn(reader.reader_ptr),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Record a measurement from an instrument\n    pub fn recordMeasurement(\n        self: *Reader,\n .noop => {},\n            .bridge => |reader| reader.recordMeasurementFn(reader.reader_ptr, instrument, value, attributes, metadata),\n        }\n    }\n};\n\n/// Interface for bridging to a more complex reader.\npub const BridgeReader = struct {\n    reader_ptr: *anyopaque,\n    collectFn: *const fn (reader_ptr: *anyopaque) void,\n    forceFlushFn: *const fn (reader_ptr: *anyopaque, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult,\n    shutdownFn: *const fn (reader_ptr: *anyopaque, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult,\n    deinitFn: *const fn (reader_ptr: *anyopaque) void,\n meter: *sdk.BasicMeter) void,\n    recordMeasurementFn: *const fn (reader_ptr: *anyopaque, instrument: *anyopaque, value: MetricValue, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue, metadata: @ptrCast(@alignCast(pointer));\n                return ptr_info.pointer.child.shutdown(self, timeout_ms);\n            }\n            pub fn deinit(pointer: *anyopaque) void {\n                const self: T = @ptrCast(@alignCast(pointer));\n                return ptr_info.pointer.child.deinit(self);\n            }\n            pub fn destroy(pointer: *anyopaque) void {\n                const self: T = @ptrCast(@alignCast(pointer));\n                return ptr_info.pointer.child.destroy(self);\n            }\n            pub fn registerMeter(pointer: *anyopaque, meter: *sdk.BasicMeter)  /// Aggregation temporality for metric data points

+            .sum_f64 => |*s| switch (value) {

+                .i64 => {},

+                .f64 => {},

+            .last_value_f64 => |*lv| switch (value) {
+                .f64 => |v| lv.record(v),
+                .i64 => {},

@import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const InstrumentType = @import(\"metadata.zig\").InstrumentType;\n    const for metric data points\npub const AggregationTemporality = enum {\n    Delta,\n    Cumulative,\n};\n\n/// Union type for all possible aggregations\npub const Aggregation = union(enum) {\n    sum_i64: sdk.aggregations.SumAggregation(i64),\n    sum_f64: sdk.aggregations.SumAggregation(f64),\n    last_value_i64: sdk.aggregations.LastValueAggregation(i64),\n    last_value_f64: => .Sum,\n        .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => .LastValue,\n        .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => .Histogram,\n    };\n}\n\n/// Per-reader aggregation state management\npub const ReaderAggregationState = struct {\n    // Phase 1: Map from instrument pointer to dynamically allocated aggregation\n    aggregations: std.AutoHashMap(*anyopaque, *Aggregation),\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    mutex: std.Thread.Mutex, // Protects map access in Phase 1\n\n    // Reader's configured temporality\n    temporality: AggregationTemporality,\n\n    // Aggregation selector selector based on instrument type\npub fn defaultAggregationSelector(instrument_type: sdk.MetricMetadata.InstrumentType) AggregationType {\n    return switch (instrument_type) {\n        .Counter, Per-reader aggregation state management\npub const ReaderAggregationState = struct {\n    // Phase 1: Map from instrument pointer to dynamically allocated aggregation\n    aggregations: std.AutoHashMap(*anyopaque, *Aggregation),\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    mutex: std.Thread.Mutex, // Protects map access in Phase 1\n\n    // Reader's configured temporality\n    temporality: AggregationTemporality,\n\n    // Aggregation selector (determines aggregation type per instrument)\n    aggregation_selector: AggregationSelector,\n\n    // For cumulative temporality, track last collection time\n    last_collection_time_ns: u64,\n\n    /// Initialize reader aggregation state\n    pub fn init(\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        temporality: AggregationTemporality,\n        aggregation_selector: AggregationSelector,\n    ) @This() {\n        return .{\n            .aggregations = std.AutoHashMap(*anyopaque, *Aggregation).init(allocator),\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .mutex = .{},\n            .temporality = temporality,\n            .aggregation_selector = aggregation_selector,\n            .last_collection_time_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up all aggregations and resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Clean up dynamically allocated aggregations\n        var iter = self.aggregations.iterator();\n        while (iter.next()) |entry| from an instrument\n    pub fn recordMeasurement(\n        self: *@This(),\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        value: anytype,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n    ) void {\n        _ = attributes; // Unused in Phase 1 (used in Phase 1b for attribute-based aggregation)\n\n        // Phase 1: Thread-safe map access\n {
  "original_path": "src/sdk/metrics/manual_reader.zig",
  "new_text": "//! OpenTelemetry Basic Metric Processor\n//!\n//! This module provides the BasicMetricProcessor that exports metrics immediately\n//! when collected. It's the simplest metric processor implementation.\n//!\n//! See: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/metrics/sdk.md#metricreader\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const BridgeMetricReader = @import(\"reader.zig\").BridgeReader;\n    const Meter = @import(\"meter.zig\").Meter;\n    const MetricExporter = @import(\"exporter.zig\").MetricExporter;\n    const Reader = @import(\"reader.zig\").Reader;\n    const MetricData = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricData;\n    const ReaderAggregationState = @import(\"reader_aggregation_state.zig\").ReaderAggregationState;\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const MetricValue = @import(\"reader.zig\").MetricValue;\n};\n\n/// Basic log processor implementation.\n///\n/// Simple processor that exports metrics manually. Users must invoke `forceFlush`.\npub const ManualReader = struct {\n    pub const PipelineStep = @import(\"../common/pipeline.zig\").PipelineStepInstructions(\n        ManualReader,\n        sdk.Reader,\n        void,\n        reader,\n        _initFn,\n        setExporter,\n    );\n    pub fn _initFn(self: *ManualReader, _: void, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) !void {\n        self.* = init(allocator, null);\n    }\n\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    exporter: ?sdk.MetricExporter,\n    mutex: std.Thread.Mutex,\n    is_shutdown: bool,\n    registered_meters: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.Meter),\n    reader_state: sdk.ReaderAggregationState,\n\n    pub fn init(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, exporter: ?sdk.MetricExporter) ManualReader {\n        return .{\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .exporter = exporter,\n            .mutex = .{},\n            .is_shutdown = false,\n            .registered_meters = .{},\n            .reader_state = sdk.ReaderAggregationState.init(\n                allocator,\n                .Delta, // Default to Delta temporality for now\n                @import(\"reader_aggregation_state.zig\").defaultAggregationSelector,\n            ),\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn deinit(self: *ManualReader) void {\n        self.reader_state.deinit();\n        self.registered_meters.deinit(self.allocator);\n        if (self.exporter) |exporter| {\n            exporter.deinit();\n            exporter.destroy();\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn destroy(self: *ManualReader) void {\n        self.allocator.destroy(self);\n    }\n\n    pub fn setExporter(self: *ManualReader, exporter: ?sdk.MetricExporter) !void {\n        if (self.exporter) |old_exporter| {\n            old_exporter.deinit();\n            old_exporter.destroy();\n        }\n        self.exporter = exporter;\n    }\n\n    pub fn recordMeasurement(\n        self: *ManualReader,\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        value: sdk.MetricValue,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n    ) void {\n        self.reader_state.recordMeasurement(instrument, value, attributes, metadata);\n    }\n\n    pub fn collect(self: *ManualReader) void {\n        var arena = std.heap.ArenaAllocator.init(self.allocator);\n        defer arena.deinit();\n        const arena_allocator = arena.allocator();\n\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Collect from reader state instead of iterating meters\n        const collected_metrics = self.reader_state.collect(arena_allocator) catch {\n            // Log error if needed\n            return;\n        };\n\n        // Export all collected metrics. Exporter must copy memory\n        // that it needs beyond the duration of this call.\n        if (self.exporter) |*exporter| {\n            const result = exporter.exportMetrics(collected_metrics);\n            if (result != .success) {\n                api.common.reportError(.{\n                    .component = .processor,\n                    .operation = \"metric_export\",\n                    .error_type = .network,\n                    .message = \"Failed to export metrics\",\n                    .context = null,\n                });\n            }\n        }\n        // Arena cleans up all the memory.\n    }\n\n    pub fn forceFlush(self: *ManualReader, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_shutdown) {\n            return .failure;\n        }\n\n        // Flush the exporter\n        const result = if (self.exporter) |*exporter| exporter.forceFlush(timeout_ms) else .success;\n        return result.asProcessResult();\n    }\n\n    pub fn shutdown(self: *ManualReader, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer   "old_text": "//! OpenTelemetry Basic Metric Processor\n//!\n//! This module provides the BasicMetricProcessor that exports metrics immediately\n//! when collected. It's the simplest metric processor implementation.\n//!\n//! See: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/metrics/sdk.md#metricreader\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const BridgeMetricReader = @import(\"reader.zig\").BridgeReader;\n    const Meter = @import(\"meter.zig\").Meter;\n    const MetricExporter = @import(\"exporter.zig\").MetricExporter;\n    const Reader = @import(\"reader.zig\").Reader;\n    const MetricData = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricData;\n    const ReaderAggregationState = @import(\"reader_aggregation_state.zig\").ReaderAggregationState;\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n};\n\n/// Basic log processor implementation.\n///\n/// Simple processor that exports metrics manually. Users must invoke `forceFlush`.\npub const ManualReader = struct {\n    pub const PipelineStep = @import(\"../common/pipeline.zig\").PipelineStepInstructions(\n        ManualReader,\n        sdk.Reader,\n        void,\n        reader,\n        _initFn,\n        setExporter,\n    );\n    pub fn _initFn(self: *ManualReader, _: void, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) !void {\n        self.* = init(allocator, null);\n    }\n\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    exporter: ?sdk.MetricExporter,\n    mutex: std.Thread.Mutex,\n    is_shutdown: bool,\n    registered_meters: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.Meter),\n    reader_state: sdk.ReaderAggregationState,\n\n    pub fn init(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, exporter: ?sdk.MetricExporter) ManualReader {\n        return .{\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .exporter = exporter,\n            .mutex = .{},\n            .is_shutdown = false,\n            .registered_meters = .{},\n            .reader_state = sdk.ReaderAggregationState.init(\n                allocator,\n                .Delta, // Default to Delta temporality for now\n                @import(\"reader_aggregation_state.zig\").defaultAggregationSelector,\n            ),\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn deinit(self: *ManualReader) void {\n        self.reader_state.deinit();\n        self.registered_meters.deinit(self.allocator);\n        if (self.exporter) |exporter| {\n            exporter.deinit();\n            exporter.destroy();\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn destroy(self: *ManualReader) void {\n        self.allocator.destroy(self);\n    }\n\n    pub fn setExporter(self: *ManualReader, exporter: ?sdk.MetricExporter) !void {\n        if (self.exporter) |old_exporter| {\n            old_exporter.deinit();\n            old_exporter.destroy();\n        }\n        self.exporter = exporter;\n    }\n\n    pub fn recordMeasurement(\n        self: *ManualReader,\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        value: anytype,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n    ) void {\n        self.reader_state.recordMeasurement(instrument, value, attributes, metadata);\n    }\n\n    pub fn collect(self: *ManualReader) void {\n        var arena = std.heap.ArenaAllocator.init(self.allocator);\n        defer arena.deinit();\n        const arena_allocator = arena.allocator();\n\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Collect from reader state instead of iterating meters\n        const collected_metrics = self.reader_state.collect(arena_allocator) catch {\n            // Log error if needed\n            return;\n        };\n\n        // Export all collected metrics. Exporter must copy memory\n        // that it needs beyond the duration of this call.\n        if (self.exporter) |*exporter| {\n            const result = exporter.exportMetrics(collected_metrics);\n            if (result != .success) {\n                api.common.reportError(.{\n                    .component = .processor,\n                    .operation = \"metric_export\",\n                    .error_type = .network,\n                    .message = \"Failed to export metrics\",\n                    .context = null,\n                });\n            }\n        }\n        // Arena cleans up all the memory.\n    }\n\n    pub fn forceFlush(self: *ManualReader, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_shutdown) {\n            return .failure;\n        }\n\n        // Flush the exporter\n        const result = if (self.exporter) |*exporter| exporter.forceFlush(timeout_ms) else .success;\n        return result.asProcessResult();\n    }\n\n    pub fn shutdown(self: *ManualReader, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_shutdown) {\n            return .success;\n        }\n\n        self.is_shutdown = true;\n\n        // Shutdown the exporter\n        const result = if (self.exporter) |*exporter| exporter.shutdown(timeout_ms) else .success;\n        return result.asProcessResult();\n    }\n\n    pub fn registerMeter(self: *ManualReader, meter: *sdk.Meter) void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_shutdown) return;\n\n        self.registered_meters.append(self.allocator, meter) catch {\n            // Handle allocation failure silently for now\n            return;\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn unregisterMeter(self: *ManualReader, meter: *sdk.Meter) void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_shutdown) return;\n\n        for (self.registered_meters.items, 0..) |registered_meter, i| {\n            if (registered_meter == meter) {\n                _ = self.registered_meters.swapRemove(i);\n                break;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn reader(self: *ManualReader) sdk.Reader {\n        return .{ .bridge = sdk.BridgeMetricReader.init(self) };\n    }\n};\n",

  "new_text": "//! Basic Periodic Metrics Processor\n//!\n//! This module provides a basic metrics processor that collects metrics from registered\n//! meters on a periodic basis using a background thread. It uses POSIX threads\n//! for cross-platform compatibility.\n//!\n//! The processor maintains a thread that wakes up at regular intervals to collect\n//! metrics from all registered meters and export them via the configured exporter.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\nconst c = std.c;\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const BridgeReader = @import(\"reader.zig\").BridgeReader;\n    const Meter = @import(\"meter.zig\").Meter;\n    const MetricData = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricData;\n    const Reader = @import(\"reader.zig\").Reader;\n    const MetricExporter = @import(\"exporter.zig\").MetricExporter;\n    const ReaderAggregationState = @import(\"reader_aggregation_state.zig\").ReaderAggregationState;\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const MetricValue = @import(\"reader.zig\").MetricValue;\n};\n\n/// Basic periodic metrics processor that collects metrics at regular intervals\npub const PeriodicReader = struct {\n    pub const PipelineStep = @import(\"../common/pipeline.zig\").PipelineStepInstructions(\n        PeriodicReader,\n        sdk.Reader,\n        ?u32,\n        reader,\n        _initFn,\n        setExporter,\n    );\n    pub fn _initFn(self: *PeriodicReader, interval: ?u32, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) !void {\n        self.* = init(allocator, null, interval);\n        try self.start();\n    }\n\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    exporter: ?sdk.MetricExporter,\n    mutex: std.Thread.Mutex,\n    condition: std.Thread.Condition,\n    is_shutdown: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    is_running: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    flush_in_progress: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    collection_in_progress: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    flush_complete: registered_meters: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.Meter),\n    reader_state: sdk.ReaderAggregationState,\n\n    /// Initialize a new basic periodic metrics processor\n    /// collection_interval_ms: How often to collect metrics (default: 60000ms = 60s)\n    pub fn init(\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        exporter: ?sdk.MetricExporter,\n        collection_interval_ms: ?u32,\n    ) PeriodicReader {\n        return .{\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .exporter = exporter,\n            .mutex = .{},\n            .condition = .{},\n            .is_shutdown = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n            .is_running = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n            .flush_in_progress = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n            .collection_in_progress = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n            .flush_complete = .{},\n            .collection_complete = .{},\n            .last_collection_time = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n            .thread = null,\n            .collection_interval_ms = collection_interval_ms orelse 60000, // 60 seconds default\n            .registered_meters = .{},\n            .reader_state = sdk.ReaderAggregationState.init(\n                allocator,\n                .Delta, // Default to Delta temporality for now\n                @import(\"reader_aggregation_state.zig\").defaultAggregationSelector,\n            ),\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Start the background collection thread\n    pub fn start(self: *PeriodicReader) !void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_running.load(.acquire) or self.thread != null) {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        self.is_running.store(true, .release);\n        self.thread = try std.Thread.spawn(.{}, collectionThreadFn, .{self});\n    }\n\n    /// Stop the background collection thread and clean up resources\n (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return .failure;\n        }\n\n        const start_time = std.time.milliTimestamp();\n\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Try to set flush_in_progress atomically\n        const was_flushing = self.flush_in_progress.swap(true, .seq_cst);\n        if (was_flushing) {\n            // self.flush_complete.wait(&self.mutex);\n            }\n            return .success;\n        }\n\n        defer {\n            self.flush_in_progress.store(false, .release);\n            self.flush_complete.broadcast();\n        }\n\n        // Wait for any collection in progress\n        while (self.collection_in_progress.load(.acquire)) {\n            const remaining_ms = if return .timeout;\n            }\n\n            if (remaining_ms) |ms| {\n                self.collection_complete.timedWait(&self.mutex, ms * std.time.ns_per_ms) catch {\n                    return .timeout;\n                };\n            } else {\n                self.collection_complete.wait(&self.mutex);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Now do immediate collection with atomic flag\n }\n\n        // Perform immediate metric collection\n        var arena = std.heap.ArenaAllocator.init(self.allocator);\n        defer arena.deinit();\n\n        // Collect from reader state instead of iterating meters\n        const collected_metrics = self.reader_state.collect(arena.allocator()) catch {\n            return .failure;\n        };\n\n        // Update last collection time atomically\n        self.last_collection_time.store(std.time.milliTimestamp(), .release);\n\n        // Export if we have metrics\n        if (collected_metrics.len > 0 and self.exporter != null) {\n            // Temporarily release mutex for export\n            self.mutex.unlock();\n            const export_result = self.exporter.?.exportMetrics(collected_metrics);\n            self.mutex.lock();\n\n            if (export_result != .success) {\n                return .failure;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Flush the exporter\n        if (self.exporter) |*exporter| {\n            self.mutex.unlock();\n            const flush_result = exporter.forceFlush(timeout_ms);\n            self.mutex.lock();\n\n            return if (flush_result == .success) .success else .failure;\n        }\n\n        return .success;\n    }\n\n    /// Shutdown the processor\n    pub fn shutdown(self: *PeriodicReader, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult {\n .release);\n        self.condition.signal();\n\n        // Shutdown the exporter\n        const result = if (self.exporter) |*exporter| exporter.shutdown(timeout_ms) else .success;\n        return if (result == .success) .success else .failure;\n    }\n\n    /// Register a meter for periodic collection\n    pub fn registerMeter(self: *PeriodicReader, meter: *sdk.Meter) void {\n the exporter for this processor\n    pub fn setExporter(self: *PeriodicReader, exporter: ?sdk.MetricExporter) !void {\n        if (self.exporter) |old_exporter| {\n            old_exporter.deinit();\n \"BasicPeriodicProcessor - no thread start without meters\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    // Create processor\n    var processor = PeriodicReader.init(allocator, null, 100);\n    defer processor.deinit();\n\n    // Verify thread is not running\n    try testing.expect(!processor.is_running.load(.acquire));\n    try testing.expect(processor.thread == null);\n\n    // Call forceFlush - should not crash\n    const result = processor.forceFlush(100);\n    try testing.expect(result == .success);\n\n    // Thread should still not be running\n    try testing.expect(!processor.is_running.load(.acquire));\n    try testing.expect(processor.thread == null);\n\n    // Shutdown should work fine\n    _ = processor.shutdown(100);\n    try testing.expect(processor.is_shutdown.load(.acquire));\n}\n",
  "old_text": "//! Basic Periodic Metrics Processor\n//!\n//! This module provides a basic metrics processor that collects metrics from registered\n//! meters on a periodic basis using a background thread. It uses POSIX threads\n//! for cross-platform compatibility.\n//!\n//! The processor maintains a thread that wakes up at regular intervals to collect\n//! metrics from all registered meters and export them via the configured exporter.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\nconst c = std.c;\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const BridgeReader = @import(\"reader.zig\").BridgeReader;\n    const Meter = @import(\"meter.zig\").Meter;\n    const MetricData = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricData;\n    const Reader = @import(\"reader.zig\").Reader;\n    const MetricExporter = @import(\"exporter.zig\").MetricExporter;\n    const ReaderAggregationState = @import(\"reader_aggregation_state.zig\").ReaderAggregationState;\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n};\n\n/// Basic periodic metrics processor that collects metrics at regular intervals\npub const PeriodicReader = struct {\n    pub const PipelineStep = @import(\"../common/pipeline.zig\").PipelineStepInstructions(\n        PeriodicReader,\n        sdk.Reader,\n        ?u32,\n        reader,\n        _initFn,\n        setExporter,\n    );\n    pub fn _initFn(self: *PeriodicReader, interval: ?u32, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) !void {\n        self.* = init(allocator, null, interval);\n        try self.start();\n    }\n\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    exporter: ?sdk.MetricExporter,\n    mutex: std.Thread.Mutex,\n    condition: std.Thread.Condition,\n    is_shutdown: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    is_running: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    flush_in_progress: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    collection_in_progress: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    flush_complete: std.Thread.Condition,\n    collection_complete: std.Thread.Condition,\n    last_collection_time: std.atomic.Value(i64),\n    thread: ?std.Thread,\n    collection_interval_ms: u32,\n    registered_meters: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.Meter),\n    reader_state: sdk.ReaderAggregationState,\n\n    /// Initialize a new basic periodic metrics processor\n    /// collection_interval_ms: How often to collect metrics (default: 60000ms = 60s)\n    pub fn init(\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        exporter: ?sdk.MetricExporter,\n        collection_interval_ms: ?u32,\n    ) PeriodicReader {\n        return .{\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .exporter = exporter,\n            .mutex = .{},\n            .condition = .{},\n            .is_shutdown = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n            .is_running = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n            .flush_in_progress = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n            .collection_in_progress = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n            .flush_complete = .{},\n            .collection_complete = .{},\n            .last_collection_time = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n            .thread = null,\n            .collection_interval_ms = collection_interval_ms orelse 60000, // 60 seconds default\n            .registered_meters = .{},\n            .reader_state = sdk.ReaderAggregationState.init(\n                allocator,\n                .Delta, // Default to Delta temporality for now\n                @import(\"reader_aggregation_state.zig\").defaultAggregationSelector,\n            ),\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Start the background collection thread\n    pub fn start(self: *PeriodicReader) !void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_running.load(.acquire) or self.thread != null) {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        self.is_running.store(true, .release);\n        self.thread = try std.Thread.spawn(.{}, collectionThreadFn, .{self});\n    }\n\n    /// Stop the background collection thread and clean up resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *PeriodicReader) void {\n        // Signal shutdown\n        self.is_shutdown.store(true, .release);\n        self.is_running.store(false, .release);\n\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        self.condition.signal();\n        self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Wait for thread to finish\n        if (self.thread) |thread| {\n            thread.join();\n        }\n\n        // Clean up resources\n        self.reader_state.deinit();\n        self.registered_meters.deinit(self.allocator);\n        if (self.exporter) |exporter| {\n            exporter.deinit();\n            exporter.destroy();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Destroy the processor and free its memory\n    pub fn destroy(self: *PeriodicReader) void {\n        self.allocator.destroy(self);\n    }\n\n    pub fn recordMeasurement(\n        self: *PeriodicReader,\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        value: anytype,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n    ) void {\n        self.reader_state.recordMeasurement(instrument, value, attributes, metadata);\n    }\n\n    /// Collect metrics from all registered meters (called by background thread)\n    pub fn collect(self: *PeriodicReader) void {\n        var arena = std.heap.ArenaAllocator.init(self.allocator);\n        defer arena.deinit();\n        const arena_allocator = arena.allocator();\n\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) return;\n\n        // Collect from reader state instead of iterating meters\n        const collected_metrics = self.reader_state.collect(arena_allocator) catch {\n            // Log error if needed\n            return;\n        };\n\n        // Export all collected metrics. Exporter must copy memory\n        // that it needs beyond the duration of this call.\n        if (self.exporter) |*exporter| _ = exporter.exportMetrics(collected_metrics);\n        // Arena cleans up all the memory.\n    }\n\n    /// Force flush the exporter\n    pub fn forceFlush(self: *PeriodicReader, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult {\n        // Quick check without mutex\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return .failure;\n        }\n\n        const start_time = std.time.milliTimestamp();\n\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Try to set flush_in_progress atomically\n        const was_flushing = self.flush_in_progress.swap(true, .seq_cst);\n        if (was_flushing) {\n            // Another flush is in progress, wait for it\n            const remaining_ms = if (timeout_ms) |ms|\n                ms -| @as(u64, @intCast(std.time.milliTimestamp() - start_time))\n            else\n                null;\n\n            if (remaining_ms == 0) {\n                return .timeout;\n            }\n\n            if (remaining_ms) |ms| {\n                self.flush_complete.timedWait(&self.mutex, ms * std.time.ns_per_ms) catch {\n                    return .timeout;\n                };\n            } else {\n                self.flush_complete.wait(&self.mutex);\n            }\n            return .success;\n        }\n\n        defer {\n            self.flush_in_progress.store(false, .release);\n            self.flush_complete.broadcast();\n        }\n\n        // Wait for any collection in progress\n        while (self.collection_in_progress.load(.acquire)) {\n            const remaining_ms = if (timeout_ms) |ms|\n                ms -| @as(u64, @intCast(std.time.milliTimestamp() - start_time))\n            else\n                null;\n\n            if (remaining_ms == 0) {\n                return .timeout;\n            }\n\n            if (remaining_ms) |ms| {\n                self.collection_complete.timedWait(&self.mutex, ms * std.time.ns_per_ms) catch {\n                    return .timeout;\n                };\n            } else {\n                self.collection_complete.wait(&self.mutex);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Now do immediate collection with atomic flag\n        self.collection_in_progress.store(true, .release);\n        defer {\n            self.collection_in_progress.store(false, .release);\n            self.collection_complete.broadcast();\n        }\n\n        // Perform immediate metric collection\n        var arena = std.heap.ArenaAllocator.init(self.allocator);\n        defer arena.deinit();\n\n        // Collect from reader state instead of iterating meters\n        const collected_metrics = self.reader_state.collect(arena.allocator()) catch {\n            return .failure;\n        };\n\n        // Update last collection time atomically\n        self.last_collection_time.store(std.time.milliTimestamp(), .release);\n\n        // Export if we have metrics\n        if (collected_metrics.len > 0 and self.exporter != null) {\n            // Temporarily release mutex for export\n            self.mutex.unlock();\n            const export_result = self.exporter.?.exportMetrics(collected_metrics);\n            self.mutex.lock();\n\n            if (export_result != .success) {\n                return .failure;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Flush the exporter\n        if (self.exporter) |*exporter| {\n            self.mutex.unlock();\n            const flush_result = exporter.forceFlush(timeout_ms);\n            self.mutex.lock();\n\n            return if (flush_result == .success) .success else .failure;\n        }\n\n        return .success;\n    }\n\n    /// Shutdown the processor\n    pub fn shutdown(self: *PeriodicReader, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_shutdown.swap(true, .seq_cst)) {\n            return .success;\n        }\n\n        self.is_running.store(false, .release);\n        self.condition.signal();\n\n        // Shutdown the exporter\n        const result = if (self.exporter) |*exporter| exporter.shutdown(timeout_ms) else .success;\n        return if (result == .success) .success else .failure;\n    }\n\n    /// Register a meter for periodic collection\n    pub fn registerMeter(self: *PeriodicReader, meter: *sdk.Meter) void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) return;\n\n        self.registered_meters.append(self.allocator, meter) catch {\n            // Handle allocation failure silently for now\n            return;\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Unregister a meter from periodic collection\n    pub fn unregisterMeter(self: *PeriodicReader, meter: *sdk.Meter) void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) return;\n\n        for (self.registered_meters.items, 0..) |registered_meter, i| {\n            if (registered_meter == meter) {\n                _ = self.registered_meters.swapRemove(i);\n                break;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the exporter for this processor\n    pub fn setExporter(self: *PeriodicReader, exporter: ?sdk.MetricExporter) !void {\n        if (self.exporter) |old_exporter| {\n            old_exporter.deinit();\n            old_exporter.destroy();\n        }\n        self.exporter = exporter;\n    }\n\n    /// Get the processor as a MetricProcessor union\n    pub fn reader(self: *PeriodicReader) sdk.Reader {\n        return .{ .bridge = sdk.BridgeReader.init(self) };\n    }\n\n    /// Background thread function that periodically collects metrics\n    fn collectionThreadFn(self: *PeriodicReader) void {\n        while (true) {\n            // Fast path check without mutex\n            if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n                break;\n            }\n\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n            // Double-check under mutex\n            if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n                break;\n            }\n\n            // Calculate wait time in nanoseconds\n            const wait_ns = @as(u64, self.collection_interval_ms) * std.time.ns_per_ms;\n\n            // Wait for the specified interval or until signaled\n            self.condition.timedWait(&self.mutex, wait_ns) catch {\n                // Timeout - normal collection cycle\n            };\n\n            // Skip if flush is in progress\n            if (self.flush_in_progress.load(.acquire)) {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Try to acquire collection lock\n            if (self.collection_in_progress.swap(true, .seq_cst)) {\n                // Already collecting, skip this cycle\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            defer {\n                self.collection_in_progress.store(false, .release);\n                self.collection_complete.broadcast();\n            }\n\n            // Update last collection time\n            self.last_collection_time.store(std.time.milliTimestamp(), .release);\n\n            // Do the collection\n            self.mutex.unlock();\n            self.collect();\n            self.mutex.lock();\n        }\n    }\n};\n\ntest \"BasicPeriodicProcessor - direct init vs pipeline init thread behavior\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n    const MockExporter = @import(\"exporter.zig\").MockMetricExporter;\n    const Resource = @import(\"../resource/resource.zig\").Resource;\n\n    // Create mock exporter\n    const mock_exporter = try allocator.create(MockExporter);\n    mock_exporter.* = MockExporter.init(allocator);\n\n    // Create processor with very short interval for testing (direct init)\n    var processor = PeriodicReader.init(allocator, mock_exporter.metricExporter(), 100); // 100ms\n    defer processor.deinit();\n\n    // Verify thread is not running when created via direct init()\n    try testing.expect(!processor.is_running.load(.acquire));\n    try testing.expect(processor.thread == null);\n\n    // Create a basic meter for testing\n    const scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    const resource = Resource.empty;\n    const basic_meter = try allocator.create(sdk.Meter);\n    basic_meter.* = try sdk.Meter.init(allocator, scope, resource);\n    defer {\n        basic_meter.deinit();\n        allocator.destroy(basic_meter);\n    }\n\n    // Start the thread manually (since we're not using pipeline)\n    try processor.start();\n\n    // Verify thread is now running\n    try testing.expect(processor.is_running.load(.acquire));\n    try testing.expect(processor.thread != null);\n\n    // Register meter\n    processor.registerMeter(basic_meter);\n\n    // Create a second meter\n    const scope2 = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter2\", \"1.0.0\");\n    const basic_meter2 = try allocator.create(sdk.Meter);\n    basic_meter2.* = try sdk.Meter.init(allocator, scope2, resource);\n    defer {\n        basic_meter2.deinit();\n        allocator.destroy(basic_meter2);\n    }\n\n    // Register second meter\n    processor.registerMeter(basic_meter2);\n\n    // Verify thread is still running and we have both meters\n    try testing.expect(processor.is_running.load(.acquire));\n    try testing.expect(processor.thread != null);\n    try testing.expect(processor.registered_meters.items.len == 2);\n\n    // Wait a bit to allow some collections to happen\n    std.time.sleep(250 * std.time.ns_per_ms);\n\n    // Verify some exports happened (thread is working)\n    const export_count = mock_exporter.exportCount();\n    try testing.expect(export_count > 0);\n\n    // Test shutdown - should stop the thread\n    _ = processor.shutdown(1000);\n    try testing.expect(processor.is_shutdown.load(.acquire));\n\n    // Give thread time to exit\n    std.time.sleep(50 * std.time.ns_per_ms);\n}\n\ntest \"BasicPeriodicProcessor - no thread start without meters\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    // Create processor\n    var processor = PeriodicReader.init(allocator, null, 100);\n    defer processor.deinit();\n\n    // Verify thread is not running\n    try testing.expect(!processor.is_running.load(.acquire));\n    try testing.expect(processor.thread == null);\n\n    // Call forceFlush - should not crash\n    const result = processor.forceFlush(100);\n    try testing.expect(result == .success);\n\n    // Thread should still not be running\n    try testing.expect(!processor.is_running.load(.acquire));\n    try testing.expect(processor.thread == null);\n\n    // Shutdown should work fine\n    _ = processor.shutdown(100);\n    try testing.expect(processor.is_shutdown.load(.acquire));\n}\n",

It manages meter lifecycle, caching, and configuration.\n//!\n//! See: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/metrics/sdk.md\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const Meter = @import(\"meter.zig\").Meter;\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const MetricValue = @import(\"reader.zig\").MetricValue;\n};\n\n/// Standard Counter implementation that forwards measurements to readers\npub fn StandardCounter(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        metadata_hash: u64,\n\n        pub fn init(\n            name: []const u8,\n            description: ?[]const u8,\n            unit: ?[]const u8,\n            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        ) !@This() {\n            const metadata_hash = sdk.MetricMetadata.computeHash(\n                name,\n                unit orelse \"\",\n                .Counter,\n                parent_meter.scope.name,\n                parent_meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                parent_meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n            );\n\n            return .{\n                .name = name,\n                .description = description,\n                .unit = unit,\n                .meter = parent_meter,\n                .metadata_hash = metadata_hash,\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            _ = self;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n            return self.name;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateCounterValue(i64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Counter.add\", \"Negative value provided\", \"counter values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == i64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, .{ .i64 = value }, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn addF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateCounterValue(f64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Counter.add\", \"Negative value provided\", \"counter values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == f64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, .{ .f64 = value }, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordI64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: i64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordF64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: f64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Standard UpDownCounter implementation that forwards measurements to readers (allowing negative)\npub fn StandardUpDownCounter(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ctx;\n\n            if (T == i64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, .{ .i64 = value }, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n Unused in Phase 1\n\n            const metadata_hash = sdk.MetricMetadata.computeHash(\n                name,\n                unit orelse \"\",\n                .Histogram,\n                parent_meter.scope.name,\n                parent_meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                parent_meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n            );\n\n            return .{\n                .name = name,\n                .description = description,\n                .unit = unit,\n                .meter = parent_meter,\n                .metadata_hash = metadata_hash,\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            _ = self;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n            return self.name;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addI64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: i64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addF64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: f64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateHistogramValue(i64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Histogram.record\", \"Negative value provided\", \"histogram values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == i64) {\n                const metadata = (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, .{ .f64 = value }, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n   "old_text": "//! OpenTelemetry Basic Meter Provider SDK Implementation\n//!\n//! This module provides the basic concrete implementation of MeterProvider for the SDK.\n//! It manages meter lifecycle, caching, and configuration.\n//!\n//! See: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/metrics/sdk.md\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const Meter = @import(\"meter.zig\").Meter;\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n};\n\n/// Standard Counter implementation that forwards measurements to readers\npub fn StandardCounter(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        metadata_hash: u64,\n\n        pub fn init(\n            name: []const u8,\n            description: ?[]const u8,\n            unit: ?[]const u8,\n            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        ) !@This() {\n            const metadata_hash = sdk.MetricMetadata.computeHash(\n                name,\n                unit orelse \"\",\n                .Counter,\n                parent_meter.scope.name,\n                parent_meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                parent_meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n            );\n\n            return .{\n                .name = name,\n                .description = description,\n                .unit = unit,\n                .meter = parent_meter,\n                .metadata_hash = metadata_hash,\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            _ = self;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n            return self.name;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateCounterValue(i64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Counter.add\", \"Negative value provided\", \"counter values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == i64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn addF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateCounterValue(f64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Counter.add\", \"Negative value provided\", \"counter values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == f64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, value, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordI64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: i64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordF64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: f64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn enabled(self: StandardUpDownCounter(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        meter: *sdk.Meter,\n    const Meter = @import(\"meter.zig\").Meter;\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const MetricValue = @import(\"reader.zig\").MetricValue;\n    const aggregations = @import(\"aggregations.zig\");\n};\n\n/// Standard Counter implementation that forwards measurements to readers\npub fn StandardCounter(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n Counter implementation that forwards measurements to readers\npub fn StandardCounter(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n metadata_hash,\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            _ = self;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n (!api.metrics.validateCounterValue(i64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Counter.add\", \"Negative value provided\", \"counter values must be non-negative\");\n
