  "new_text": "//! Basic Periodic Metrics Processor\n//!\n//! This module provides a basic metrics processor that collects metrics from registered\n//! meters on a periodic basis using a background thread. It uses POSIX threads\n//! for cross-platform compatibility.\n//!\n//! The processor maintains a thread that wakes up at regular intervals to collect\n//! metrics from all registered meters and export them via the configured exporter.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\nconst c = std.c;\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const BridgeReader = @import(\"reader.zig\").BridgeReader;\n    const Meter = @import(\"meter.zig\").Meter;\n    const MetricData = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricData;\n    const Reader = @import(\"reader.zig\").Reader;\n    const MetricExporter = @import(\"exporter.zig\").MetricExporter;\n    const ReaderAggregationState = @import(\"reader_aggregation_state.zig\").ReaderAggregationState;\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const MetricValue = @import(\"reader.zig\").MetricValue;\n};\n\n/// Basic periodic metrics processor that collects metrics at regular intervals\npub const PeriodicReader = struct {\n    pub const PipelineStep = @import(\"../common/pipeline.zig\").PipelineStepInstructions(\n        PeriodicReader,\n        sdk.Reader,\n        ?u32,\n        reader,\n        _initFn,\n        setExporter,\n    );\n    pub fn _initFn(self: *PeriodicReader, interval: ?u32, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) !void {\n        self.* = init(allocator, null, interval);\n        try self.start();\n    }\n\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    exporter: ?sdk.MetricExporter,\n    mutex: std.Thread.Mutex,\n    condition: std.Thread.Condition,\n    is_shutdown: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    is_running: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    flush_in_progress: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    collection_in_progress: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    flush_complete: std.Thread.Condition,\n    collection_complete: std.Thread.Condition,\n    last_collection_time: std.atomic.Value(i64),\n    thread: ?std.Thread,\n    collection_interval_ms: u32,\n    registered_meters: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.Meter),\n    reader_state: sdk.ReaderAggregationState,\n\n    /// Initialize a new basic periodic metrics processor\n    /// collection_interval_ms: How often to collect metrics (default: 60000ms = 60s)\n    pub fn init(\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        exporter: ?sdk.MetricExporter,\n        collection_interval_ms: ?u32,\n    ) PeriodicReader {\n        return .{\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .exporter = exporter,\n            .mutex = .{},\n            .condition = .{},\n            .is_shutdown = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n            .is_running = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n            .flush_in_progress = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n            .collection_in_progress = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n            .flush_complete = .{},\n            .collection_complete = .{},\n            .last_collection_time = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n            .thread = null,\n            .collection_interval_ms = collection_interval_ms orelse 60000, // 60 seconds default\n            .registered_meters = .{},\n            .reader_state = sdk.ReaderAggregationState.init(\n                allocator,\n                .Delta, // Default to Delta temporality for now\n                @import(\"reader_aggregation_state.zig\").defaultAggregationSelector,\n            ),\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Start the background collection thread\n    pub fn start(self: *PeriodicReader) !void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_running.load(.acquire) or self.thread != null) {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        self.is_running.store(true, .release);\n        self.thread = try std.Thread.spawn(.{}, collectionThreadFn, .{self});\n    }\n\n    /// Stop the background collection thread and clean up resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *PeriodicReader) void {\n        // Signal shutdown\n        self.is_shutdown.store(true, .release);\n        self.is_running.store(false, .release);\n\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        self.condition.signal();\n        self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Wait for thread to finish\n        if (self.thread) |thread| {\n            thread.join();\n        }\n\n        // Clean up resources\n        self.reader_state.deinit();\n        self.registered_meters.deinit(self.allocator);\n        if (self.exporter) |exporter| {\n            exporter.deinit();\n            exporter.destroy();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Destroy the processor and free its memory\n    pub fn destroy(self: *PeriodicReader) void {\n        self.allocator.destroy(self);\n    }\n\n    pub fn recordMeasurement(\n        self: *PeriodicReader,\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        value: sdk.MetricValue,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n    ) void {\n        self.reader_state.recordMeasurement(instrument, value, attributes, metadata);\n    }\n\n    /// Collect metrics from all registered meters (called by background thread)\n    pub fn collect(self: *PeriodicReader) void {\n        var arena = std.heap.ArenaAllocator.init(self.allocator);\n        defer arena.deinit();\n        const arena_allocator = arena.allocator();\n\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) return;\n\n        // Collect from reader state instead of iterating meters\n        const collected_metrics = self.reader_state.collect(arena_allocator) catch {\n            // Log error if needed\n            return;\n        };\n\n        // Export all collected metrics. Exporter must copy memory\n        // that it needs beyond the duration of this call.\n        if (self.exporter) |*exporter| _ = exporter.exportMetrics(collected_metrics);\n        // Arena cleans up all the memory.\n    }\n\n    /// Force flush the exporter\n    pub fn forceFlush(self: *PeriodicReader, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult {\n        // Quick check without mutex\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return .failure;\n        }\n\n        const start_time = std.time.milliTimestamp();\n\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Try to set flush_in_progress atomically\n        const was_flushing = self.flush_in_progress.swap(true, .seq_cst);\n        if (was_flushing) {\n            // Another flush is in progress, wait for it\n            const remaining_ms = if (timeout_ms) |ms|\n                ms -| @as(u64, @intCast(std.time.milliTimestamp() - start_time))\n            else\n                null;\n\n            if (remaining_ms == 0) {\n                return .timeout;\n            }\n\n            if (remaining_ms) |ms| {\n                self.flush_complete.timedWait(&self.mutex, ms * std.time.ns_per_ms) catch {\n                    return .timeout;\n                };\n            } else {\n                self.flush_complete.wait(&self.mutex);\n            }\n            return .success;\n        }\n\n        defer {\n            self.flush_in_progress.store(false, .release);\n            self.flush_complete.broadcast();\n        }\n\n        // Wait for any collection in progress\n        while (self.collection_in_progress.load(.acquire)) {\n            const remaining_ms = if (timeout_ms) |ms|\n                ms -| @as(u64, @intCast(std.time.milliTimestamp() - start_time))\n            else\n                null;\n\n            if (remaining_ms == 0) {\n                return .timeout;\n            }\n\n            if (remaining_ms) |ms| {\n                self.collection_complete.timedWait(&self.mutex, ms * std.time.ns_per_ms) catch {\n                    return .timeout;\n                };\n            } else {\n                self.collection_complete.wait(&self.mutex);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Now do immediate collection with atomic flag\n        self.collection_in_progress.store(true, .release);\n        defer {\n            self.collection_in_progress.store(false, .release);\n            self.collection_complete.broadcast();\n        }\n\n        // Perform immediate metric collection\n        var arena = std.heap.ArenaAllocator.init(self.allocator);\n        defer arena.deinit();\n\n        // Collect from reader state instead of iterating meters\n        const collected_metrics = self.reader_state.collect(arena.allocator()) catch {\n            return .failure;\n        };\n\n        // Update last collection time atomically\n        self.last_collection_time.store(std.time.milliTimestamp(), .release);\n\n        // Export if we have metrics\n        if (collected_metrics.len > 0 and self.exporter != null) {\n            // Temporarily release mutex for export\n            self.mutex.unlock();\n            const export_result = self.exporter.?.exportMetrics(collected_metrics);\n            self.mutex.lock();\n\n            if (export_result != .success) {\n                return .failure;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Flush the exporter\n        if (self.exporter) |*exporter| {\n            self.mutex.unlock();\n            const flush_result = exporter.forceFlush(timeout_ms);\n            self.mutex.lock();\n\n            return if (flush_result == .success) .success else .failure;\n        }\n\n        return .success;\n    }\n\n    /// Shutdown the processor\n    pub fn shutdown(self: *PeriodicReader, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_shutdown.swap(true, .seq_cst)) {\n            return .success;\n        }\n\n        self.is_running.store(false, .release);\n        self.condition.signal();\n\n        // Shutdown the exporter\n        const result = if (self.exporter) |*exporter| exporter.shutdown(timeout_ms) else .success;\n        return if (result == .success) .success else .failure;\n    }\n\n    /// Register a meter for periodic collection\n    pub fn registerMeter(self: *PeriodicReader, meter: *sdk.Meter) void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) return;\n\n        self.registered_meters.append(self.allocator, meter) catch {\n            // Handle allocation failure silently for now\n            return;\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Unregister a meter from periodic collection\n    pub fn unregisterMeter(self: *PeriodicReader, meter: *sdk.Meter) void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) return;\n\n        for (self.registered_meters.items, 0..) |registered_meter, i| {\n            if (registered_meter == meter) {\n                _ = self.registered_meters.swapRemove(i);\n                break;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the exporter for this processor\n    pub fn setExporter(self: *PeriodicReader, exporter: ?sdk.MetricExporter) !void {\n        if (self.exporter) |old_exporter| {\n            old_exporter.deinit();\n            old_exporter.destroy();\n        }\n        self.exporter = exporter;\n    }\n\n    /// Get the processor as a MetricProcessor union\n    pub fn reader(self: *PeriodicReader) sdk.Reader {\n        return .{ .bridge = sdk.BridgeReader.init(self) };\n    }\n\n    /// Background thread function that periodically collects metrics\n    fn collectionThreadFn(self: *PeriodicReader) void {\n        while (true) {\n            // Fast path check without mutex\n            if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n                break;\n            }\n\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n            // Double-check under mutex\n            if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n                break;\n            }\n\n            // Calculate wait time in nanoseconds\n            const wait_ns = @as(u64, self.collection_interval_ms) * std.time.ns_per_ms;\n\n            // Wait for the specified interval or until signaled\n            self.condition.timedWait(&self.mutex, wait_ns) catch {\n                // Timeout - normal collection cycle\n            };\n\n            // Skip if flush is in progress\n            if (self.flush_in_progress.load(.acquire)) {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Try to acquire collection lock\n            if (self.collection_in_progress.swap(true, .seq_cst)) {\n                // Already collecting, skip this cycle\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            defer {\n                self.collection_in_progress.store(false, .release);\n                self.collection_complete.broadcast();\n            }\n\n            // Update last collection time\n            self.last_collection_time.store(std.time.milliTimestamp(), .release);\n\n            // Do the collection\n            self.mutex.unlock();\n            self.collect();\n            self.mutex.lock();\n        }\n    }\n};\n\ntest \"BasicPeriodicProcessor - direct init vs pipeline init thread behavior\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n    const MockExporter = @import(\"exporter.zig\").MockMetricExporter;\n    const Resource = @import(\"../resource/resource.zig\").Resource;\n\n    // Create mock exporter\n    const mock_exporter = try allocator.create(MockExporter);\n    mock_exporter.* = MockExporter.init(allocator);\n\n    // Create processor with very short interval for testing (direct init)\n    var processor = PeriodicReader.init(allocator, mock_exporter.metricExporter(), 100); // 100ms\n    defer processor.deinit();\n\n    // Verify thread is not running when created via direct init()\n    try testing.expect(!processor.is_running.load(.acquire));\n    try testing.expect(processor.thread == null);\n\n    // Create a basic meter for testing\n    const scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    const resource = Resource.empty;\n    const basic_meter = try allocator.create(sdk.Meter);\n    basic_meter.* = try sdk.Meter.init(allocator, scope, resource, null);\n    defer {\n        basic_meter.deinit();\n        allocator.destroy(basic_meter);\n    }\n\n    // Start the thread manually (since we're not using pipeline)\n    try processor.start();\n\n    // Verify thread is now running\n    try testing.expect(processor.is_running.load(.acquire));\n    try testing.expect(processor.thread != null);\n\n    // Register meter\n    processor.registerMeter(basic_meter);\n\n    // Create a second meter\n    const scope2 = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter2\", \"1.0.0\");\n    const basic_meter2 = try allocator.create(sdk.Meter);\n    basic_meter2.* = try sdk.Meter.init(allocator, scope2, resource, null);\n    defer {\n        basic_meter2.deinit();\n        allocator.destroy(basic_meter2);\n    }\n\n    // Register second meter\n    processor.registerMeter(basic_meter2);\n\n    // Verify thread is still running and we have both meters\n    try testing.expect(processor.is_running.load(.acquire));\n    try testing.expect(processor.thread != null);\n    try testing.expect(processor.registered_meters.items.len == 2);\n\n    // Wait a bit to allow some collections to happen\n    std.time.sleep(250 * std.time.ns_per_ms);\n\n    // Verify some exports happened (thread is working)\n    const export_count = mock_exporter.exportCount();\n    try testing.expect(export_count > 0);\n\n    // Test shutdown - should stop the thread\n    _ = processor.shutdown(1000);\n    try testing.expect(processor.is_shutdown.load(.acquire));\n\n    // Give thread time to exit\n    std.time.sleep(50 * std.time.ns_per_ms);\n}\n\ntest \"BasicPeriodicProcessor - no thread start without meters\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    // Create processor\n    var processor = PeriodicReader.init(allocator, null, 100);\n    defer processor.deinit();\n\n    // Verify thread is not running\n    try testing.expect(!processor.is_running.load(.acquire));\n    try testing.expect(processor.thread == null);\n\n    // Call forceFlush - should not crash\n    const result = processor.forceFlush(100);\n    try testing.expect(result == .success);\n\n    // Thread should still not be running\n    try testing.expect(!processor.is_running.load(.acquire));\n    try testing.expect(processor.thread == null);\n\n    // Shutdown should work fine\n    _ = processor.shutdown(100);\n    try testing.expect(processor.is_shutdown.load(.acquire));\n}\n",
  "old_text": "//! Basic Periodic Metrics Processor\n//!\n//! This module provides a basic metrics processor that collects metrics from registered\n//! meters on a periodic basis using a background thread. It uses POSIX threads\n//! for cross-platform compatibility.\n//!\n//! The processor maintains a thread that wakes up at regular intervals to collect\n//! metrics from all registered meters and export them via the configured exporter.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\nconst c = std.c;\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const BridgeReader = @import(\"reader.zig\").BridgeReader;\n    const Meter = @import(\"meter.zig\").Meter;\n    const MetricData = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricData;\n    const Reader = @import(\"reader.zig\").Reader;\n    const MetricExporter = @import(\"exporter.zig\").MetricExporter;\n    const ReaderAggregationState = @import(\"reader_aggregation_state.zig\").ReaderAggregationState;\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const MetricValue = @import(\"reader.zig\").MetricValue;\n};\n\n/// Basic periodic metrics processor that collects metrics at regular intervals\npub const PeriodicReader = struct {\n    pub const PipelineStep = @import(\"../common/pipeline.zig\").PipelineStepInstructions(\n        PeriodicReader,\n        sdk.Reader,\n        ?u32,\n        reader,\n        _initFn,\n        setExporter,\n    );\n    pub fn _initFn(self: *PeriodicReader, interval: ?u32, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) !void {\n        self.* = init(allocator, null, interval);\n        try self.start();\n    }\n\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    exporter: ?sdk.MetricExporter,\n    mutex: std.Thread.Mutex,\n    condition: std.Thread.Condition,\n    is_shutdown: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    is_running: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    flush_in_progress: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    collection_in_progress: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    flush_complete: std.Thread.Condition,\n    collection_complete: std.Thread.Condition,\n    last_collection_time: std.atomic.Value(i64),\n    thread: ?std.Thread,\n    collection_interval_ms: u32,\n    registered_meters: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.Meter),\n    reader_state: sdk.ReaderAggregationState,\n\n    /// Initialize a new basic periodic metrics processor\n    /// collection_interval_ms: How often to collect metrics (default: 60000ms = 60s)\n    pub fn init(\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        exporter: ?sdk.MetricExporter,\n        collection_interval_ms: ?u32,\n    ) PeriodicReader {\n        return .{\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .exporter = exporter,\n            .mutex = .{},\n            .condition = .{},\n            .is_shutdown = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n            .is_running = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n            .flush_in_progress = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n            .collection_in_progress = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n            .flush_complete = .{},\n            .collection_complete = .{},\n            .last_collection_time = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n            .thread = null,\n            .collection_interval_ms = collection_interval_ms orelse 60000, // 60 seconds default\n            .registered_meters = .{},\n            .reader_state = sdk.ReaderAggregationState.init(\n                allocator,\n                .Delta, // Default to Delta temporality for now\n                @import(\"reader_aggregation_state.zig\").defaultAggregationSelector,\n            ),\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Start the background collection thread\n    pub fn start(self: *PeriodicReader) !void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_running.load(.acquire) or self.thread != null) {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        self.is_running.store(true, .release);\n        self.thread = try std.Thread.spawn(.{}, collectionThreadFn, .{self});\n    }\n\n    /// Stop the background collection thread and clean up resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *PeriodicReader) void {\n        // Signal shutdown\n        self.is_shutdown.store(true, .release);\n        self.is_running.store(false, .release);\n\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        self.condition.signal();\n        self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Wait for thread to finish\n        if (self.thread) |thread| {\n            thread.join();\n        }\n\n        // Clean up resources\n        self.reader_state.deinit();\n        self.registered_meters.deinit(self.allocator);\n        if (self.exporter) |exporter| {\n            exporter.deinit();\n            exporter.destroy();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Destroy the processor and free its memory\n    pub fn destroy(self: *PeriodicReader) void {\n        self.allocator.destroy(self);\n    }\n\n    pub fn recordMeasurement(\n        self: *PeriodicReader,\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        value: sdk.MetricValue,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n    ) void {\n        self.reader_state.recordMeasurement(instrument, value, attributes, metadata);\n    }\n\n    /// Collect metrics from all registered meters (called by background thread)\n    pub fn collect(self: *PeriodicReader) void {\n        var arena = std.heap.ArenaAllocator.init(self.allocator);\n        defer arena.deinit();\n        const arena_allocator = arena.allocator();\n\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) return;\n\n        // Collect from reader state instead of iterating meters\n        const collected_metrics = self.reader_state.collect(arena_allocator) catch {\n            // Log error if needed\n            return;\n        };\n\n        // Export all collected metrics. Exporter must copy memory\n        // that it needs beyond the duration of this call.\n        if (self.exporter) |*exporter| _ = exporter.exportMetrics(collected_metrics);\n        // Arena cleans up all the memory.\n    }\n\n    /// Force flush the exporter\n    pub fn forceFlush(self: *PeriodicReader, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult {\n        // Quick check without mutex\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return .failure;\n        }\n\n        const start_time = std.time.milliTimestamp();\n\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Try to set flush_in_progress atomically\n        const was_flushing = self.flush_in_progress.swap(true, .seq_cst);\n        if (was_flushing) {\n            // Another flush is in progress, wait for it\n            const remaining_ms = if (timeout_ms) |ms|\n                ms -| @as(u64, @intCast(std.time.milliTimestamp() - start_time))\n            else\n                null;\n\n            if (remaining_ms == 0) {\n                return .timeout;\n            }\n\n            if (remaining_ms) |ms| {\n                self.flush_complete.timedWait(&self.mutex, ms * std.time.ns_per_ms) catch {\n                    return .timeout;\n                };\n            } else {\n                self.flush_complete.wait(&self.mutex);\n            }\n            return .success;\n        }\n\n        defer {\n            self.flush_in_progress.store(false, .release);\n            self.flush_complete.broadcast();\n        }\n\n        // Wait for any collection in progress\n        while (self.collection_in_progress.load(.acquire)) {\n            const remaining_ms = if (timeout_ms) |ms|\n                ms -| @as(u64, @intCast(std.time.milliTimestamp() - start_time))\n            else\n                null;\n\n            if (remaining_ms == 0) {\n                return .timeout;\n            }\n\n            if (remaining_ms) |ms| {\n                self.collection_complete.timedWait(&self.mutex, ms * std.time.ns_per_ms) catch {\n                    return .timeout;\n                };\n            } else {\n                self.collection_complete.wait(&self.mutex);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Now do immediate collection with atomic flag\n        self.collection_in_progress.store(true, .release);\n        defer {\n            self.collection_in_progress.store(false, .release);\n            self.collection_complete.broadcast();\n        }\n\n        // Perform immediate metric collection\n        var arena = std.heap.ArenaAllocator.init(self.allocator);\n        defer arena.deinit();\n\n        // Collect from reader state instead of iterating meters\n        const collected_metrics = self.reader_state.collect(arena.allocator()) catch {\n            return .failure;\n        };\n\n        // Update last collection time atomically\n        self.last_collection_time.store(std.time.milliTimestamp(), .release);\n\n        // Export if we have metrics\n        if (collected_metrics.len > 0 and self.exporter != null) {\n            // Temporarily release mutex for export\n            self.mutex.unlock();\n            const export_result = self.exporter.?.exportMetrics(collected_metrics);\n            self.mutex.lock();\n\n            if (export_result != .success) {\n                return .failure;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Flush the exporter\n        if (self.exporter) |*exporter| {\n            self.mutex.unlock();\n            const flush_result = exporter.forceFlush(timeout_ms);\n            self.mutex.lock();\n\n            return if (flush_result == .success) .success else .failure;\n        }\n\n        return .success;\n    }\n\n    /// Shutdown the processor\n    pub fn shutdown(self: *PeriodicReader, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_shutdown.swap(true, .seq_cst)) {\n            return .success;\n        }\n\n        self.is_running.store(false, .release);\n        self.condition.signal();\n\n        // Shutdown the exporter\n        const result = if (self.exporter) |*exporter| exporter.shutdown(timeout_ms) else .success;\n        return if (result == .success) .success else .failure;\n    }\n\n    /// Register a meter for periodic collection\n    pub fn registerMeter(self: *PeriodicReader, meter: *sdk.Meter) void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) return;\n\n        self.registered_meters.append(self.allocator, meter) catch {\n            // Handle allocation failure silently for now\n            return;\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Unregister a meter from periodic collection\n    pub fn unregisterMeter(self: *PeriodicReader, meter: *sdk.Meter) void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) return;\n\n        for (self.registered_meters.items, 0..) |registered_meter, i| {\n            if (registered_meter == meter) {\n                _ = self.registered_meters.swapRemove(i);\n                break;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the exporter for this processor\n    pub fn setExporter(self: *PeriodicReader, exporter: ?sdk.MetricExporter) !void {\n        if (self.exporter) |old_exporter| {\n            old_exporter.deinit();\n            old_exporter.destroy();\n        }\n        self.exporter = exporter;\n    }\n\n    /// Get the processor as a MetricProcessor union\n    pub fn reader(self: *PeriodicReader) sdk.Reader {\n        return .{ .bridge = sdk.BridgeReader.init(self) };\n    }\n\n    /// Background thread function that periodically collects metrics\n    fn collectionThreadFn(self: *PeriodicReader) void {\n        while (true) {\n            // Fast path check without mutex\n            if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n                break;\n            }\n\n            self.mutex.lock();\n in nanoseconds\n            const wait_ns = @as(u64, self.collection_interval_ms) * std.time.ns_per_ms;\n\n            // Wait for the specified interval or until signaled\n            self.condition.timedWait(&self.mutex, wait_ns) catch {\n                // Timeout - normal collection cycle\n            };\n\n            // Skip if flush is in progress\n            if (self.flush_in_progress.load(.acquire)) {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Try to acquire collection lock\n            if (self.collection_in_progress.swap(true, .seq_cst)) {\n                // Already collecting, skip this cycle\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            defer {\n                self.collection_in_progress.store(false, .release);\n                self.collection_complete.broadcast();\n            }\n\n            // Update last collection time\n            self.last_collection_time.store(std.time.milliTimestamp(), .release);\n\n            // Do the collection\n            self.mutex.unlock();\n            self.collect();\n            self.mutex.lock();\n        }\n    }\n};\n\ntest \"BasicPeriodicProcessor - direct init vs pipeline init thread behavior\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n    const MockExporter = @import(\"exporter.zig\").MockMetricExporter;\n    const Resource = @import(\"../resource/resource.zig\").Resource;\n\n    // Create mock exporter\n    const mock_exporter = try allocator.create(MockExporter);\n    mock_exporter.* = MockExporter.init(allocator);\n\n processor.deinit();\n\n    // Verify thread is not running when created via direct init()\n    try testing.expect(!processor.is_running.load(.acquire));\n    try testing.expect(processor.thread == null);\n\n    // Create a basic meter for testing\n    const scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    const resource = Resource.empty;\n    const basic_meter = try allocator.create(sdk.Meter);\n    basic_meter.* = try sdk.Meter.init(allocator, scope, resource);\n    defer {\n        basic_meter.deinit();\n        allocator.destroy(basic_meter);\n    }\n\n testing.expect(processor.thread != null);\n\n    // Register meter\n    processor.registerMeter(basic_meter);\n\n    // Create a second meter\n    const scope2 = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter2\", \"1.0.0\");\n    const basic_meter2 = try allocator.create(sdk.Meter);\n    basic_meter2.* = try sdk.Meter.init(allocator, scope2, resource);\n Phase 1: Move Aggregations to Readers\n2. Phase 1b: Eliminate Instrument-to-Aggregation Map  \n3. Phase 1c: Lock-Free Implementation\n4. Phase 2: View Support (Foundation)\n\n**Timeline Estimate:** 16-20 days total\n\n---\n\n## Phase 1: Move Aggregations to Readers (3-4 days)\n\n### Goals\n- Move aggregations from instruments to readers (each reader owns instrument → aggregation map)\n- Support multiple readers with independent aggregation states\n- Invert control flow from pull-based to push-based model\n- Maintain current functionality with new architecture\n- Ensure all existing tests pass without modification\n\n### Implementation Tasks\n\n#### 1. (1-2 days, parallel with phases)\n\n### Directory Structure Setup\n- [ ] Create `benchmarks/metrics/` directory\n- [ ] Add benchmark targets to root `build.zig`\n- [ ] Create benchmark documentation ownership change)\n- Only aggregation ownership moves from instruments to readers\n- Each reader owns its aggregation state completely\n- Use pre-allocated pools where possible to avoid runtime allocation\n\n### Error Handling\n- Use `api.common.handleError()` throughout\n- `ErrorType.resource_exhausted` for cardinality limits\n- `ErrorType.validation` for view/instrument compatibility errors\n\n### Thread Safety\n- Phase 1: Mutex per reader aggregation map\n- Phase 1c: Lock-free aggregations, minimal locking for map access\n- Always ensure measurements are not lost or Update Aggregation Types (Prepare for Phase 1b)\n**Files:** `src/sdk/metrics/aggregations.zig`\n- [ ] Keep existing aggregation types but ensure they're compatible with dynamic allocation\n- [ ] Add metadata fields to aggregation types:\n  - `instrument_name: []const u8`\n  - `instrument_type: InstrumentType` \n  - `instrument_unit: []const u8`\n\n### Success Criteria\n- [ ] `zig build test-sdk` passes\n- [ ] All existing examples work unchanged\n- [ ] Multiple readers work independently \n- [ ] Measurements flow: Instrument → Provider's Readers → Reader's Aggregations\n- [ ] No more iteration over instruments during collection\n- [ ] Architecture ready for Phase 1b\n\n### Deferred Items\n- Lock-free operations (Phase 1c)\n- View support (Phase 2)\n- Attribute-based aggregation indexing (Phase 1b)\n- Optimized cardinality management (Phase 1b)\n\n---\n\n## Phase 1b: Eliminate Instrument-to-Aggregation Map (2 days)\n\n### Goals  \n- Remove the instrument → aggregation map\n- Reader directly owns aggregations indexed by attribute combinations\n- Prepare for lock-free by reducing lock points\n- Maintain all Phase 1 functionality\n\n### Implementation Tasks\n\n#### 1. Create AttributeAggregationMap\n**Files:** `src/sdk/metrics/attribute_aggregation_map.zig` (new file)\n- [ ] Create `AttributeAggregationMap` struct with:\n  - `aggregations: std.AutoHashMap(u128, *Aggregation)`\n  - `aggregation_pool: [MAX_CARDINALITY]Aggregation`\n  - `next_free: usize = 0`\n  - `cardinality: usize = 0`\n  - `MAX_CARDINALITY: usize = 2000`\n  - `overflow_aggregation: ?*Aggregation = null`\n- [ ] Implement `getOrCreateAggregation()` method with cardinality enforcement\n- [ ] Implement `createAggregationForType()` helper\n- [ ] Add overflow handling with `{\"otel.metric.overflow\": true}` attribute\n- [ ] Integrate with error handler for `ErrorType.resource_exhausted`\n\n#### 2. Implement Attribute Hash Function\n**Files:** `src/sdk/metrics/attribute_hash.zig` (new file)\n- [ ] Implement `computeAttributeHash()` function\n- [ ] Ensure hash is commutative (order-independent)\n- [ ] Handle all AttributeValue types (string, int, float, bool, arrays)\n- [ ] Use any reasonable hash algorithm (FNV, xxhash, etc.)\n\n#### 3. Update ReaderAggregationState\n**Files:** `src/sdk/metrics/reader_aggregation_state.zig`\n- [ ] Replace `std.AutoHashMap(*anyopaque, *Aggregation)` with `AttributeAggregationMap`\n- [ ] Update `recordMeasurement()` to:\n  1. Compute attribute hash\n  2. Combine with metadata hash: `combined_hash = metadata.metadata_hash ^ attr_hash`\n  3. Get or create aggregation from AttributeAggregationMap\n  4. Record measurement on aggregation\n- [ ] Pre-allocate aggregation pool at reader creation\n- [ ] Remove dynamic allocation during measurement recording\n\n### Success Criteria\n- [ ] All Phase 1 tests still pass\n- [ ] Reduced lock contention points (single lock per reader instead of per instrument)\n- [ ] Cardinality limit enforced at 2000 per reader\n- [ ] Overflow handling working with error reporting\n- [ ] Ready for lock-free conversion\n\n---\n\n## Phase 1c: Lock-Free Implementation (2-3 days)\n\n### Goals\n- Replace mutex-based aggregation with lock-free operations\n- Remove all mutexes from aggregation types  \n- Maintain all functionality from Phase 1b\n- Improve performance for high-concurrency scenarios\n- Support all aggregation types (sum, histogram, last-value)\n\n### Implementation Tasks\n\n#### 1. Convert Aggregation Types to Lock-Free\n**Files:** `src/sdk/metrics/aggregations.zig`\n- [ ] Update `SumAggregation(T)`:\n  - Replace `value: T` with `value: std.atomic.Value(T)`\n  - Replace `add()` with atomic `fetchAdd()`\n  - Remove mutex field\n- [ ] Update `LastValueAggregation(T)`:\n  - Replace `value: ?T` with `value: std.atomic.Value(?T)`\n  - Replace `record()` with atomic swap\n  - Remove mutex field\n- [ ] Update `HistogramAggregation(T)`:\n  - Replace fields with atomic versions:\n    - `sum: std.atomic.Value(f64)`\n    - `count: std.atomic.Value(u64)`\n    - `min: std.atomic.Value(f64)`\n    - `max: std.atomic.Value(f64)`\n    - `bucket_counts: [MAX_BUCKETS]std.atomic.Value(u64)`\n  - Implement CAS loops for min/max updates\n  - Use atomic operations for sum/count/bucket updates\n  - Remove mutex field\n\n#### 2. Update ReaderAggregationState for Lock-Free Access\n**Files:** `src/sdk/metrics/reader_aggregation_state.zig`\n- [ ] Remove mutex from aggregation access (keep for map modifications if needed)\n- [ ] Ensure `recordMeasurement()` uses only atomic operations for aggregation updates\n- [ ] Consider lock-free map or accept brief locking for map access\n\n#### 3. Performance Validation\n- [ ] Create benchmark comparing Phase 1b (mutex) vs Phase 1c (lock-free)\n- [ ] Verify no race conditions or data loss\n- [ ] Test high concurrency scenarios\n\n### Success Criteria\n- [ ] All Phase 1b tests still pass\n- [ ] Measurable performance improvement (target: 2x for high contention)\n- [ ] No race conditions or data loss\n- [ ] All aggregation types working lock-free\n\n### Deferred Items\n- Lock-free hashmap implementation (accept brief locking for map access)\n\n---\n\n## Phase 2: View Support (Foundation) (4-5 days)\n\n### Goals\n- Support views that can generate multiple data points\n- Enable attribute filtering/transformation  \n- Views immutable after meter creation\n- Handle incompatible aggregation types with warnings\n\n### Implementation Tasks\n\n#### 1. Create View System Types\n**Files:** `src/sdk/metrics/view.zig` (new file)\n- [ ] Create `View` struct with:\n  - `instrument_selector: InstrumentSelector`\n  - `name: ?[]const u8`\n  - `description: ?[]const u8`\n  - `attribute_allowed_keys: ?[]const []const u8`\n  - `aggregation_override: ?AggregationType`\n- [ ] Create `InstrumentSelector` struct with matching logic\n- [ ] Create `ViewApplication` struct for applying views\n- [ ] Add `View.default` constant for instruments with no matching views\n\n#### 2. Add AggregationType Enum\n**Files:** `src/sdk/metrics/aggregations.zig`\n- [ ] Create `AggregationType` enum: `sum, histogram, last_value, drop`\n- [ ] Create `Aggregation` union with all aggregation types\n- [ ] Add `drop` aggregation type that ignores measurements\n\n#### 3. Integrate Views with MeterProvider\n**Files:** `src/sdk/metrics/meter_provider.zig`\n- [ ] Add `views: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(View)` field\n- [ ] Add `addView()` method for view registration\n- [ ] Implement `applyViews()` method for view matching and application\n- [ ] Add validation for view/instrument compatibility\n\n#### 4. Update Instruments for View Processing\n**Files:** `src/sdk/metrics/instruments.zig`\n- [ ] Update measurement methods to:\n  1. Apply all matching views to transform attributes/metadata\n  2. Forward transformed measurements to readers\n  3. Handle drop aggregation by skipping forwarding\n- [ ] Cache view applications at instrument creation time\n\n#### 5. Add View Configuration to Setup\n**Files:** `src/sdk/metrics/setup.zig`\n- [ ] Extend `setupGlobalProvider()` to accept views parameter\n- [ ] Follow existing variadic pattern like links parameter\n\n### Success Criteria\n- [ ] Views can filter attributes (allow list)\n- [ ] Views can rename instruments\n- [ ] Multiple views can match same instrument creating multiple streams\n- [ ] Drop aggregation works (measurements ignored)\n- [ ] Integration with error handler for validation errors\n- [ ] View configuration helpers added to setupGlobalProvider\n\n### Deferred Items\n- Advanced view features (wildcards beyond \"*\", complex selectors)\n- Dynamic view reconfiguration\n- View performance optimizations (pre-computed projections)\n- Exemplars\n- Complex aggregation configurations\n\n---\n\n## Testing Strategy\n\n### Phase 1 Tests\n- [ ] All existing SDK tests pass (`zig build test-sdk`)\n- [ ] Multi-reader independence tests\n- [ ] Control flow inversion verification\n- [ ] Memory management tests (no leaks)\n\n### Phase 1b Tests  \n- [ ] Cardinality limit enforcement\n- [ ] Overflow attribute generation\n- [ ] Attribute hash collision handling\n- [ ] Error handler integration\n\n### Phase 1c Tests\n- [ ] Lock-free operation correctness\n- [ ] High concurrency stress tests\n- [ ] Performance benchmarks vs Phase 1b\n- [ ] Race condition detection\n\n### Phase 2 Tests\n- [ ] View matching logic\n- [ ] Attribute filtering\n- [ ] Multi-stream generation  \n- [ ] Drop aggregation\n- [ ] View configuration\n\n## Benchmark Infrastructure (1-2 days, parallel with phases)\n\n### Directory Structure Setup\n- [ ] Create `benchmarks/metrics/` directory\n- [ ] Add benchmark targets to root `build.zig`\n- [ ] Create benchmark documentation in `benchmarks/README.md`\n\n### Benchmark Scenarios\n- [ ] Throughput benchmark (ops/second at various thread counts)\n- [ ] Cardinality benchmark (behavior at 2000 limit)\n- [ ] Multi-reader benchmark (performance with multiple readers)\n- [ ] Lock-free comparison (Phase 1b vs 1c)\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Notes\n\n### Memory Management\n- Instruments remain owned by meters (no ownership change)\n- Only aggregation ownership moves from instruments to readers\n- Each reader owns its aggregation state completely\n- Use pre-allocated pools where possible to avoid runtime allocation\n\n### Error Handling\n- Use `api.common.handleError()` throughout\n- `ErrorType.resource_exhausted` for cardinality limits\n- `ErrorType.validation` for view/instrument compatibility errors\n\n### Thread Safety\n- Phase 1: Mutex per reader aggregation map\n- Phase 1c: Lock-free aggregations, minimal locking for map access\n- Always ensure measurements are not lost or double-counted\n\n### Performance Targets\n- Phase 1: Maintain current performance\n- Phase 1c: 2x improvement for high contention scenarios\n- All phases: No performance regression for single-reader case\n\n---\n\n## Risk Mitigation\n\n### Performance Risks\n- Extensive benchmarking after each phase\n- Keep mutex-based fallback for debugging\n- Monitor memory usage throughout\n\n### Compatibility Risks  \n- Run full test suite after each phase\n- Verify all examples still work\n- Test with multiple readers from start\n\n### Implementation Risks\n- Complete each phase fully before proceeding\n- Validate success criteria before moving to next phase\n- Keep detailed change log for debugging\n\n---\n\n## Definition of Done\n\nEach phase is complete when:\n1.
