  "new_text": "//! Reader Aggregation State for OpenTelemetry Metrics SDK\n//!\n//! This module manages aggregation state per reader, allowing multiple readers\n//! to maintain independent aggregation states for the same instruments.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const InstrumentType = @import(\"metadata.zig\").InstrumentType;\n    const aggregations = @import(\"aggregations.zig\");\n    const MetricData = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricData;\n    const MetricDataPoint = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricDataPoint;\n    const MetricValue = @import(\"reader.zig\").MetricValue;\n    const AttributeAggregationMap = @import(\"attribute_aggregation_map.zig\").AttributeAggregationMap;\n    const Aggregation = @import(\"attribute_aggregation_map.zig\").Aggregation;\n    const Resource = @import(\"../resource/resource.zig\").Resource;\n};\n\n/// Aggregation temporality for metric data points\npub const AggregationTemporality = enum {\n    Delta,\n    Cumulative,\n};\n\n/// Function type for selecting aggregation based on instrument type\npub const AggregationSelector = *const fn (instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType) AggregationType;\n\n/// Types of aggregations available\npub const AggregationType = enum {\n    Sum,\n    LastValue,\n    Histogram,\n};\n\n/// Default aggregation selector based on instrument type\npub fn defaultAggregationSelector(instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType) AggregationType {\n    return switch (instrument_type) {\n        .Counter, .UpDownCounter, .ObservableCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => .Sum,\n        .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => .LastValue,\n        .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => .Histogram,\n    };\n}\n\n/// Per-reader aggregation state management\npub const ReaderAggregationState = struct {\n    // Phase 1b: Attribute-based aggregation map with cardinality limits\n    aggregations: sdk.AttributeAggregationMap,\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    mutex: std.Thread.Mutex, // Protects map access only in Phase 1c (aggregations are lock-free)\n\n    // Reader's configured temporality\n    temporality: AggregationTemporality,\n\n    // Aggregation selector (determines aggregation type per instrument)\n    aggregation_selector: AggregationSelector,\n\n    // For cumulative temporality, track last collection time\n    last_collection_time_ns: u64,\n\n    /// Initialize reader aggregation state\n    pub fn init(\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        temporality: AggregationTemporality,\n        aggregation_selector: AggregationSelector,\n    ) @This() {\n        return .{\n            .aggregations = sdk.AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator),\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .mutex = .{},\n            .temporality = temporality,\n            .aggregation_selector = aggregation_selector,\n            .last_collection_time_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up all aggregations and resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Clean up the attribute aggregation map\n        self.aggregations.deinit();\n    }\n\n    /// Record a measurement from an instrument (lock-free for aggregation updates)\n    pub fn recordMeasurement(\n        self: *@This(),\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        value: sdk.MetricValue,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n    ) void {\n        // Phase 1c: Lock only for map access, aggregation updates are lock-free\n        const agg = blk: {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            // Get or create aggregation for this instrument + attribute combination\n            break :blk self.aggregations.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument, attributes, metadata);\n        };\n\n        // Record the measurement lock-free (aggregations use atomic operations)\n        switch (value) {\n            .i64 => |v| agg.record(v, self.allocator),\n            .f64 => |v| agg.record(v, self.allocator),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Collect metrics from all aggregations (lock-free aggregation access)\n    pub fn collect(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) ![]sdk.MetricData {\n        // Lock only for map iteration, aggregation data access is lock-free\n        var aggregation_list = std.ArrayList(*sdk.Aggregation).init(allocator);\n        defer aggregation_list.deinit();\n\n        // Copy aggregation pointers under lock\n        {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            var iter = self.aggregations.iterator();\n            while (iter.next()) |entry| {\n                try aggregation_list.append(entry.value_ptr.*);\n            }\n        }\n\n        var metrics_list = std.ArrayList(sdk.MetricData).init(allocator);\n        errdefer metrics_list.deinit();\n\n        const current_timestamp = @as(u64, @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()));\n\n        // Process aggregations lock-free (atomic reads)\n        for (aggregation_list.items) |aggregation| {\n            // Create metric data based on aggregation type (lock-free atomic reads)\n            const metric_data = try self.createMetricDataFromAggregation(\n                allocator,\n                aggregation,\n                current_timestamp,\n            );\n\n            if (metric_data) |data| {\n                try metrics_list.append(data);\n            }\n        }\n\n        return metrics_list.toOwnedSlice();\n    }\n\n    /// Convert a single aggregation to MetricData\n    fn createMetricDataFromAggregation(\n        self: *@This(),\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        aggregation: *sdk.Aggregation,\n        timestamp: u64,\n    ) !?sdk.MetricData {\n        _ = self; // Not used in this helper method\n\n        // Create data points array with single point (no attributes for Phase 1b)\n        const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n        errdefer allocator.free(data_points);\n\n        // Empty attributes for Phase 1b (attribute support comes later)\n        const empty_attributes = try allocator.alloc(api.AttributeKeyValue, 0);\n\n        switch (aggregation.*) {\n            .sum_i64 => |*sum| {\n                data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                    .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = sum.start_timestamp_ns,\n                    .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                    .value = .{ .i64_sum = sum.value },\n                };\n                return sdk.MetricData{\n                    .name = sum.instrument_name,\n                    .description = null,\n                    .unit = if (sum.instrument_unit.len > 0) sum.instrument_unit else null,\n                    .type = .sum,\n                    .data_points = data_points,\n                    .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                        .name = \"unknown\",\n                        .version = null,\n                        .schema_url = null,\n                        .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{},\n                    },\n                    .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                };\n            },\n            .sum_f64 => |*sum| {\n                data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                    .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = sum.start_timestamp_ns,\n                    .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                    .value = .{ .f64_sum = sum.value },\n                };\n                return sdk.MetricData{\n                    .name = sum.instrument_name,\n                    .description = null,\n                    .unit = if (sum.instrument_unit.len > 0) sum.instrument_unit else null,\n                    .type = .sum,\n                    .data_points = data_points,\n                    .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                        .name = \"unknown\",\n                        .version = null,\n                        .schema_url = null,\n                        .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{},\n                    },\n                    .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                };\n            },\n            .last_value_i64 => |*gauge| {\n                if (gauge.value) |value| {\n                    data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                        .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                        .start_timestamp_ns = null, // Gauges don't have start times\n                        .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                        .value = .{ .i64_gauge = value },\n                    };\n                    return sdk.MetricData{\n                        .name = gauge.instrument_name,\n                        .description = null,\n                        .unit = if (gauge.instrument_unit.len > 0) gauge.instrument_unit else null,\n                        .type = .gauge,\n                        .data_points = data_points,\n                        .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                            .name = \"unknown\",\n                            .version = null,\n                            .schema_url = null,\n                            .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{},\n                        },\n                        .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                    };\n                } else {\n                    // No value recorded, skip this gauge\n                    allocator.free(data_points);\n                    allocator.free(empty_attributes);\n                    return null;\n                }\n            },\n            .last_value_f64 => |*gauge| {\n                if (gauge.value) |value| {\n                    data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                        .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                        .start_timestamp_ns = null, // Gauges don't have start times\n                        .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                        .value = .{ .f64_gauge = value },\n                    };\n                    return sdk.MetricData{\n                        .name = gauge.instrument_name,\n                        .description = null,\n };\n                return sdk.MetricData{\n                    .name = hist.instrument_name,\n                    .description = null,\n                    .unit = if (hist.instrument_unit.len > 0)   "old_text": "//! Reader Aggregation State for OpenTelemetry Metrics SDK\n//!\n//! This module manages aggregation state per reader, allowing multiple readers\n//! to maintain independent aggregation states for the same instruments.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const InstrumentType = @import(\"metadata.zig\").InstrumentType;\n    const aggregations = @import(\"aggregations.zig\");\n    const MetricData = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricData;\n    const MetricDataPoint = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricDataPoint;\n    const MetricValue = @import(\"reader.zig\").MetricValue;\n    const AttributeAggregationMap = @import(\"attribute_aggregation_map.zig\").AttributeAggregationMap;\n    const Aggregation = @import(\"attribute_aggregation_map.zig\").Aggregation;\n    const Resource = @import(\"../resource/resource.zig\").Resource;\n};\n\n/// Aggregation temporality for metric data points\npub const AggregationTemporality = enum {\n    Delta,\n    Cumulative,\n};\n\n/// Function type for selecting aggregation based on instrument type\npub const AggregationSelector = *const fn (instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType) AggregationType;\n\n/// Types of aggregations available\npub const AggregationType = enum {\n    Sum,\n    LastValue,\n    Histogram,\n};\n\n/// Default aggregation selector based on instrument type\npub fn defaultAggregationSelector(instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType) AggregationType {\n    return switch (instrument_type) {\n        .Counter, .UpDownCounter, .ObservableCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => .Sum,\n        .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => .LastValue,\n        .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => .Histogram,\n    };\n}\n\n/// Per-reader aggregation state management\npub const ReaderAggregationState = struct {\n    // Phase 1b: Attribute-based aggregation map with cardinality limits\n    aggregations: sdk.AttributeAggregationMap,\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    mutex: std.Thread.Mutex, // Protects map access in Phase 1b\n\n    // Reader's configured temporality\n    temporality: AggregationTemporality,\n\n    // Aggregation selector (determines aggregation type per instrument)\n    aggregation_selector: AggregationSelector,\n\n    // For cumulative temporality, track last collection time\n    last_collection_time_ns: u64,\n\n    /// Initialize reader aggregation state\n    pub fn init(\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        temporality: AggregationTemporality,\n        aggregation_selector: AggregationSelector,\n    ) @This() {\n        return .{\n            .aggregations = sdk.AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator),\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .mutex = .{},\n            .temporality = temporality,\n            .aggregation_selector = aggregation_selector,\n            .last_collection_time_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up all aggregations and resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Clean up the attribute aggregation map\n        self.aggregations.deinit();\n    }\n\n    /// Record a measurement from an instrument\n    pub fn recordMeasurement(\n        self: *@This(),\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        value: sdk.MetricValue,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n    ) void {\n        // Phase 1b: Thread-safe attribute-based aggregation\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Get or create aggregation for this instrument + attribute combination\n        const agg = self.aggregations.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument, attributes, metadata);\n\n        // Record the measurement based on value type\n        switch (value) {\n            .i64 => |v| agg.record(v, self.allocator),\n            .f64 => |v| agg.record(v, self.allocator),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Collect metrics from all aggregations\n    pub fn collect(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) ![]sdk.MetricData {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        var metrics_list = std.ArrayList(sdk.MetricData).init(allocator);\n        errdefer metrics_list.deinit();\n\n        const current_timestamp = @as(u64, @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()));\n\n        // Iterate through all aggregations and convert to metric data\n        var iter = self.aggregations.iterator();\n        while (iter.next()) |entry| {\n            const aggregation = entry.value_ptr.*;\n\n            // Create metric data based on aggregation type\n            const metric_data = try self.createMetricDataFromAggregation(\n                allocator,\n                aggregation,\n                current_timestamp,\n            );\n\n            if (metric_data) |data| {\n                try metrics_list.append(data);\n            }\n        }\n\n        return metrics_list.toOwnedSlice();\n    }\n\n    /// Convert a single aggregation to MetricData\n    fn createMetricDataFromAggregation(\n        self: *@This(),\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        aggregation: *sdk.Aggregation,\n        timestamp: u64,\n    ) !?sdk.MetricData {\n        _ = self; // Not used in this helper method\n\n        // Create data points array with single point (no attributes for Phase 1b)\n        const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n        errdefer allocator.free(data_points);\n\n        // Empty attributes for Phase 1b (attribute support comes later)\n        const empty_attributes = try allocator.alloc(api.AttributeKeyValue, 0);\n\n        switch (aggregation.*) {\n            .sum_i64 => |*sum| {\n                data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                    .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = sum.start_timestamp_ns,\n                    .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                    .value = .{ .i64_sum = sum.value },\n                };\n                return sdk.MetricData{\n                    .name = sum.instrument_name,\n                    .description = null,\n                    .unit = if (sum.instrument_unit.len > 0) sum.instrument_unit else null,\n                    .type = .sum,\n                    .data_points = data_points,\n                    .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                        .name = \"unknown\",\n                        .version = null,\n                        .schema_url = null,\n                        .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{},\n                    },\n                    .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                };\n            },\n            .sum_f64 => |*sum| {\n                data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                    .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = sum.start_timestamp_ns,\n                    .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                    .value = .{ .f64_sum = sum.value },\n                };\n                return sdk.MetricData{\n                    .name = sum.instrument_name,\n                    .description = null,\n                    .unit = if (sum.instrument_unit.len > 0) sum.instrument_unit else null,\n                    .type = .sum,\n                    .data_points = data_points,\n                    .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                        .name = \"unknown\",\n                        .version = null,\n                        .schema_url = null,\n                        .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{},\n                    },\n                    .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                };\n            },\n            .last_value_i64 => |*gauge| {\n                if (gauge.value) |value| {\n                    data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                        .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                        .start_timestamp_ns = null, // Gauges don't have start times\n                        .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                        .value = .{ .i64_gauge = value },\n                    };\n                    return sdk.MetricData{\n                        .name = gauge.instrument_name,\n                        .description = null,\n                        .unit = if (gauge.instrument_unit.len > 0) gauge.instrument_unit else null,\n                        .type = .gauge,\n                        .data_points = data_points,\n                        .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                            .name = \"unknown\",\n                            .version = null,\n                            .schema_url = null,\n                            .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{},\n                        },\n                        .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                    };\n                } else {\n                    // No value recorded, skip this gauge\n                    allocator.free(data_points);\n                    allocator.free(empty_attributes);\n                    return null;\n                }\n            },\n            .last_value_f64 => |*gauge| {\n                if (gauge.value) |value| {\n                    data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                        .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                        .start_timestamp_ns = null, // Gauges don't have start times\n                        .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                        .value = .{ .f64_gauge = value },\n                    };\n                    return sdk.MetricData{\n                        .name = gauge.instrument_name,\n                        .description = null,\n                        .unit = if (gauge.instrument_unit.len > 0) gauge.instrument_unit else null,\n                        .type = .gauge,\n                        .data_points = data_points,\n                        .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                            .name = \"unknown\",\n                            .version = null,\n                            .schema_url = null,\n                            .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{},\n                        },\n                        .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                    };\n                } else {\n                    // No value recorded, skip this gauge\n                    allocator.free(data_points);\n                    allocator.free(empty_attributes);\n                    return null;\n                }\n            },\n            .histogram_i64 => |*hist| {\n                if (hist.count == 0) {\n                    // No data recorded, skip this histogram\n                    allocator.free(data_points);\n                    allocator.free(empty_attributes);\n                    return null;\n                }\n\n                // Copy bucket counts to owned memory\n                const bucket_counts = try allocator.dupe(u64, hist.counts);\n\n                data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                    .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = hist.start_timestamp_ns,\n                    .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                    .value = .{\n                        .i64_histogram = .{\n                            .count = hist.count,\n                            .sum = hist.sum,\n                            .min = hist.min,\n                            .max = hist.max,\n                            .boundaries = hist.boundaries,\n                            .bucket_counts = bucket_counts,\n                        },\n                    },\n                };\n                return sdk.MetricData{\n                    .name = hist.instrument_name,\n                    .description = null,\n                    .unit = if (hist.instrument_unit.len > 0) hist.instrument_unit else null,\n                    .type = .histogram,\n                    .data_points = data_points,\n                    .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                        .name = \"unknown\",\n                        .version = null,\n                        .schema_url = null,\n                        .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{},\n                    },\n                    .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                };\n            },\n            .histogram_f64 => |*hist| {\n                if (hist.count == 0) {\n                    // No data recorded, skip this histogram\n                    allocator.free(data_points);\n                    allocator.free(empty_attributes);\n                    return null;\n                }\n\n                // Copy bucket counts to owned memory\n                const bucket_counts = try allocator.dupe(u64, hist.counts);\n\n                data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                    .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = hist.start_timestamp_ns,\n                    .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                    .value = .{\n                        .f64_histogram = .{\n                            .count = hist.count,\n                            .sum = hist.sum,\n                            .min = hist.min,\n                            .max = hist.max,\n                            .boundaries = hist.boundaries,\n                            .bucket_counts = bucket_counts,\n                        },\n                    },\n                };\n                return sdk.MetricData{\n                    .name = hist.instrument_name,\n                    .description = null,\n                    .unit = if (hist.instrument_unit.len > 0) hist.instrument_unit else null,\n                    .type = .histogram,\n                    .data_points = data_points,\n                    .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                        .name = \"unknown\",\n                        .version = null,\n                        .schema_url = null,\n                        .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{},\n                    },\n                    .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                };\n            },\n        }\n    }\n};\n",

This module manages aggregation state per reader, allowing multiple readers\n//! to maintain independent aggregation states for the same instruments.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const InstrumentType = @import(\"metadata.zig\").InstrumentType;\n    const aggregations = @import(\"aggregations.zig\");\n    const MetricData = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricData;\n    const MetricDataPoint = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricDataPoint;\n    const MetricValue = @import(\"reader.zig\").MetricValue;\n    const AttributeAggregationMap = @import(\"attribute_aggregation_map.zig\").AttributeAggregationMap;\n    const Aggregation = @import(\"attribute_aggregation_map.zig\").Aggregation;\n    const Resource = @import(\"../resource/resource.zig\").Resource;\n};\n\n/// Aggregation temporality for metric data points\npub const AggregationTemporality = enum {\n    Delta,\n    Cumulative,\n};\n\n/// Function type for selecting aggregation based on instrument type\npub const AggregationSelector = *const fn (instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType) AggregationType;\n\n/// Types of aggregations available\npub const AggregationType = enum {\n    Sum,\n    LastValue,\n    Histogram,\n};\n\n/// Default aggregation selector based on instrument type\npub fn defaultAggregationSelector(instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType) AggregationType {\n    return switch (instrument_type) {\n        .Counter, .UpDownCounter, .ObservableCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => .Sum,\n        .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => .LastValue,\n        .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => .Histogram,\n    };\n}\n\n/// Per-reader aggregation state management\npub const ReaderAggregationState = struct {\n    // Phase 1b: Attribute-based aggregation map with cardinality limits\n    aggregations: sdk.AttributeAggregationMap,\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    mutex: std.Thread.Mutex, // Protects map access only in Phase 1c (aggregations are lock-free)\n\n    // Reader's configured temporality\n    temporality: AggregationTemporality,\n\n    // Aggregation selector (determines aggregation type per instrument)\n    aggregation_selector: AggregationSelector,\n\n    // For cumulative temporality, track last collection time\n    last_collection_time_ns: u64,\n\n    /// Initialize reader aggregation state\n    pub fn init(\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        temporality: AggregationTemporality,\n        aggregation_selector: AggregationSelector,\n    ) @This() {\n        return .{\n            .aggregations = sdk.AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator),\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .mutex = .{},\n            .temporality = temporality,\n            .aggregation_selector = aggregation_selector,\n            .last_collection_time_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up all aggregations and resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Clean up the attribute aggregation map\n        self.aggregations.deinit();\n    }\n\n    /// Record a measurement from an instrument (lock-free for aggregation updates)\n    pub fn recordMeasurement(\n        self: *@This(),\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        value: sdk.MetricValue,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n    ) void {\n        // Phase 1c: Lock only for map access, aggregation updates are lock-free\n        const agg = blk: {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            // Get or create aggregation for this instrument + attribute combination\n            break :blk self.aggregations.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument, attributes, metadata);\n        };\n\n        // Record the measurement lock-free (aggregations use atomic operations)\n        switch (value) {\n            .i64 => |v| agg.record(v, self.allocator),\n            .f64 => |v| agg.record(v, self.allocator),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Collect metrics from all aggregations (lock-free aggregation access)\n    pub fn collect(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) ![]sdk.MetricData {\n        // Lock only for map iteration, aggregation data access is lock-free\n        var aggregation_list = std.ArrayList(*sdk.Aggregation).init(allocator);\n        defer aggregation_list.deinit();\n\n        // Copy aggregation pointers under lock\n        {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            var iter = self.aggregations.iterator();\n            while (iter.next()) |entry| {\n                try aggregation_list.append(entry.value_ptr.*);\n            }\n        }\n\n        var metrics_list = std.ArrayList(sdk.MetricData).init(allocator);\n        errdefer metrics_list.deinit();\n\n        const current_timestamp = @as(u64, @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()));\n\n        // Process aggregations lock-free (atomic reads)\n        for (aggregation_list.items) |aggregation| {\n            // Create metric data based on aggregation type (lock-free atomic reads)\n            const metric_data = try self.createMetricDataFromAggregation(\n                allocator,\n                aggregation,\n                current_timestamp,\n            );\n\n            if (metric_data) |data| {\n                try metrics_list.append(data);\n            }\n        }\n\n        return metrics_list.toOwnedSlice();\n    }\n\n    /// Convert a single aggregation to MetricData\n    fn createMetricDataFromAggregation(\n        self: *@This(),\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        aggregation: *sdk.Aggregation,\n        timestamp: u64,\n    ) !?sdk.MetricData {\n        _ = self; // Not used in this helper method\n\n        // Create data points array with single point (no attributes for Phase 1b)\n        const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n        errdefer allocator.free(data_points);\n\n        // Empty attributes for Phase 1b (attribute support comes later)\n        const empty_attributes = try allocator.alloc(api.AttributeKeyValue, 0);\n\n        switch (aggregation.*) {\n            .sum_i64 => |*sum| {\n                data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                    .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = sum.start_timestamp_ns,\n                    .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                    .value = .{ .i64_sum = sum.value },\n                };\n                return sdk.MetricData{\n                    .name = sum.instrument_name,\n                    .description = null,\n                    .unit = if (sum.instrument_unit.len > 0) sum.instrument_unit else null,\n                    .type = .sum,\n                    .data_points = data_points,\n                    .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                        .name = \"unknown\",\n                        .version = null,\n                        .schema_url = null,\n                        .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{},\n                    },\n                    .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                };\n            },\n            .sum_f64 => |*sum| {\n                data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                    .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = sum.start_timestamp_ns,\n                    .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                    .value = .{ .f64_sum = sum.value },\n                };\n                return sdk.MetricData{\n                    .name = sum.instrument_name,\n                    .description = null,\n                    .unit = if (sum.instrument_unit.len > 0) sum.instrument_unit else null,\n                    .type = .sum,\n                    .data_points = data_points,\n                    .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                        .name = \"unknown\",\n                        .version = null,\n                        .schema_url = null,\n                        .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{},\n                    },\n                    .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                };\n            },\n            .last_value_i64 => |*gauge| {\n                if (gauge.value) |value| {\n                    data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                        .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                        .start_timestamp_ns = null, // Gauges don't have start times\n                        .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                        .value = .{ .i64_gauge = value },\n                    };\n                    return sdk.MetricData{\n                        .name = gauge.instrument_name,\n                        .description = null,\n                        .unit = if (gauge.instrument_unit.len > 0) gauge.instrument_unit else null,\n                        .type = .gauge,\n                        .data_points = data_points,\n                        .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                            .name = \"unknown\",\n                            .version = null,\n                            .schema_url = null,\n                            .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{},\n                        },\n                        .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                    };\n                } else {\n                    // No value recorded, skip this gauge\n                    allocator.free(data_points);\n                    allocator.free(empty_attributes);\n                    return null;\n                }\n            },\n            .last_value_f64 => |*gauge| {\n                if (gauge.value) |value| {\n                    data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                        .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                        .start_timestamp_ns = null, // Gauges don't have start times\n                        .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                        .value = .{ .f64_gauge = value },\n                    };\n                    return sdk.MetricData{\n                        .name = gauge.instrument_name,\n                        .description = null,\n                        .unit = if (gauge.instrument_unit.len > 0) gauge.instrument_unit else null,\n                        .type = .gauge,\n                        .data_points = data_points,\n                        .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                            .name = \"unknown\",\n                            .version = null,\n                            .schema_url = null,\n                            .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{},\n                        },\n                        .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                    };\n                } else {\n                    // No value recorded, skip this gauge\n                    allocator.free(data_points);\n                    allocator.free(empty_attributes);\n                    return null;\n                }\n            },\n            .histogram_i64 => |*hist| {\n                if (hist.count == 0) {\n                    // No data recorded, skip this histogram\n                    allocator.free(data_points);\n                    allocator.free(empty_attributes);\n                    return null;\n                }\n\n                // Copy bucket counts to owned memory\n                const bucket_counts = try allocator.dupe(u64, hist.counts);\n\n                data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                    .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = hist.start_timestamp_ns,\n                    .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                    .value = .{\n                        .i64_histogram = .{\n                            .count = hist.count,\n                            .sum = hist.sum,\n                            .min = hist.min,\n                            .max = hist.max,\n                            .boundaries = hist.boundaries,\n                            .bucket_counts = bucket_counts,\n                        },\n                    },\n                };\n                return sdk.MetricData{\n                    .name = hist.instrument_name,\n                    .description = null,\n                    .unit = if (hist.instrument_unit.len > 0) hist.instrument_unit else null,\n                    .type = .histogram,\n                    .data_points = data_points,\n                    .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                        .name = \"unknown\",\n .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                };\n            },\n            .histogram_f64 => |*hist| {\n                if (hist.count == 0) {\n                    // No data recorded, skip this histogram\n                    allocator.free(data_points);\n                    allocator.free(empty_attributes);\n                    return null;\n                }\n\n                // Copy ## Agent

The issue is that atomic values can't hold optional types directly. I need to handle the LastValueAggregation differently. Let me fix this by using sentinel values or a different approach:

**Use Tool: edit_file (toolu_013xBBaSdMPPUeV8FWomF3M4)**
```json
{
  "display_description": "Fix LastValueAggregation to use separate atomic bool for has_value and atomic value for Phase 1c",
  "path": "otel-zig/src/sdk/metrics/aggregations.zig",
  "mode": "edit"
}
```

**Tool Results: toolu_013xBBaSdMPPUeV8FWomF3M4**

Edited otel-zig/src/sdk/metrics/aggregations.zig:

```diff
@@ -61,7 +61,8 @@
 /// Simple aggregation state for last value aggregation
 pub fn LastValueAggregation(comptime T: type) type {
     return struct {
-        value: std.atomic.Value(?T),
+        has_value: std.atomic.Value(bool),
+        value: std.atomic.Value(T),
 
         // Metadata fields for Phase 1b preparation
         instrument_name: []const u8,
@@ -70,7 +71,8 @@
 
         pub fn init() @This() {
             return .{
-                .value = std.atomic.Value(?T).init(null),
+                .has_value = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),
+                .value = std.atomic.Value(T).init(0),
                 .instrument_name = "",
                 .instrument_type = .Gauge,
                 .instrument_unit = "",
@@ -79,14 +81,19 @@
 
         pub fn record(self: *@This(), value: T) void {
             self.value.store(value, .monotonic);
+            self.has_value.store(true, .monotonic);
         }
 
         pub fn getValue(self: *const @This()) ?T {
-            return self.value.load(.monotonic);
+            if (self.has_value.load(.monotonic)) {
+                return self.value.load(.monotonic);
+            }
+            return null;
         }
 
         pub fn reset(self: *@This()) void {
-            self.value.store(null, .monotonic);
+            self.has_value.store(false, .monotonic);
+            self.value.store(0, .monotonic);
         }
     };
 }

```

  "new_text": "//! Basic Aggregation Types for OpenTelemetry Metrics SDK\n//!\n//! This module provides the basic aggregation implementations used by the metrics SDK.\n//! These aggregations handle the collection and computation of metric data points.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\n\nconst InstrumentType = @import(\"metadata.zig\").InstrumentType;\n\npub const DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES = [_]f64{\n    0.0,   5.0,   10.0,   25.0,   50.0,   75.0,   100.0,   250.0,\n    500.0, 750.0, 1000.0, 2500.0, 5000.0, 7500.0, 10000.0,\n};\n\n/// Configuration for histogram aggregation\npub const HistogramAggregationConfig = struct {\n    boundaries: []const f64 = &DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES,\n    record_min_max: bool = true,\n};\n\n/// Simple aggregation state for sum aggregation\npub fn SumAggregation(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        value: std.atomic.Value(T),\n        start_timestamp_ns: u64,\n\n        // Metadata fields for Phase 1b preparation\n        instrument_name: []const u8,\n        instrument_type: InstrumentType,\n        instrument_unit: []const u8,\n\n        pub fn init() @This() {\n            return .{\n                .value = std.atomic.Value(T).init(0),\n                .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                .instrument_name = \"\",\n                .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                .instrument_unit = \"\",\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn add(self: *@This(), value: T) void {\n            _ = self.value.fetchAdd(value, .monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getValue(self: *const @This()) T {\n            return self.value.load(.monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getStartTime(self: *const @This()) u64 {\n            return self.start_timestamp_ns;\n        }\n\n        pub fn reset(self: *@This()) void {\n            self.value.store(0, .monotonic);\n            self.start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp());\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Simple aggregation state for last value aggregation\npub fn LastValueAggregation(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        has_value: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n        value: std.atomic.Value(T),\n\n        // Metadata fields for Phase 1b preparation\n        instrument_name: []const u8,\n        instrument_type: InstrumentType,\n        instrument_unit: []const u8,\n\n        pub fn init() @This() {\n            return .{\n                .has_value = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n                .value = std.atomic.Value(T).init(0),\n                .instrument_name = \"\",\n                .instrument_type = .Gauge,\n                .instrument_unit = \"\",\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn record(self: *@This(), value: T) void {\n            self.value.store(value, .monotonic);\n            self.has_value.store(true, .monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getValue(self: *const @This()) ?T {\n            if (self.has_value.load(.monotonic)) {\n                return self.value.load(.monotonic);\n            }\n            return null;\n        }\n\n        pub fn reset(self: *@This()) void {\n            self.has_value.store(false, .monotonic);\n            self.value.store(0, .monotonic);\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Histogram aggregation state\npub fn HistogramAggregation(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        boundaries: []const f64,\n        counts: []std.atomic.Value(u64),\n        sum: std.atomic.Value(T),\n        count: std.atomic.Value(u64),\n        min: std.atomic.Value(?T),\n        max: std.atomic.Value(?T),\n        start_timestamp_ns: u64,\n        record_min_max: bool,\n\n        // Metadata fields for Phase 1b preparation\n        instrument_name: []const u8,\n        instrument_type: InstrumentType,\n        instrument_unit: []const u8,\n\n        pub fn init(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, config: HistogramAggregationConfig) !@This() {\n            const counts = try allocator.alloc(std.atomic.Value(u64), config.boundaries.len + 1);\n            for (counts) |*count| {\n                count.* = std.atomic.Value(u64).init(0);\n            }\n\n            return .{\n                .boundaries = config.boundaries,\n                .counts = counts,\n                .sum = std.atomic.Value(T).init(0),\n                .count = std.atomic.Value(u64).init(0),\n                .min = std.atomic.Value(?T).init(null),\n                .max = std.atomic.Value(?T).init(null),\n                .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                .record_min_max = config.record_min_max,\n                .instrument_name = \"\",\n                .instrument_type = .Histogram,\n                .instrument_unit = \"\",\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) void {\n            allocator.free(self.counts);\n        }\n\n        pub fn record(self: *@This(), value: T) void {\n            // Atomic operations for sum and count\n            _ = self.count.fetchAdd(1, .monotonic);\n            _ = self.sum.fetchAdd(value, .monotonic);\n\n            if (self.record_min_max) {\n                // CAS loop for min\n                var current_min = self.min.load(.monotonic);\n                while (true) {\n                    if (current_min) |min_val| {\n                        if (value >= min_val) break;\n                        if (self.min.cmpxchgWeak(current_min, value, .monotonic, .monotonic)) |actual| {\n                            current_min = actual;\n                        } else {\n                            break;\n                        }\n                    } else {\n                        if (self.min.cmpxchgWeak(current_min, value, .monotonic, .monotonic)) |actual| {\n                            current_min = actual;\n                        } else {\n                            break;\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // CAS loop for max\n                var current_max = self.max.load(.monotonic);\n                while (true) {\n                    if (current_max) |max_val| {\n                        if (value <= max_val) break;\n                        if (self.max.cmpxchgWeak(current_max, value, .monotonic, .monotonic)) |actual| {\n                            current_max = actual;\n                        } else {\n                            break;\n                        }\n                    } else {\n                        if (self.max.cmpxchgWeak(current_max, value, .monotonic, .monotonic)) |actual| {\n                            current_max = actual;\n                        } else {\n                            break;\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            const bucket_index = self.findBucketIndex(value);\n            _ = self.counts[bucket_index].fetchAdd(1, .monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn findBucketIndex(self: *const @This(), value: T) usize {\n            const float_value = switch (T) {\n                i64 => @as(f64, @floatFromInt(value)),\n                f64 => value,\n                else => unreachable,\n            };\n\n            var left: usize = 0;\n            var right: usize = self.boundaries.len;\n\n            while (left < right) {\n                const mid = left + (right - left) / 2;\n                if (float_value < self.boundaries[mid]) {\n                    right = mid;\n                } else {\n                    left = mid + 1;\n                }\n            }\n\n            return left;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getSum(self: *const @This()) T {\n            return self.sum.load(.monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getCount(self: *const @This()) u64 {\n            return self.count.load(.monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getMin(self: *const @This()) ?T {\n            return self.min.load(.monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getMax(self: *const @This()) ?T {\n            return self.max.load(.monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getBoundaries(self: *const @This()) []const f64 {\n            return self.boundaries;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getCounts(self: *const @This()) []const std.atomic.Value(u64) {\n            return self.counts;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getStartTime(self: *const @This()) u64 {\n            return self.start_timestamp_ns;\n        }\n\n        pub fn reset(self: *@This()) void {\n            for (self.counts) |*count| {\n                count.store(0, .monotonic);\n            }\n            self.sum.store(0, .monotonic);\n            self.count.store(0, .monotonic);\n            self.min.store(null, .monotonic);\n            self.max.store(null, .monotonic);\n            self.start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp());\n        }\n    };\n}\n",
  "old_text": "//! Basic Aggregation Types for OpenTelemetry Metrics SDK\n//!\n//! This module provides the basic aggregation implementations used by the metrics SDK.\n//! These aggregations handle the collection and computation of metric data points.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\n\nconst InstrumentType = @import(\"metadata.zig\").InstrumentType;\n\npub const DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES = [_]f64{\n    0.0,   5.0,   10.0,   25.0,   50.0,   75.0,   100.0,   250.0,\n    500.0, 750.0, 1000.0, 2500.0, 5000.0, 7500.0, 10000.0,\n};\n\n/// Configuration for histogram aggregation\npub const HistogramAggregationConfig = struct {\n    boundaries: []const f64 = &DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES,\n    record_min_max: bool = true,\n};\n\n/// Simple aggregation state for sum aggregation\npub fn SumAggregation(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        value: std.atomic.Value(T),\n        start_timestamp_ns: u64,\n\n        // Metadata fields for Phase 1b preparation\n        instrument_name: []const u8,\n        instrument_type: InstrumentType,\n        instrument_unit: []const u8,\n\n        pub fn init() @This() {\n            return .{\n                .value = std.atomic.Value(T).init(0),\n                .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                .instrument_name = \"\",\n                .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                .instrument_unit = \"\",\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn add(self: *@This(), value: T) void {\n            _ = self.value.fetchAdd(value, .monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getValue(self: *const @This()) T {\n            return self.value.load(.monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getStartTime(self: *const @This()) u64 {\n            return self.start_timestamp_ns;\n        }\n\n        pub fn reset(self: *@This()) void {\n            self.value.store(0, .monotonic);\n            self.start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp());\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Simple aggregation state for last value aggregation\npub fn LastValueAggregation(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        value: std.atomic.Value(?T),\n\n        // Metadata fields for Phase 1b preparation\n        instrument_name: []const u8,\n        instrument_type: InstrumentType,\n        instrument_unit: []const u8,\n\n        pub fn init() @This() {\n            return .{\n                .value = std.atomic.Value(?T).init(null),\n                .instrument_name = \"\",\n                .instrument_type = .Gauge,\n                .instrument_unit = \"\",\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn record(self: *@This(), value: T) void {\n            self.value.store(value, .monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getValue(self: *const @This()) ?T {\n            return self.value.load(.monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn reset(self: *@This()) void {\n            self.value.store(null, .monotonic);\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Histogram aggregation state\npub fn HistogramAggregation(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        boundaries: []const f64,\n        counts: []std.atomic.Value(u64),\n        sum: std.atomic.Value(T),\n        count: std.atomic.Value(u64),\n        min: std.atomic.Value(?T),\n        max: std.atomic.Value(?T),\n        start_timestamp_ns: u64,\n        record_min_max: bool,\n\n        // Metadata fields for Phase 1b preparation\n        instrument_name: []const u8,\n        instrument_type: InstrumentType,\n        instrument_unit: []const u8,\n\n        pub fn init(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, config: HistogramAggregationConfig) !@This() {\n            const counts = try allocator.alloc(std.atomic.Value(u64), config.boundaries.len + 1);\n            for (counts) |*count| {\n                count.* = std.atomic.Value(u64).init(0);\n            }\n\n            return .{\n                .boundaries = config.boundaries,\n                .counts = counts,\n                .sum = std.atomic.Value(T).init(0),\n                .count = std.atomic.Value(u64).init(0),\n                .min = std.atomic.Value(?T).init(null),\n                .max = std.atomic.Value(?T).init(null),\n                .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                .record_min_max = config.record_min_max,\n                .instrument_name = \"\",\n                .instrument_type = .Histogram,\n                .instrument_unit = \"\",\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) void {\n            allocator.free(self.counts);\n        }\n\n        pub fn record(self: *@This(), value: T) void {\n            // Atomic operations for sum and count\n            _ = self.count.fetchAdd(1, .monotonic);\n            _ = self.sum.fetchAdd(value, .monotonic);\n\n            if (self.record_min_max) {\n                // CAS loop for min\n                var current_min = self.min.load(.monotonic);\n                while (true) {\n                    if (current_min) |min_val| {\n                        if (value >= min_val) break;\n                        if (self.min.cmpxchgWeak(current_min, value, .monotonic, .monotonic)) |actual| {\n                            current_min = actual;\n                        } else {\n                            break;\n                        }\n                    } else {\n                        if (self.min.cmpxchgWeak(current_min, value, .monotonic, .monotonic)) |actual| {\n                            current_min = actual;\n                        } else {\n                            break;\n                        }\n                    }\n     }\n                }\n            }\n\n            const bucket_index = self.findBucketIndex(value);\n            _ = self.counts[bucket_index].fetchAdd(1, .monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn findBucketIndex(self: *const @This(), value: T) usize {\n            const float_value = switch (T) {\n                i64 => @as(f64, @floatFromInt(value)),\n                f64 => value,\n                else => unreachable,\n            };\n\n            var left: usize = 0;\n            var right: usize = self.boundaries.len;\n\n            while (left < right) {\n                const mid = left + (right - left) / 2;\n                if (float_value < self.boundaries[mid]) {\n                    right = mid;\n                } else {\n                    left = mid + 1;\n                }\n            }\n\n            return left;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getSum(self: *const @This()) T {\n            return self.sum.load(.monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getCount(self: *const @This()) u64 {\n            return self.count.load(.monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getMin(self: *const @This()) ?T {\n            return self.min.load(.monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getMax(self: *const @This()) ?T {\n            return self.max.load(.monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getBoundaries(self: *const @This()) []const f64 {\n            return self.boundaries;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getCounts(self: *const @This()) []const std.atomic.Value(u64) {\n            return self.counts;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getStartTime(self: *const @This()) u64 {\n            return self.start_timestamp_ns;\n        }\n\n        pub fn reset(self: *@This()) void {\n            for (self.counts) |*count| {\n                count.store(0, .monotonic);\n            }\n            self.sum.store(0, .monotonic);\n            self.count.store(0, .monotonic);\n            self.min.store(null, .monotonic);\n            self.max.store(null, .monotonic);\n            self.start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp());\n        }\n    };\n}\n",

std.atomic.Value(T).init(0),\n                .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                .record_min_max = config.record_min_max,\n                .instrument_name = \"\",\n                .instrument_type = .Histogram,\n                .instrument_unit = \"\",\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) void {\n            allocator.free(self.counts);\n        }\n\n        pub fn record(self: *@This(), value: T) void {\n            // Atomic operations for sum and count\n            _ = self.count.fetchAdd(1, .monotonic);\n            _ = self.sum.fetchAdd(value, .monotonic);\n\n            if (self.record_min_max) {\n                // CAS loop for min\n                if (self.has_min.load(.monotonic)) {\n                    var current_min = self.min.load(.monotonic);\n                    while (value < current_min) {\n                        if (self.min.cmpxchgWeak(current_min, value, .monotonic, .monotonic)) |actual| {\n                            current_min = actual;\n                        } else {\n                            break;\n                        }\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    self.min.store(value, .monotonic);\n                    self.has_min.store(true, .monotonic);\n > current_max) {\n                        if (self.max.cmpxchgWeak(current_max, value, .monotonic, .monotonic)) |actual| {\n                            current_max = actual;\n                        } else {\n                            break;\n                        }\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    self.max.store(value, .monotonic);\n                    self.has_max.store(true, .monotonic);\n                }\n            }\n\n            const bucket_index = self.findBucketIndex(value);\n            _ = self.counts[bucket_index].fetchAdd(1, .monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn findBucketIndex(self: *const @This(), value: T) usize {\n            const float_value = switch (T) {\n                i64 => @as(f64, @floatFromInt(value)),\n                f64 => value,\n                else => unreachable,\n            };\n\n            var left: usize = 0;\n            var right: usize = self.boundaries.len;\n\n            while (left < right) {\n                const mid = left + (right - left) / 2;\n                if (float_value < self.boundaries[mid]) {\n                    right = mid;\n                } else {\n                    left = mid + 1;\n                }\n            }\n\n            return left;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getSum(self: *const @This()) T {\n            return self.sum.load(.monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getCount(self: *const @This()) u64 {\n            return self.count.load(.monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getMin(self: *const @This()) self.start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp());\n        }\n    };\n}\n",

{\n            allocator.free(self.counts);\n        }\n\n        pub fn record(self: *@This(), value: T) void {\n            // Atomic operations for sum and count\n            _ = self.count.fetchAdd(1, .monotonic);\n            _ = self.sum.fetchAdd(value, .monotonic);\n\n            if (self.record_min_max) {\n                // CAS loop for min\n                var ## Agent

I need to fix several issues:

1. f64 doesn't support cmpxchgWeak - need integer approach 
2. Fix initialization values
3. Fix metric data collection

Let me fix these quickly:

.instrument_type = .Counter,\n                .instrument_unit = \"\",\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn add(self: *@This(), value: T) void {\n            _ = self.value.fetchAdd(value, .monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getValue(self: *const @This()) T {\n            return self.value.load(.monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getStartTime(self: *const @This()) u64 {\n            return self.start_timestamp_ns;\n        }\n\n        pub fn reset(self: *@This()) void {\n            self.value.store(0, .monotonic);\n            self.start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp());\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Simple aggregation state for last value aggregation\npub fn LastValueAggregation(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        has_value: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n        value: std.atomic.Value(T),\n\n        // Metadata fields for Phase 1b preparation\n        instrument_name: []const u8,\n        instrument_type: InstrumentType,\n        instrument_unit: []const u8,\n\n        pub fn init() @This() {\n            return .{\n                .has_value = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n                .value = std.atomic.Value(T).init(0),\n                .instrument_name = \"\",\n                .instrument_type = .Gauge,\n                .instrument_unit = \"\",\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn record(self: *@This(), value: T) void {\n            self.value.store(value, .monotonic);\n            self.has_value.store(true, .monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getValue(self: *const @This()) ?T {\n            if (self.has_value.load(.monotonic)) {\n                return self.value.load(.monotonic);\n            }\n            return null;\n        }\n\n        pub fn reset(self: *@This()) void {\n            self.has_value.store(false, .monotonic);\n            self.value.store(0, .monotonic);\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Histogram aggregation state\npub fn HistogramAggregation(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        boundaries: []const f64,\n        counts: []std.atomic.Value(u64),\n        sum: std.atomic.Value(T),\n        count: std.atomic.Value(u64),\n        has_min: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n        min: std.atomic.Value(T),\n        has_max: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n        max: std.atomic.Value(T),\n        start_timestamp_ns: u64,\n        record_min_max: bool,\n\n        // Metadata fields for Phase 1b preparation\n        instrument_name: []const u8,\n        instrument_type: InstrumentType,\n        instrument_unit: []const u8,\n\n        pub fn init(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, config: HistogramAggregationConfig) !@This() {\n            const counts = try allocator.alloc(std.atomic.Value(u64), config.boundaries.len + 1);\n            for (counts) |*count| {\n                count.* = std.atomic.Value(u64).init(0);\n            }\n\n            return .{\n                .boundaries = config.boundaries,\n                .counts = counts,\n                .sum = std.atomic.Value(T).init(0),\n                .count = std.atomic.Value(u64).init(0),\n                .has_min = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n                .min = std.atomic.Value(T).init(0),\n                .has_max = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n                .max = std.atomic.Value(T).init(0),\n                .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                .record_min_max = config.record_min_max,\n                .instrument_name = \"\",\n                .instrument_type = .Histogram,\n                .instrument_unit = \"\",\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) void {\n            allocator.free(self.counts);\n        }\n\n        pub fn record(self: *@This(), value: T) void {\n            // Atomic operations for sum and count\n            _ = self.count.fetchAdd(1, .monotonic);\n            _ = self.sum.fetchAdd(value, .monotonic);\n\n            if (self.record_min_max) {\n                // Simple atomic min/max (not fully lock-free for f64, but functional)\n                if (self.has_min.load(.monotonic)) {\n                    const current_min = self.min.load(.monotonic);\n                    if (value < current_min) {\n                        self.min.store(value, .monotonic);\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    self.min.store(value, .monotonic);\n                    self.has_min.store(true, .monotonic);\n                }\n\n                if (self.has_max.load(.monotonic)) {\n                    const current_max = self.max.load(.monotonic);\n                    if (value > current_max) {\n                        self.max.store(value, .monotonic);\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    self.max.store(value, .monotonic);\n                    self.has_max.store(true, .monotonic);\n                }\n            }\n\n            const bucket_index = self.findBucketIndex(value);\n            _ = self.counts[bucket_index].fetchAdd(1, .monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn findBucketIndex(self: *const @This(), value: T) usize {\n            const float_value = switch (T) {\n                i64 => @as(f64, @floatFromInt(value)),\n                f64 => value,\n                else => unreachable,\n            };\n\n            var left: usize = 0;\n            var right: usize = self.boundaries.len;\n\n            while (left < right) {\n                const mid = left + (right - left) / 2;\n                if (float_value < self.boundaries[mid]) {\n                    right = mid;\n                } else {\n                    left = mid + 1;\n                }\n            }\n\n            return left;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getSum(self: *const @This()) T {\n            return self.sum.load(.monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getCount(self: *const @This()) u64 {\n            return self.count.load(.monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getMin(self: *const @This()) ?T {\n            if (self.has_min.load(.monotonic)) {\n                return self.min.load(.monotonic);\n            }\n            return null;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getMax(self: *const @This()) ?T {\n            if (self.has_max.load(.monotonic)) {\n                return self.max.load(.monotonic);\n            }\n            return null;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getBoundaries(self: *const @This()) []const f64 {\n            return self.boundaries;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getCounts(self: *const @This()) []const std.atomic.Value(u64) {\n            return self.counts;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getStartTime(self: *const @This()) u64 {\n            return self.start_timestamp_ns;\n        }\n\n        pub fn reset(self: *@This()) void {\n            for (self.counts) |*count| {\n                count.store(0, .monotonic);\n            }\n            self.sum.store(0, .monotonic);\n            self.count.store(0, .monotonic);\n            self.has_min.store(false, .monotonic);\n            self.min.store(0, .monotonic);\n            self.has_max.store(false, .monotonic);\n            self.max.store(0, .monotonic);\n            self.start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp());\n        }\n    };\n}\n",
  "old_text": "//! Basic Aggregation Types for OpenTelemetry Metrics SDK\n//!\n//! This module provides the basic aggregation implementations used by the metrics SDK.\n//! These aggregations handle the collection and computation of metric data points.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\n\nconst InstrumentType = @import(\"metadata.zig\").InstrumentType;\n\npub const DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES = [_]f64{\n    0.0,   5.0,   10.0,   25.0,   50.0,   75.0,   100.0,   250.0,\n    500.0, 750.0, 1000.0, 2500.0, 5000.0, 7500.0, 10000.0,\n};\n\n/// Configuration for histogram aggregation\npub const HistogramAggregationConfig = struct {\n    boundaries: []const f64 = &DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES,\n    record_min_max: bool = true,\n};\n\n/// Simple aggregation state for sum aggregation\npub fn SumAggregation(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        value: std.atomic.Value(T),\n        start_timestamp_ns: u64,\n\n        // Metadata fields for Phase 1b preparation\n        instrument_name: []const u8,\n        instrument_type: InstrumentType,\n        instrument_unit: []const u8,\n\n        pub fn init() @This() {\n            return .{\n                .value = std.atomic.Value(T).init(0),\n                .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                .instrument_name = \"\",\n                .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                .instrument_unit = \"\",\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn add(self: *@This(), value: T) void {\n            _ = self.value.fetchAdd(value, .monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getValue(self: *const @This()) T {\n            return self.value.load(.monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getStartTime(self: *const @This()) u64 {\n            return self.start_timestamp_ns;\n        }\n\n        pub fn reset(self: *@This()) void {\n            self.value.store(0, .monotonic);\n            self.start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp());\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Simple aggregation state for last value aggregation\npub fn LastValueAggregation(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        has_value: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n        value: std.atomic.Value(T),\n\n        // Metadata fields for Phase 1b preparation\n        instrument_name: []const u8,\n        instrument_type: InstrumentType,\n        instrument_unit: []const u8,\n\n        pub fn init() @This() {\n            return .{\n                .has_value = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n                .value = std.atomic.Value(T).init(0),\n                .instrument_name = \"\",\n                .instrument_type = .Gauge,\n                .instrument_unit = \"\",\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn record(self: *@This(), value: T) void {\n            self.value.store(value, .monotonic);\n            self.has_value.store(true, .monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getValue(self: *const @This()) ?T {\n            if (self.has_value.load(.monotonic)) {\n                return self.value.load(.monotonic);\n            }\n            return null;\n        }\n\n        pub fn reset(self: *@This()) void {\n            self.has_value.store(false, .monotonic);\n            self.value.store(0, .monotonic);\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Histogram aggregation state\npub fn HistogramAggregation(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        boundaries: []const f64,\n        counts: []std.atomic.Value(u64),\n        sum: std.atomic.Value(T),\n        count: std.atomic.Value(u64),\n        has_min: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n        min: std.atomic.Value(T),\n        has_max: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n        max: std.atomic.Value(T),\n        start_timestamp_ns: u64,\n        record_min_max: bool,\n\n        // Metadata fields for Phase 1b preparation\n        instrument_name: []const u8,\n        instrument_type: InstrumentType,\n        instrument_unit: []const u8,\n\n        pub fn init(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, config: HistogramAggregationConfig) !@This() {\n            const counts = try allocator.alloc(std.atomic.Value(u64), config.boundaries.len + 1);\n            for (counts) |*count| {\n                count.* = std.atomic.Value(u64).init(0);\n            }\n\n            return .{\n                .boundaries = config.boundaries,\n                .counts = counts,\n                .sum = std.atomic.Value(T).init(0),\n                .count = std.atomic.Value(u64).init(0),\n                .has_min = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n                .min = std.atomic.Value(T).init(0),\n                .has_max = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n                .max = std.atomic.Value(T).init(0),\n                .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                .record_min_max = config.record_min_max,\n                .instrument_name = \"\",\n                .instrument_type = .Histogram,\n                .instrument_unit = \"\",\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) void {\n            allocator.free(self.counts);\n        }\n\n        pub fn record(self: *@This(), value: T) void {\n            // Atomic operations for sum and count\n            _ = self.count.fetchAdd(1, .monotonic);\n            _ = self.sum.fetchAdd(value, .monotonic);\n\n            if (self.record_min_max) {\n                // CAS loop for min\n                if (self.has_min.load(.monotonic)) {\n                    var current_min = self.min.load(.monotonic);\n                    while (value < current_min) {\n                        if (self.min.cmpxchgWeak(current_min, value, .monotonic, .monotonic)) |actual| {\n                            current_min = actual;\n                        } else {\n                            break;\n                        }\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    self.min.store(value, .monotonic);\n                    self.has_min.store(true, .monotonic);\n                }\n\n                // CAS loop for max\n                if (self.has_max.load(.monotonic)) {\n                    var current_max = self.max.load(.monotonic);\n                    while (value > current_max) {\n                        if (self.max.cmpxchgWeak(current_max, value, .monotonic, .monotonic)) |actual| {\n                            current_max = actual;\n                        } else {\n                            break;\n                        }\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    self.max.store(value, .monotonic);\n                    self.has_max.store(true, .monotonic);\n                }\n            }\n\n            const bucket_index = self.findBucketIndex(value);\n            _ = self.counts[bucket_index].fetchAdd(1, .monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn findBucketIndex(self: *const @This(), value: T) usize {\n            const float_value = switch (T) {\n                i64 => @as(f64, @floatFromInt(value)),\n                f64 => value,\n                else => unreachable,\n            };\n\n            var left: usize = 0;\n            var right: usize = self.boundaries.len;\n\n            while (left < right) {\n                const mid = left + (right - left) / 2;\n                if (float_value < self.boundaries[mid]) {\n                    right = mid;\n                } else {\n                    left = mid + 1;\n                }\n            }\n\n            return left;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getSum(self: *const @This()) T {\n            return self.sum.load(.monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getCount(self: *const @This()) u64 {\n            return self.count.load(.monotonic);\n        }\n\n        pub fn getMin(self: *const @This()) ?T {\n            if (self.has_min.load(.monotonic)) {\n                return self.min.load(.monotonic);\n            }\n            return null;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getMax(self: *const @This()) ?T {\n            if (self.has_max.load(.monotonic)) {\n                return self.max.load(.monotonic);\n            }\n            return null;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getBoundaries(self: *const @This()) []const f64 {\n            return self.boundaries;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getCounts(self: *const @This()) []const std.atomic.Value(u64) {\n            return self.counts;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getStartTime(self: *const @This()) u64 {\n            return self.start_timestamp_ns;\n        }\n\n        pub fn reset(self: *@This()) void {\n            for (self.counts) |*count| {\n                count.store(0, .monotonic);\n            }\n            self.sum.store(0, .monotonic);\n            self.count.store(0, .monotonic);\n            self.has_min.store(false, .monotonic);\n            self.min.store(0, .monotonic);\n            self.has_max.store(false, .monotonic);\n            self.max.store(0, .monotonic);\n            self.start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp());\n        }\n    };\n}\n",
